Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a Data Analyst to join our growing Data & Analytics team at Funko. In this role, you will leverage data to deliver actionable insights that support business growth, operational efficiency, and smarter decision-making. You’ll work closely with cross-functional partners to build scalable reporting, analyze trends, and help drive a more data-informed culture.This is a hands-on individual contributor role ideal for someone who enjoys translating data into meaningful business impact.What You’ll Do
Partner with Operations, Sales, Marketing, Finance, and other teams to support data-driven decisions
Build and maintain dashboards, reports, and KPI tracking in BI tools (Power BI, Tableau Looker, etc.)
Develop AI-enabled and automated reporting solutions to enhance insight generation and reduce manual work
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends, risks, and opportunities
Translate complex datasets into clear insights and business recommendations
Write and optimize SQL queries to work with large, complex datasets
Identify predictive modeling opportunities and build models
Collaborate with Data Engineering to improve data models, data quality, and reporting scalability
Support reporting and analytics needs tied to ERP and operational systems
What You’ll Bring
Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related field (or equivalent experience)
3–5+ years of hands-on experience in data analytics
Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets
Proficiency in BI and data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker, etc.)
Data science skill set: knowledgeable in statistical modeling (e.g. linear and logistical regression, classification, clustering) and tooling (python, R, etc.) with broad domain applicability
Experience with AI-driven analytics, automated reporting, or leveraging machine learning outputs in reporting workflows
Familiarity with cloud data platforms (Snowflake, AWS, GCP, Azure, etc.)
Experience working with ERP systems (Microsoft D365, Oracle, or similar) strongly preferred
Solid understanding of data warehousing and data modeling concepts
Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and ability to manage multiple priorities
Clear communication skills with the ability to present insights to non-technical stakeholders 
Salary InformationThe base salary range for this position in the selected city is $90,000 - $110,000 annually.​Compensation may vary outside of this range depending on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience, and location. Base pay is one part of the Total Package that is provided to compensate and recognize employees for their work, and this role may be eligible for additional discretionary bonuses/incentives, and restricted stock units.

What Funko Offers
Funko offers a competitive compensation package with full benefits and a 401(K) plan with matching contributions from the company. Most importantly, we offer a creative work environment with people who love pop culture just as much as you do. Can’t wait to gush about your latest binge? Neither can we! Looking for a place where your favorite pop culture t-shirt will receive the compliments it deserves? We know how you feel!


This role will be based in our Everett, WA HQ office. We will prioritize local candidates first and candidates located near a local office. This is NOT a remote opportunity at this moment.

Title: Data Analyst - Asset Reporting
Description:
Costco IT is responsible for the technical future of Costco Wholesale , the third largest retailer in the world with wholesale operations in fourteen countries. Despite our size and explosive international expansion, we continue to provide a family, employee centric atmosphere in which our employees thrive and succeed.

This is an environment unlike anything in the high-tech world and the secret of Costco’s success is its culture. The value Costco puts on its employees is well documented in articles from a variety of publishers including Bloomberg and Forbes. Our employees and our members come FIRST. Costco is well known for its generosity and community service and has won many awards for its philanthropy. The company joins with its employees to take an active role in volunteering by sponsoring many opportunities to help others.

Come join the Costco Wholesale IT family . Costco IT is a dynamic, fast-paced environment, working through exciting transformation efforts. We are building the next generation retail environment where you will be surrounded by dedicated and highly professional employees.

Data Analysts are responsible for importing, transforming, and validating (or querying) data with the purpose of understanding or drawing conclusions from the data to drive decision-making. They may use modeling techniques to form data in ways that can be analyzed and/or queried to identify data quality issues, anomalies, or deviations. They generate ad hoc reports and regular datasets or report information for end-users using system tools, database or data warehouse queries, and scripts. They also integrate data from multiple sources to produce requested or required data elements. They work closely with business groups, IT verticals, and governance teams to provide information to drive data quality and usage decisions.

The Asset Reporting Analyst manages the development, accuracy, and integrity of KPIs for IT hardware and software assets across all corporate and warehouse locations. Supporting IT Asset Management (ITAM) and related functions, this role transforms raw data from disparate sources into actionable business intelligence for both operational and executive leadership.

The analyst is responsible for the end-to-end reporting lifecycle, including gathering stakeholder requirements, identifying reporting gaps, and building new dashboards from scratch while maintaining and optimizing legacy reporting. This position requires a highly inquisitive, self-motivated professional capable of executing a rapid ramp-up through independent data discovery and the critical analysis of legacy documentation. Beyond ensuring data accuracy, the analyst provides the proactive insights necessary to drive asset lifecycle management, fulfillment efficiency, and organizational compliance.

If you want to be a part of one of the worldwide BEST companies “to work for”, simply apply and let your career be reimagined.

ROLE


Gathers and documents complex business requirements from stakeholders to architect, build, and deploy new reporting solutions and dashboards from the ground up.
Maintains, audits, and optimizes legacy reporting tools to ensure continued accuracy, performance, and operational relevance.
Proactively identifies reporting gaps and unmet business needs, proposing and implementing data-driven solutions to improve organizational visibility.
Identifies and interprets trends, patterns, and correlations among various data points to provide actionable insights to business owners.
Analyzes data for quality and completeness in strict accordance with established data governance policies, standards, and best practices.
Develops advanced scripts and queries to extract, transform, and normalize data from disparate sources for deep-dive analysis.
Designs and manages the data reporting environment, including the oversight of data sources, security protocols, and metadata management.
Creates and executes data quality control processes and audits to ensure integrity is maintained across all extraction and reporting workflows.
Performs data profiling and logical modeling to determine the most effective ways to measure, visualize, and provide data to the business.
Applies statistical methods and visualization techniques to translate raw, complex datasets into meaningful business narratives.
Integrates multiple disparate data sources to form accurate, comprehensive, and high-fidelity datasets for cross-functional use.
Defines and ensures adherence to process improvement opportunities to streamline data collection, normalization, and analysis.
Performs rigorous testing and validation of data and data sources to ensure the highest level of accuracy for executive-level reporting.
Executes data mapping and tracing from system to system to resolve complex business problems or identify technical system gaps.
Provides technical expertise on data migration, mining, and cleansing to support large-scale asset management initiatives.
Collaborates with and informs data stewards and custodians to ensure the quality and consistency of master data changes and additions.
Prepares comprehensive documentation regarding data relationships, integration flows, and reporting logic to ensure long-term sustainability and knowledge transfer.
Develops a deep working knowledge of business processes to ensure all reporting is contextually relevant and directly supports operational decision-making.
Identifies and resolves complex problems, often anticipating technical or data-related issues before they impact stakeholders or business operations.
Leads the development and continuous improvement of KPIs to measure and drive the effectiveness of asset management, fulfillment, and purchasing functions.


Required


1-3 years’ writing queries, report writing, and presenting findings.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and disseminate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy.
Detail oriented and possess strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to analyze a situation for potential future problems.
Proven ability to gather and document complex or unclear stakeholder requirements and translate them into technical reporting specifications and new builds, iteratively working with stakeholders to ensure delivery meets requirements
Strong technical and end user documentation and writing skills.
Strong verbal and strategic communication skills, with the ability to translate complex technical findings into actionable insights for stakeholders at all levels, including executive leadership
Ability to leverage new and emerging tools for learning and understanding (ex. Google Gems)
Exceptional analytical skills with a relentless focus on detail and the accuracy of disseminated information
Responsible, conscientious, and possess a passion and curiosity for using data to improve on processes and source data quality
High degree of autonomy and self-motivation, with a track record of navigating legacy documentation and raw data independently to reach proficiency
Innate curiosity and a passion for using data to improve business processes and source data quality
Demonstrates an ability to grow technical skills and learn new technologies.


Recommended


Knowledge of supply chain and asset lifecycle management
Data virtualization using Power BI, SQL, VBA Macros.
Power BI
Scripting (PowerShell, Apps Script, etc.)
Expert/Advanced experience in Google Sheets and related functions (pivot tables, Apps Script, macros)
Experience and basic knowledge of Google Gem creation
ServiceNow a plus (HAM Pro, SAM Pro, ITAM)
Proficient in Google Workspace applications, including Sheets, Docs, Slides, and Gmail.


Required Documents


Cover Letter
Resume


California applicants, please click here to review the Costco Applicant Privacy Notice.

Pay Ranges

Level 1 - $36.06/hr - $48.08/hr

Level 2 - $90,000 - $120,000

Level 3 - $110,000 - $140,000

Level 4 - $130,000 - $160,000

We offer a comprehensive package of benefits including paid time off, health benefits - medical/dental/vision/hearing aid/pharmacy/behavioral health/employee assistance, health care reimbursement account, dependent care assistance plan, short-term disability and long-term disability insurance, AD&D insurance, life insurance, 401(k), stock purchase plan to eligible employees.

Costco is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace. Costco is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or any other legally protected status. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or the recruiting process, please send a request to IT-Recruiting@costco.com

If hired, you will be required to provide proof of authorization to work in the United States. Applicants and employees for this position will not be sponsored for work authorization, including, but not limited to H1-B visas .

Required

  • 1-3 years’ writing queries, report writing, and presenting findings.
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and disseminate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Detail oriented and possess strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to analyze a situation for potential future problems.
  • Proven ability to gather and document complex or unclear stakeholder requirements and translate them into technical reporting specifications and new builds, iteratively working with stakeholders to ensure delivery meets requirements
  • Strong technical and end user documentation and writing skills.
  • Strong verbal and strategic communication skills, with the ability to translate complex technical findings into actionable insights for stakeholders at all levels, including executive leadership
  • Ability to leverage new and emerging tools for learning and understanding (ex. Google Gems)
  • Exceptional analytical skills with a relentless focus on detail and the accuracy of disseminated information
  • Responsible, conscientious, and possess a passion and curiosity for using data to improve on processes and source data quality
  • High degree of autonomy and self-motivation, with a track record of navigating legacy documentation and raw data independently to reach proficiency
  • Innate curiosity and a passion for using data to improve business processes and source data quality
  • Demonstrates an ability to grow technical skills and learn new technologies.

Recommended

  • Knowledge of supply chain and asset lifecycle management
  • Data virtualization using Power BI, SQL, VBA Macros.
  • Power BI
  • Scripting (PowerShell, Apps Script, etc.)
  • Expert/Advanced experience in Google Sheets and related functions (pivot tables, Apps Script, macros)
  • Experience and basic knowledge of Google Gem creation
  • ServiceNow a plus (HAM Pro, SAM Pro, ITAM)
  • Proficient in Google Workspace applications, including Sheets, Docs, Slides, and Gmail.

Required Documents

  • Cover Letter
  • Resume

California applicants, please click to review the Costco Applicant Privacy Notice.

Level 1 - $36.06/hr - $48.08/hr

Level 2 - $90,000 - $120,000

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About magniX: Based in the beautiful Pacific Northwest, magniX is a pioneer in electric propulsion and battery technology for the aerospace industry. We’re working to revolutionize how the world connects by transforming the way aircraft are powered. magniX has been at the leading edge of electric propulsion innovation for almost 20 years, and has successfully demonstrated our technology in flight. We are now on the path to take our proven technology and innovative products to market. Join us to be part of a passionate team that is ushering in a new era of aviation.

About the role: The Data Analyst role exists to make our data trustworthy. magniX operates across multiple systems — ERP, program management, and financial planning tools — and this analyst will be the person who understands how data flows between them, where it breaks down, and how to fix it. Working closely with our Program Analytics PM and Finance lead, you will own the data layer that makes everyone else’s analysis reliable.

This is a build environment, not a maintain environment. If you are excited by the idea of creating structure where there isn’t any yet — and doing it in an aerospace company that is redefining what electric flight looks like — this role is for you.

Primary Responsibilities

Data Integrity & Source Understanding


Develop a working understanding of magniX’s key data sources — ERP, program management systems, and financial planning tools — and how data flows between them.
Identify inconsistencies in data (e.g., project codes, cost center IDs, naming conventions) across systems and work with stakeholders to resolve them at the source.
Build and maintain basic data controls: validation rules, naming standards, and flagging logic to catch upstream errors before they propagate downstream.
Create and maintain a living data dictionary: document what key fields mean, where they come from, and who owns them.


Power BI Development & Maintenance


Build, maintain, and improve Power BI reports and dashboards supporting Finance, Program Management, and Operations.
Write and maintain Power Query (M) transformations to clean, shape, and stage data for reporting.
Partner with the Finance lead on financial model logic and own the technical implementation in Power BI Desktop.
Diagnose and resolve data load errors, schema mismatches, and query failures in the Power BI model.
Support the evolution of department-level dashboards into executive-level reporting tools.


Reporting & Analysis Support


Produce recurring financial and operational reports (actuals vs. budget, program cost tracking, variance analysis) with accuracy and on schedule.
Translate data into clear insights for non-technical stakeholders — program managers, finance, and senior leadership.
Support preparation of PSRs, PMRs, and QERs with accurate, well-structured data packages.


Cross-Functional Collaboration


Work alongside the Program Analytics PM to align data definitions, reporting standards, and dashboard strategy across programs and functions.
Engage with program managers and operations staff to understand their data needs and surface gaps early.
Proactively communicate data issues and their downstream impacts rather than waiting to be asked.


Required Qualifications


3+ years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, FP&A, or a closely related function.
Proficiency in Power BI, including report and dashboard development.
Hands-on Power Query (M) experience — ability to write, read, and debug M code, not just use the GUI.
Familiarity with data modeling concepts: fact vs. dimension tables, relationships, cardinality.
Financial literacy: ability to read and interpret a P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow statement; understanding of actuals vs. budget vs. forecast.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in an aerospace, defense, manufacturing or aviation environment.
FP&A background or experience supporting a finance function directly.
Exposure to ERP systems (SAP, Odoo)
Familiarity with SharePoint as a data source.
Familiarity with program financial reporting (EVMS, Earned Value, program cost tracking).
Exposure to project management tooling (MS Project, Unanet, or similar).


Location: Applicants must be willing and able to commute to magniX's location in Everett, Washington.

Compensation & Benefits


100% company-paid premiums for medical, dental, and vision insurance (for employees & dependents).
4 weeks paid vacation + 1-week annual end-of-year shutdown, sick time, and 11 paid holidays.
401K retirement plan with industry leading 9.5% company contribution.
Company paid Life insurance, AD&D, Short-Term and Long-Term disability insurance
Catered lunches and quarterly company fun-day events!


Salary Range: $90k-$130k

E-Verify: magniX utilizes E-Verify for employment eligibility verification

Pre-employment Screenings: Employment with magniX is contingent upon satisfactory completion of pre-employment requirements including a background screen.

Export Control Compliance: This position may require access to export-controlled data. To meet export control compliance requirements, candidates must be a “U.S. Person” (U.S. citizens, lawful permanent residents, refugees, or asylees), or an appropriate export authorization by the U.S. Government may be required prior to such access. Employment, and the continuity of employment of non-U.S. persons, is contingent upon the company’s ability to secure and maintain the necessary export control authorization.

Diversity and Inclusion: magniX is an equal opportunity employer. We value and embrace diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, marital status, age, or disability status.

(MS Project, Unanet, or similar).

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

At Boeing, we innovate and collaborate to make the world a better place. We’re committed to fostering an environment for every teammate that’s welcoming, respectful and inclusive, with great opportunity for professional growth. Find your future with us.

Job Description

Boeing Commercial Aircraft (BCA) Training and Compliance is hiring an Experience Data Analyst (Level 3) to support the Assembly Advisement Team at the Skill Enhancement Center. The position will be located in Everett, Washington.

The Data Analyst will play a key role in establishing standard processes and metrics used across BCA to ensure consistency in analysis and reporting of staffing information.

Position Responsibilities:



Conducts assessments of processes and practices for comparison to applicable standards and criteria
Analyzes and interprets data
Provides feedback on assessment results
Works with organizations to develop strategies, plans and metrics to accomplish company initiatives using Lean methodologies or other applicable tools and processes
Works at the appropriate level in the organization to implement strategies and plans
Works under general direction



Basic Qualifications (Required Skills/Experience):



3+ years of experience in Data Analysis
Proficiency with MS Office applications
1+ years of experience working in cross-functional teams
1+ years of experience with process analysis, process development, and process improvement



Preferred Qualifications (Desired Skills/Experience):



Bachelor’s degree or higher preferred.
3+ years of experience in collaborative problem solving, building lasting relationships & proficiency in written and verbal communication
Experience and proficiency reading and interpreting blueprints, drawings and templates.
Excellent analytical, communication and problem solving skills
Possess Manufacturing experience in one or more of these areas: Mechanical, Fluids/Pneumatics, Electrical Integration, Composites Fabrication, Composites Assembly



Shift:

This role is primarily 1st shift; however, there may be additional shift requirements to support program objectives.

COI:

Successful candidate for this job must satisfy the company's Conflict of Interest (COI) assessment process

Drug Free Workplace:

Boeing is a Drug Free Workplace where post offer applicants and employees are subject to testing for marijuana, cocaine, opioids, amphetamines, PCP, and alcohol when criteria is met as outlined in our policies.

Total Rewards

At Boeing, we strive to deliver a Total Rewards package that will attract, engage and retain the top talent. Elements of the Total Rewards package include competitive base pay and variable compensation opportunities.

The Boeing Company also provides eligible employees with an opportunity to enroll in a variety of benefit programs, generally including health insurance, flexible spending accounts, health savings accounts, retirement savings plans, life and disability insurance programs, and a number of programs that provide for both paid and unpaid time away from work.

The specific programs and options available to any given employee may vary depending on eligibility factors such as geographic location, date of hire, and the applicability of collective bargaining agreements.

Please note that the information shown below is a general guideline only. Pay is based upon candidate experience and qualifications, as well as market and business considerations.

Summary pay range: $102,600 - $125,400

Applications for this position will be accepted until Jun. 08, 2026

Export Control Requirements:

This is not an Export Control position.

Relocation

Relocation assistance is not a negotiable benefit for this position.

Visa Sponsorship

Employer will not sponsor applicants for employment visa status.

Shift

This position is for 1st shift

Equal Opportunity Employer:

Boeing is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, physical or mental disability, genetic factors, military/veteran status or other characteristics protected by law.

Title: BI Analyst II
Description:
Purpose Brands, the parent company of Orangetheory, Anytime Fitness, Waxing the City, and The Bar Method, is seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst II, to join its team. This is a great position for someone who is looking to expand their career, and join a company with a fun, fast-paced and inspirational culture.

As part of our Analytics team, this data and analytics professional analyzes business questions, assesses the impact of business initiatives. They think critically, are mathematically savvy, know how to query data, and apply analytical methods to derive insights. They work with our data engineers to augment datasets to enable dashboarding, reports, and operationalizing insights. They partner with business stakeholders to help them make use of analysis results and deploy data-informed decision-making processes.

Purpose/Impact: (Duties & Essential Functions)

Reporting & Analytics


Analyze business questions and communicate findings through briefs, presentations, reports or other media. Clarify and document the questions, how findings may be used, and the analysis approach.
Conduct impact analyses, leading to a deeper understanding of business performance, the member experience and fitness results. Guidance on approach provided by senior analyst or BI Manager.
Develop simple deterministic models that project outcomes based on observed relationships, e.g., break-even projections, simple systems models.
Develop reports and dashboards for an entire problem space, such as website performance, lead conversion or studio portfolio analysis. These are product features that are supported and evolved over time as opposed to one-off analyses.
Develop internal and external documentation and training on Analytics products, such as dashboards, reports, KPI's, source data collection and transformation, data lineage and suggested use cases.
Develop end-user applications with low-code tools to surface actionable analytics insights, collect data or execute analytics driven workflows/actions.
Work with data engineers to augment existing datasets with metrics and categorizations that make it easier to answer common business and analysis questions and publish appropriate reports.
Build the company knowledge base of data-informed insights, documenting findings and promoting and communicating those findings through appropriate internal channels.


Performance Expectations


Able to develop or collaborate with a data engineer on the design (metrics, attributes) of “last mile” datasets for analyzing common questions in a specific business domain.
Well versed in most of the functionality our BI platforms offer and able to make efficient use of them.
Uses a toolkit of analytic methods, e.g., variance analysis, sampling, establishing correlations, methods of analyzing causality to answer business questions with data.
Able to ask questions to fully understand the context and business question asked and how answering it will make a difference. Occasionally still needs help from senior analyst or manager to frame the problem.
Chooses or proposes a path of inquiry that is logical and coherent.
Follows applicable COE (Center of Excellence) tenets and methodologies when analyzing business questions or developing reports and dashboards.
Makes accurate inferences from the data most of the time. Still needs some help from more senior analysts or manager to validate conclusions.
Presents findings in an easy-to-understand way for business stakeholders, using COE practices.
Shares and documents approach and findings, making sure the rest of the team has visibility and can build on them.
Is recognized by business stakeholders as contributing to their decision-making processes or improving their operations.
Regularly reviews work with more senior analysts, seeks to learn and incorporates learnings into work products.


Background and Strengths


BS/BA in computer science, statistics, mathematics, business administration, economics, or related field with a focus on logic and quantitative analysis.
3-5 years of experience in reporting and analysis. Experience with implementing, maintaining or developing reporting in Power BI, Tableau or comparable platforms is a plus.
Basic understanding of statistics (sampling, statistical significance).
Proficiency in querying and manipulating data using SQL. Know how to use subqueries, window and analytic functions, regular expressions.
Experience with Python, R or similar languages.


Strengths


Well organized. Thinks about how to do the work, then acts. Seeks to contextualize the work.
Demonstrates understanding of which analytic methods to use to answer certain types of questions.
Good interpersonal skills, seeks to understand others’ viewpoints, motivations, ways of thinking.
Communicates clearly and knows best practices for presentations.
Shows curiosity about the industry and can explain how our brands are differentiated.


Salary Range: $90k - $115k

What’s in it for you?

We offer a competitive salary along with exceptional benefits such as:


Medical, Dental and Vision Coverage
Hybrid Work Environment
Life and Disability Insurance
Unlimited Time off + Paid Holidays
Flexible Friday's between Memorial Day and Labor Day
401(K) Savings Plan Matching at 4%
10 Coaching and Therapy sessions
Mental Health Benefits
Brand Discounts & Reimbursements
In-house workout facilities
Professional Development Opportunities
Team Building, Employee Engagement Activities & so much more


WORK SCHEDULE

Purpose Brands LLC, Currently Observe The Following Hybrid Work Model For Employees At Our Boca Raton (FL), Woodbury (MN), And Seattle (WA) Offices


Remote optional: Fridays
On-site days: Mondays, Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Thursdays


DIVERSITY, EQUITY, AND INCLUSION STATEMENT

Purpose Brands is committed to encouraging, facilitating, and upholding an environment centered on diversity, equity, and inclusion across every facet of the Purpose Brands. We will work to create a sustainable culture that supports a healthy space for learning and growing, valuing, and empowering every employee, inspiring a diverse franchise network, and uplifting the members and communities we serve.

EEO STATEMENT

Purpose Brands provides equal employment opportunity to all individuals regardless of their race, color, creed, religion, gender, age, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by state, federal, or local law. Discrimination of any type will not be tolerated.

Title: Data Analyst 2: Inventory (Hybrid - Seattle, WA)
Description:
Job Description

The Data Analyst 2 will support the Inventory Audit organization at Nordstrom, collaborating with cross-functional business teams to deliver advanced analytics, reporting, and data storytelling. This role focuses on actionable insights that inform strategic decisions and optimize business performance, with a strong emphasis on data integrity, process improvement, and stakeholder engagement.

A day in the life...


Own the intake, management, and integrity of all data utilized by the Inventory

Advanced experience in data visualization (Tableau, Power BI), building interactive dashboards, and applying user experience principles.
Strong statistical expertise, applying descriptive and inferential methods (hypothesis testing, correlation analysis, confidence intervals).
Experience integrating and synthesizing data from multiple sources, with proficiency in data wrangling and transformation techniques.
Hands-on experience with cloud-based data platforms (AWS, GCP) and scalable data solutions.

Experience defining and evaluating KPIs, with a strong understanding of modeling and performance metrics.

Applied skills in SQL, AWS Analytics and Database, Teradata, Tableau Server, Alteryx or similar.

Experience supporting modernization of reporting and data visualization programs.
Experience translating ambiguous business problems into structured analytical approaches.
Strong understanding of data warehousing, query construction and dimensional data modeling
Comfort in presenting to business stakeholders and leadership.
Strong operational business understanding, including the impact of business decisions on stakeholders and systems.

Title: Business Analyst - Trust, Safety, and Fraud Analytics
Description:
Who we are:
At Rover, pets and their people are at the heart of everything we do. We connect pet parents with trusted pet care across the U.S., Canada, Europe, and Australia.

Headquartered in Seattle and Barcelona, we’re a values driven, fast-growing tech company focused on building safe and personalized experiences tailored to the needs of each unique pet. We’re investing in AI as a business accelerator and provide every team member access to AI tools in service of creating better experiences for our community.

We are proud to be recognized as a great place to work, having been named among the 100 Best Companies to Work For in Seattle Business Magazine and Washington’s Best Workplaces in the Puget Sound Business Journal. At Rover we’re committed to creating an accessible, inclusive, and welcoming community, which starts with our employees.

Want to make an impact? Join our pack and come work (and play!) with us.



This is a hybrid position that has the minimum expectation to work out of our Downtown Seattle Office two days per week on Mondays and Thursdays. For this reason, candidates must be based in the Greater Seattle Area or willing to relocate




Additionally, applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis, without the need for current or future employer sponsorship for an employment-based visa status.

Who we're looking for:
We are seeking a Business Analyst to partner closely with Rover’s Infosec team and Trust and Safety Leadership team, driving initiatives that make Rover a safer community for pet parents and sitters. This is a high-visibility role where you will collaborate directly with a diverse set of stakeholders to define, measure, and improve the success of Rover’s Fraud program. You will be responsible for conducting in-depth analysis of new and developing fraud trends, which includes proactively identifying issues and then developing and proposing actionable solutions. This role requires a unique skillset: the ideal candidate must be comfortable translating ambiguous business problems into crisp requirements, and then using those to build creative data products that meet niche business needs. You should be a natural builder - someone who is passionate about starting from ground zero and building a highly effective analytical program with relative autonomy.

Your Responsibilities:


Strategic Planning & Measurement: Partner with Fraud leadership to define the strategy for measuring program health, operational performance, and magnitude of Fraud’s impact on our business. This includes establishing comprehensive metrics, developing crisp reporting, and setting up monitoring rhythms.
Performance Analysis: Conduct in-depth analysis of key Fraud metrics to identify emerging trends and opportunities which will inform deeper research and analytical efforts.
Develop and refine detection models: Develop and improve tooling and models to more effectively identify emerging fraud attacks, empowering the Fraud team to take quicker, more effective action to mitigate fraud across Rover's platform.
Executive Communication: Develop and present crisp narratives resulting from analytics findings and investigations to senior Operations leaders, focusing on both identified problems and proposed solutions.
Thought Partnership: Serve as a strategic partner and advisor to business leaders, making data-driven recommendations on investments and optimization strategies within Trust and Safety and Fraud.




Your Qualifications:


At least 4 years experience working cross-functionally with complex datasets to support organizational decision making.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills. Specifically, you know how to narrate analysis and results to business partners in a useful, concise, and meaningful way.
You have a passion for developing and embracing new ways of working using AI tools, with a strong desire to automate mundane tasks and develop more efficient analytical / technical workflows.
You are extremely comfortable with receiving stakeholder questions and independently translating them into clear, actionable analytical problems to solve, all with relative autonomy.
You have hands-on experience building out data abstractions using tools such as DBT or Airflow as well as a variety of data visualization tools (e.g., Mode, Looker, Periscope, Tableau, etc.)
Strong proficiency with SQL. You would feel confident reviewing code produced by other analysts and data scientists




Nice to Have:


Masters degree in a quantitative field OR on the job experience with applied statistics.
Ability to code in Python/R is a plus
Prior experience performing advanced fraud analytics within the technology sector.
Experience with the development and optimization of machine learning models
Hands on experience using AI tools (Claude Code, Codex, etc) daily to simplify analytical work
Hands-on experience working with customer service tool data (e.g., Zendesk)
Hands-on experience running A/B tests and experiment design




Benefits of working for Rover:


Competitive compensation
401k
Flexible PTO
Competitive benefits package, including medical, dental, and vision insurance
Commuter benefits
Bring your dog to work (and unlimited puppy time)
Doggy benefits, including $1000 toward adopting your first dog
Stocked fridges, coffee, soda, and lots of treats (for humans and dogs) and free catered lunches semi-monthly
Regular team activities performed in person and virtually




Compensation:


In the greater Seattle area the first-year salary range is $108,694-$136,046. Additionally, Rover offers benefits to full-time employees
The cash compensation offered for this role will be dependent on the candidate's experience, qualifications, skills, and abilities as demonstrated in the interview and hiring process




At Rover, we’re driven by seeing our people succeed and grow, while doing our jobs better than ever before. We believe the best business outcomes come from a diverse set of perspectives and we’re committed to promoting an inclusive, inventive, and fun environment with amazing employees. We welcome and encourage applicants from all backgrounds to apply.

Rover is an equal opportunity employer. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, military or veteran status, sex, pregnancy, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, marital or partnership status, genetic predisposition, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws.

We are committed to work with you to look for reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

_

Title: Data Analyst II - Inventory & SEO Growth
Description:
Please submit your application by June 30, 2026 to be considered for this position. Applications received after this date may not be reviewed.

As a Data Analyst II within Growth Analytics, you’ll leverage some of the most robust web and app traffic data in the industry to generate insights that inform business strategies across product growth. You will analyze listings and traffic data, define and monitor key quality and growth metrics, evaluate experiments, and build scalable reporting solutions that enable confident, data-driven decision-making.

About The Role


Serve as a subject matter expert on Redfin’s listings data.
Leverage analytical expertise to deliver simple answers to complex, ambiguous questions.
Build and maintain Tableau dashboards supporting the Growth analytics reporting portfolio.
Define success metrics and perform rigorous analyses for experiments.
Collaborate with team members to review analyses, code, and dashboards, ensuring high-quality deliverables that answer the right questions.


About You


Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field required; advanced degree a plus.
3+ years of experience in an analytical role within a business environment.
Strong proficiency in SQL, Excel, Tableau, Python or R, and statistical analysis.
Experience with web/app traffic analytics and A/B test evaluation preferred.
Experience using AI code generation tools such as Snowflake Intelligence, Github CoPilot, and Anthropic Claude Code preferred.
Strong cross-functional collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across teams and build alignment.
Strong communication and data storytelling skills, with the ability to explain complex ideas clearly to non-technical audiences in both written and verbal formats.
Strong curiosity that doesn’t stop at the “What?” but seeks to understand the “Why?” and “What if?”


What You’ll Get

Our team members fuel our strategy, innovation and growth, so we ensure the health and well-being of not just you, but your family, too! We go above and beyond to give you the support you need on an individual level and offer all sorts of ways to help you live your best life. We are proud to offer eligible team members perks and health benefits that will help you have peace of mind. Simply put: We’ve got your back. Check out our full list of Benefits and Perks.

About Us

Redfin is revolutionizing the $75 billion real estate industry. We use data, beautiful software, and innovative design to put customers first at every step in the home-buying and selling process. Get ready to dive headfirst into our award-winning website and mobile apps, solving complex business problems in a highly visible, customer-centric way. If you value doing great work in a collaborative environment, join our team!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

On site in Redmond WA

Must have PLM tools experience

US Citizen or GC Holder only - no c2c, W2 required by client


Job Description for Data Analyst - PLM Project
GENERAL SUMMARY Under the specific direction of a senior analyst, supervisor or manager; you will collect, process, map, and analyze data to support the loading of accurate data for Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) system implementation. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to determine how data will be transformed from the current PLM system to the new PLM system and verify and validate the data loads for accuracy to ensure a successful implementation of the PLM. In this role you will be involved with data clean-up in the PLM of data objects and the correction and maintenance of attributes and other information; you will partner with business users to implement data updates in accordance with existing business processes where applicable. You will review systems requirements and business processes, codes, tests, debugs and implement software solutions for the PLM system. You will be expected to operate in an individual contributor role that requires the use of judgment in applying expertise and is expected to work independently with minimal supervision.
PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES

· Support the Project Lead/Work Stream Lead for Bills of Material, Parts and associated documentation
· Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data needs and business objectives
· Complete validation and verification of data for multiple loads of data into the PLM tool.
· Support and complete data clean-up by submitting requirements and collaborating with IT for PLM and ERP data clean-up and maintenance following the IT process for mass updates of data.
· Develop reports, dashboards, and data visualizations using tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Excel
· Analyze data to identify trends, patterns, and insights
· Translate complex data findings into clear, actionable insights
· Analyze, define, and develop documentation control methodologies to support the PLM tool
· Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency across systems
· Automate data processes and improve reporting efficiency where possible
· Pull data and analyze and make informed decisions and think critically
· Perform other related duties as assigned


BASIC QUALIFICATIONS:

· Bachelor's Degree is required


PREFERRED/DESIRED QUALIFICATIONS:

· Bachelor's degree in data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related field
· Proven experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar role
· 1-3 years of related experience in a business environment
· Experience implementing and configuring PLM systems
· Intermediate to advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office applications (Excel, Outlook, Word, PowerPoint)
· Professional presentation and strong interpersonal communications skills
· Strong proficiency in data analysis tools (e.g., Excel, SQL)
· Ability to interpret complex data and communicate insights clearly
· Experience working in PLM or other collaborative environments
· Proficient task management including prioritization skills
· Self-motivated, organized, efficient and process oriented
· Strong work ethic and Team player
· Tactful, exercises independent judgment and discretion
· Works well under pressure in a dynamic environment
· Must show strong judgment and time management skills
· Ability to independently write, review, and implement work instruction content in support of new systems and processes.

Desired Skills and Experience

Data Analyst
On site in Redmond WA
Must have PLM tools experience
US Citizen or GC Holder only - no c2c, W2 required by client



Dexian stands at the forefront of Talent + Technology solutions with a presence spanning more than 70 locations worldwide and a team exceeding 10,000 professionals. As one of the largest technology and professional staffing companies and one of the largest minority-owned staffing companies in the United States, Dexian combines over 30 years of industry expertise with cutting-edge technologies to deliver comprehensive global services and support.

Data Analyst

On site in Redmond WA

Must have PLM tools experience

US Citizen or GC Holder only - no c2c, W2 required by client


Job Description for Data Analyst - PLM Project
GENERAL SUMMARY Under the specific direction of a senior analyst, supervisor or manager; you will collect, process, map, and analyze data to support the loading of accurate data for Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) system implementation. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to determine how data will be transformed from the current PLM system to the new PLM system and verify and validate the data loads for accuracy to ensure a successful implementation of the PLM. In this role you will be involved with data clean-up in the PLM of data objects and the correction and maintenance of attributes and other information; you will partner with business users to implement data updates in accordance with existing business processes where applicable. You will review systems requirements and business processes, codes, tests, debugs and implement software solutions for the PLM system. You will be expected to operate in an individual contributor role that requires the use of judgment in applying expertise and is expected to work independently with minimal supervision.
PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • · Support the Project Lead/Work Stream Lead for Bills of Material, Parts and associated documentation
  • · Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data needs and business objectives
  • · Complete validation and verification of data for multiple loads of data into the PLM tool.
  • · Support and complete data clean-up by submitting requirements and collaborating with IT for PLM and ERP data clean-up and maintenance following the IT process for mass updates of data.
  • · Develop reports, dashboards, and data visualizations using tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Excel
  • · Analyze data to identify trends, patterns, and insights
  • · Translate complex data findings into clear, actionable insights
  • · Analyze, define, and develop documentation control methodologies to support the PLM tool
  • · Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency across systems
  • · Automate data processes and improve reporting efficiency where possible
  • · Pull data and analyze and make informed decisions and think critically
  • · Perform other related duties as assigned


BASIC QUALIFICATIONS:

  • · Bachelor's Degree is required


PREFERRED/DESIRED QUALIFICATIONS:

  • · Bachelor's degree in data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related field
  • · Proven experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar role
  • · 1-3 years of related experience in a business environment
  • · Experience implementing and configuring PLM systems
  • · Intermediate to advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office applications (Excel, Outlook, Word, PowerPoint)
  • · Professional presentation and strong interpersonal communications skills
  • · Strong proficiency in data analysis tools (e.g., Excel, SQL)
  • · Ability to interpret complex data and communicate insights clearly
  • · Experience working in PLM or other collaborative environments
  • · Proficient task management including prioritization skills
  • · Self-motivated, organized, efficient and process oriented
  • · Strong work ethic and Team player
  • · Tactful, exercises independent judgment and discretion
  • · Works well under pressure in a dynamic environment
  • · Must show strong judgment and time management skills
  • · Ability to independently write, review, and implement work instruction content in support of new systems and processes.
Desired Skills and Experience

Data Analyst
On site in Redmond WA
Must have PLM tools experience
US Citizen or GC Holder only - no c2c, W2 required by client

Title: Business Intelligence Engineer II
Description:
Job Description:

Business Intelligence Engineer II

Bellevue, WA – 7000 AI and Data Innovation – Full time – R29884

Job Description:

What you’ll do:


Own the design, development, and maintenance of ongoing metrics, reports, analyses, dashboards, etc. to drive key decisions and process improvements.
Work closely with the Supply Chain teams in areas including forecasting analytics and design, inventory placement, customer experience and replenishment management continuously optimizing for improved customer experience and in-stock positions.
Analyze demand and resource requirements against available resources to identify risks and opportunities and provide recommendations to solve for resource constraints.
Interface with other technology teams to extract, transform, and load data from a wide variety of data sources using SQL and various technological platforms.
Recognize and adopt best practices in reporting and analysis: data integrity, test design, analysis, validation, and documentation.
Respond to high priority requests from senior business leaders.
Continually improve ongoing reporting and analysis processes, automating or simplifying self-service support for secondary data sets.


What You’ll Need:


Minimum Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics, Supply Chain, Information Management, Data Science, or related field of study and 3 years of experience required as a Business Intelligence Analysts or a related position/occupation.
Alternate Education and Experience: Master’s degree in Business Analytics, Supply Chain, Information Management, Data Science, or related field of study and 1 year of experience required as a Business Intelligence Analysts or a related position/occupation.
Full term of experience must include:
SQL and data modeling within large-scale data warehouse or data lake environments.
Building automated data warehousing environments for production reports or tables.
Python and data visualization tools such as Streamlit/FastAPI/Plotly/Dash/React/D3Js etc.
Data visualization tools like Power BI, Tableau, Looker, Quicksight or similar.
Ability to draw insights from data and clearly communicate them to the stakeholders and senior management as required.
Experience must also include:
Data management fundamentals, storage principles, and developing scalable data transformation pipelines using dbt (data build tool) including modular SQL modeling, testing, and documentation.
Building AI-powered tools such as chatbots or AI agents to support data access, reporting, or automation.

Employer: Chewy, Inc.

Office Address: 1110 112th Avenue NE, Suite 500, Bellevue, WA 98004

Other: 5% domestic travel required.

The base salary range for this role is $148,000 to $180,500 per year.


The specific salary offered to a candidate may be influenced by a variety of factors including but not limited to the candidate’s relevant experience, education, and work location. In addition, this position is eligible for 401k and a new hire and annual equity grant. C08+ positions may also be eligible for annual bonus.


We offer different types of insurance and benefits, such as medical/Rx, vision, dental, life, disability, hospital indemnity, critical illness, and accident. We offer parental leave, family services benefits, backup dependent care, flexible spending accounts, telemedicine, pet adoption reimbursement, employee assistance program, and many discounts including 10% off pet insurance and 20% off at Chewy.com.

Exempt salary team members have unlimited PTO, subject to manager approval. Team members will receive six paid holidays per year. Team members may be eligible for paid sick and family leave in compliance with applicable state and local regulations.

Chewy is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, ancestry, national origin, gender, citizenship, marital status, religion, age, disability, gender identity, results of genetic testing, veteran status, as well as any other legally-protected characteristic. If you have a disability under the Americans with Disabilities Act or similar law, and you need an accommodation during the application process or to perform these job requirements, or if you need a religious accommodation, please contact CAAR@chewy.com.

To access Chewy's California CPRA Job Applicant Privacy Policy, please click here.

Title: BI Engineer - Data Analytics
Description:
Costco IT is responsible for the technical future of Costco Wholesale , the third largest retailer in the world with wholesale operations in fourteen countries. Despite our size and explosive international expansion, we continue to provide a family, employee centric atmosphere in which our employees thrive and succeed.

This is an environment unlike anything in the high-tech world and the secret of Costco’s success is its culture. The value Costco puts on its employees is well documented in articles from a variety of publishers including Bloomberg and Forbes. Our employees and our members come FIRST. Costco is well known for its generosity and community service and has won many awards for its philanthropy. The company joins with its employees to take an active role in volunteering by sponsoring many opportunities to help others.

Come join the Costco Wholesale IT family . Costco IT is a dynamic, fast-paced environment, working through exciting transformation efforts. We are building the next generation retail environment where you will be surrounded by dedicated and highly professional employees.

Business Intelligence (BI) Engineers are responsible for the successful delivery, design, and support of high visibility desktop reports, mobile reports, and dashboards. BI Engineers leverage BI applications in accordance with application development standards and best practices. BI Engineers work closely with Product Owners and UI/UX Designers during wireframe and prototype development. This role will also engage with Architects (Solution/Data), Engineers (Data, Platforms, DevOps, etc.), and Stakeholders to deliver timely, accurate, high performing, and reliable BI products and solutions to end-users. BI Engineers identify ways to improve data reliability, efficiency, and quality of data management required for building BI applications and reports.

If you want to be a part of one of the worldwide BEST companies “to work for”, simply apply and let your career be reimagined.

ROLE


Owns responsibility for end-to-end development and support of Costco’s BI reporting environments for ongoing metrics, reports, dashboards, etc.
Assists in the design and development of proof of concept and prototype application environments.
Adopts and adapts to newly approved technologies, programming languages, tools, and standards for business intelligence.
Collaborates to ensure application designs utilize appropriate patterns and standards.
Documents BI application specifications and produces operational support manuals.
Analyzes business needs and software requirements to determine the feasibility of design within time and cost constraints, including the ability to estimate work needed.
Identifies and interprets trends, patterns, and correlations among various data points.
Assesses functional and nonfunctional requirements and creates designs to meet both business and technical needs.
Designs builds and maintains automated reporting, dashboards, and analysis to enable teams to self-service data needs.
Supports defining technical requirements, such as tool or platform selection criteria for the reporting environments.
Assists in solution design efforts (i.e. analysis, data warehouse design, architecture) and understands the data warehouse or data marts implementation related to BI.
Takes full responsibility for the balance between functional, system management, and service quality.
Ensures the appropriate BI architectural standards are adhered to.
Tests and resolves problems, performs root cause analysis, identifies gaps, recommends solutions and preventative measures.
Automates, documents, shares, educates, delegates, and improves processes.
Applies knowledge to practical and sustainable applications and capabilities.
Partners with product owners, product managers, the solution leads, and other stakeholders to create and maintain robust datasets and models to support reporting and self-service solutions.
Builds prototypes and wireframes of potential features.
Translates business needs into technical specifications to deliver customized reporting and software solutions (BI, Advanced analytics, APIs/Services).
Works in tandem with BI/Solution Architects, Data Architects, Data Stewards, Data Engineers, Data Scientists, and Data Quality Engineers to design efficient visualizations, score cards, dashboards, reports; and recommends ongoing optimization of BI data models, data sets, and data quality.
Oversees the end-to-end development and support of Costco’s BI reporting environments for ongoing metrics, reports, dashboards using Power BI, Power App, Power Automate, Airflow, UC4, Cloud Run, Azure Functions, and other technologies as needed.
Creates data visualizations that illustrate data learnings and insights in an understandable and user-friendly manner through software UI / UX design using Power BI and other tools as needed.
Creates, refines, and optimizes dashboards for reporting applications by connecting to one or many of the following: Big Query, Oracle, SAP BW, SAP HANA, and Azure services, such as Azure SQL DW (Synapse), ADLS, Cosmos, Databricks, Delta-Lake and fabric mirroring to improve and speed up delivery of BI solutions.
Identifies, designs, and implements internal process improvements: automating manual processes, optimizing BI capabilities.
Identifies ways to improve data reliability, efficiency, and quality of data management required for building BI applications and reports.
Communicates technical concepts to non-technical audiences both in written and verbal form.
Performs peer reviews for other BI engineers’ work.


Required


3+ years’ experience working with BI technologies and tools, such as Microsoft Power BI, IBM Cognos, SAP Webi and Tableau; proven and successful implementations of Microsoft Power BI.
2+ years’ of experience with Power BI.
1+ years’ using programing languages, such as DAX, MDX, SQL, Python, Java, C#
1+ years’ BI related experience using ETL, Data warehousing management, Data Mining, Report Designer or development; ability to write complex SQL queries against a variety of data sources.
Experience in using Azure cloud architecture, low code power apps, visualization technologies.
Strong analytical and problem solving skills.


Recommended


BA/BS or higher in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent software/services experience.
Microsoft Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, Microsoft Power Platforms, IBM Cognos, Tableau certifications.
Azure Certifications.
Experience with Git / Azure DevOps.
Experience with Big Query.
Experience delivering reporting design, visualizations, and solutions through agile software development methodologies.
Experience working with sensitive data and ecommerce/distribution/logistics business areas.
Exposure to the retail industry.
Exposure to developing solutions for retail metrics (sales, inventory, margin, projections etc.).
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Experience with Airflow, UC4 Job Scheduler (or similar Job Schedulers), Cloud Run and Power Automate toolset.
Proficient in Google Workspace applications, including Sheets, Docs, Slides, and Gmail.


Required Documents


Cover Letter
Resume


California applicants, please click to review the Costco Applicant Privacy Notice.

Pay Ranges

Level 1 - $85,000 - $110,000

Level 2 - $105,000 - $135,000

Level 3 - $130,000 - $160,000

Level SR - $150,000 - $190,000, Bonus and Restricted Stock Unit (RSU) eligible

Level Staff - $180,000 - $225,000, Bonus and Restricted Stock Unit (RSU) eligible

We offer a comprehensive package of benefits including paid time off, health benefits - medical/dental/vision/hearing aid/pharmacy/behavioral health/employee assistance, health care reimbursement account, dependent care assistance plan, short-term disability and long-term disability insurance, AD&D insurance, life insurance, 401(k), stock purchase plan to eligible employees.

Costco is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace. Costco is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or any other legally protected status. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or the recruiting process, please send a request to IT-Recruiting@costco.com

If hired, you will be required to provide proof of authorization to work in the United States. In some cases, applicants and employees for selected positions will not be sponsored for work authorization, including, but not limited to H1-B visas.

Title: Supply Chain Analyst
Description:
Nintendo of America

About Nintendo of America: From the launch of the Nintendo Entertainment System™ more than 30 years ago, Nintendo’s mission has been to create smiles through unique entertainment experiences. Here at Nintendo of America Inc., we deliver on this mission by partnering closely with Nintendo Co., Ltd., to bring Nintendo’s iconic and cherished franchises including Mario™, Donkey Kong™, The Legend of Zelda™, Metroid™, Animal Crossing™, Pikmin™ and Splatoon™ across the Americas through our video games, hardware systems, and collaborations with partners on a range of other entertainment initiatives like feature films and theme parks.

We are an equal opportunity employer offering a welcoming and inclusive environment in service to one another, our products, and the diverse consumers and communities we call home. For more information about Nintendo, please visit the company’s website at https://www.nintendo.com/.

Summary

Leads complex reporting and analysis for assigned business teams to provide performance summaries across channels. Identify and deliver regular, actionable business and customer insights to business teams, NOA Leadership, and other stakeholders. Drive efficiencies through developing automated reporting/dashboards, maintaining related systems, developing new processes, implementing testing, and forecasting.

Description Of Duties


Act as a strategic partner to business teams holding regular insight touch points and proactively providing data driven recommendations.
Contribute to forecasting processes by interpreting data, documenting assumptions, refining models, and making operational adjustments to input / output.
Use analytical and database skills to develop reports and analysis to support partner teams.
Initiates and collaborates with team leadership to drive the creation of metrics, understand goals, and measure results.
Provide technical expertise in the design, development, and delivery of report automation; Automate frequently utilized reporting.
Manage data flow from data warehouse to visualization tools. Perform data auditing and troubleshooting.
Manage procedural responsibilities such as management of existing suites of automated reports.
Perform ad-hoc analyses to answer business questions or generate data sets.
Develop presentations of complex data and content for stakeholders in an accurate, understandable, and engaging manner.


Summary Of Requirements


3+ years of experience in a Business or BI Analyst role developing dashboards, analysis, reporting, data warehousing solutions.
Experience with time series forecasts and forecast model improvements.
Expertise in SQL, relational databases, data warehousing, automation.
Ability to work with extremely large datasets.
Experience in working with Data visualization tools such as Tableau, building interactive reports, complex dashboards for different audiences, creating calculated KPIs or metrics by writing complex formulas.
Ability to translate complex analytical work into meaningful insights and recommendations for business stakeholders preferred.
Proven ability to quickly learn new systems and tools.
Ability to work under pressure, to fixed deadlines.
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment with changing priorities.
Knowledge of how to extract, clean and manipulate data from multiple systems into reporting objects for analysis.
Requires working knowledge and experience in own job family and broadens capabilities; has worked with and is proficient with current practices and procedures.
Undergraduate degree or equivalent in computer science, information systems, engineering, economics, or other technical field.
For Bilingual, must be proficient in target language.


Applicants must be legally eligible to work in the United States to be considered. Visa sponsorship is not available for this role.

This position is hybrid in Redmond, WA. Hybrid positions require regular onsite work following the schedule and guidelines for their division. This position is not open to fully remote status at this time.

This position includes the base pay range listed below, potential for a semi-annual discretionary performance bonus, and a comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k), and paid time off.

Pay Range

$94,250—$150,800 USD

  • Provide technical expertise in the design, development, and delivery of report automation; Automate frequently utilized reporting.
  • Manage data flow from data warehouse to visualization tools. Perform data auditing and troubleshooting.
  • Manage procedural responsibilities such as management of existing suites of automated reports.
  • Perform ad-hoc analyses to answer business questions or generate data sets.
  • Develop presentations of complex data and content for stakeholders in an accurate, understandable, and engaging manner.

Summary Of Requirements

  • 3+ years of experience in a Business or BI Analyst role developing dashboards, analysis, reporting, data warehousing solutions.
  • Experience with time series forecasts and forecast model improvements.
  • Expertise in SQL, relational databases, data warehousing, automation.
  • Ability to work with extremely large datasets.
  • Experience in working with Data visualization tools such as Tableau, building interactive reports, complex dashboards for different audiences, creating calculated KPIs or metrics by writing complex formulas.
  • Ability to translate complex analytical work into meaningful insights and recommendations for business stakeholders preferred.
  • Proven ability to quickly learn new systems and tools.
  • Ability to work under pressure, to fixed deadlines.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment with changing priorities.
  • Knowledge of how to extract, clean and manipulate data from multiple systems into reporting objects for analysis.
  • Requires working knowledge and experience in own job family and broadens capabilities; has worked with and is proficient with current practices and procedures.
  • Undergraduate degree or equivalent in computer science, information systems, engineering, economics, or other technical field.
  • For Bilingual, must be proficient in target language.

Applicants must be legally eligible to work in the United States to be considered. Visa sponsorship is not available for this role.

This position is hybrid in Redmond, WA. Hybrid positions require regular onsite work following the schedule and guidelines for their division. This position is not open to fully remote status at this time.

This position includes the base pay range listed below, potential for a semi-annual discretionary performance bonus, and a comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k), and paid time off.

Pay Range

$94,250—$150,800 USD

Title: Mid-Level Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Job Description

At Boeing, we innovate and collaborate to make the world a better place. We’re committed to fostering an environment for every teammate that’s welcoming, respectful and inclusive, with great opportunity for professional growth. Find your future with us.

The Boeing Company is currently seeking a Mid-Level Business Intelligence Analyst to join the team in Seattle, WA; Berkeley, MO; Hazelwood, MO; or Dallas, TX.

Partners in the design of Business Intelligence information models, dashboards, metrics and reports using standard technologies and methodologies that meet business objectives. Combines SQL, Business Intelligence (BI) best practices, cloud data engineering awareness, and domain knowledge to deliver efficient and scalable BI solutions that improve productivity and business outcomes. This role is primarily a BI Analyst role but requires practical familiarity with Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) and prompt engineering techniques to accelerate analytics, automate workflows, and prototype Large Language Model (LLM)-enabled features.

Position Responsibilities:



Design reusable BI information models, views, dashboards, metrics and reports that meet business needs across supply chain and related functions
Translate business requirements into technical designs, user stories and acceptance criteria; validate delivered solutions with stakeholders
Build, optimize and maintain SQL (Redshift/Oracle/Teradata/SQL Server) queries, Extract Transfer Load (ETL)/data wrangling pipelines and data transformations for reliable reporting
Produce analytics deliverables (ad-hoc analysis, trend reports, root-cause investigations) to support decision-making
Prototype and evaluate GenAI/LLM use cases to augment BI workflows (e.g., natural-language querying, automated data summarization, intelligent insights generation, prompt templates for analysts)
Apply prompt engineering best practices: design, test, and optimize prompts and chains for consistent, accurate outputs; document prompt libraries and guardrails
Integrate LLM/GenAI capabilities with BI tools or data platforms where appropriate (e.g., Application Programming Interfaces (APIs), wrappers, microservices) in collaboration with engineers and data scientists
Ensure responsible use of GenAI: implement data privacy, Personally Identifiable Information (PII) handling, output validation, and model risk mitigation practices
Collaborate with data scientists, Machine Learning (ML) engineers, and platform teams on productionizing Artificial Intelligence (AI)-enabled features and understanding model performance and limitations
Develop and deliver stakeholder-facing demos, playbooks, and training materials that show how GenAI augments BI workflows
Work with cloud data platforms (Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and Amazon Web Services (AWS)) to design, develop, and maintain data products — including data ingestion, storage, transformation, and serving layers
Build and validate data products on cloud platforms (BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataflow, AWS S3, Redshift, Glue, etc.) and collaborate with platform/engineering teams on deploying them to production
Participate in agile delivery: sprint planning, estimation, development, testing and release activities
Identify opportunities to improve automation, reduce analyst toil, and scale analysis via templated assets and self-service



Basic Qualifications (Required Skills/Experience):



5+ years of experience in Business Intelligence, Analytics, or Data Engineering roles
Experience with SQL and relational databases (Oracle, Teradata, SQL Server)
Experience working with cloud platforms (GCP and/or AWS)
Experience with core data services (storage, ETL/streaming, warehouses, serverless computing)
Experience building data products on cloud platforms (ingestion, transformation, cataloging, serving)
Experience with BI/visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI and ability to design scalable dashboards and data models
Experience with Python and/or R for data preparation, analysis, and/or prototyping
Experience with data modeling, ETL patterns, data quality practices, and common BI architecture
Experience with Agile, DevOps and release methodologies



Preferred Qualifications (Desired Skills/Experience):



Master’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, Data Science, Business Analytics, or related field
Experience with strong communication skills with the ability to translate business problems into analytics solutions
Experience working in supply chain, manufacturing, and/or aerospace domains
Experience with GenAI/LLM tools and APIs (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, or on-prem alternatives) and prompt engineering for analytics use cases
Experience building and/or integrating LLM-powered features: natural-language-to-SQL, summary/explainability layers, intelligent alerting, and/or conversational analytics
Experience with model operationalization concepts (MLOps), versioning, monitoring, and latency/throughput tradeoffs
Experience with secure and compliant handling of data when using LLMs, including PII redaction techniques and enterprise governance practices
Experience with automation frameworks, orchestration (Airflow, Prefect), and/or microservice APIs for embedding GenAI into workflows
Experience prototyping production-ready PoCs that demonstrate measurable business value from GenAI and/or ML enhancements
Experience with scripting and tooling for prompt testing, evaluation metrics, and prompt libraries
Experience with cloud-native managed services on GCP and AWS for data products (e.g., BigQuery, Dataflow, Dataproc, Cloud Composer, S3, Redshift, Glue, Lambda)



Drug Free Workplace:

Boeing is a Drug Free Workplace where post offer applicants and employees are subject to testing for marijuana, cocaine, opioids, amphetamines, PCP, and alcohol when criteria is met as outlined in our policies.

Pay & Benefits:

At Boeing, we strive to deliver a Total Rewards package that will attract, engage and retain the top talent. Elements of the Total Rewards package include competitive base pay and variable compensation opportunities.

The Boeing Company also provides eligible employees with an opportunity to enroll in a variety of benefit programs, generally including health insurance, flexible spending accounts, health savings accounts, retirement savings plans, life and disability insurance programs, and a number of programs that provide for both paid and unpaid time away from work.

The specific programs and options available to any given employee may vary depending on eligibility factors such as geographic location, date of hire, and the applicability of collective bargaining agreements.

Pay is based upon candidate experience and qualifications, as well as market and business considerations.

Summary pay range: $105,400 - $152,950

Applications for this position will be accepted until Jun. 15, 2026

Export Control Requirements:

This position must meet U.S. export control compliance requirements. To meet U.S. export control compliance requirements, a “U.S. Person” as defined by 22 C.F.R.



120.62 is required. “U.S. Person” includes U.S. Citizen, U.S. National, lawful permanent resident, refugee, or asylee.



Export Control Details:

US based job, US Person required

Relocation

This position offers relocation based on candidate eligibility.

Visa Sponsorship

Employer will not sponsor applicants for employment visa status.

Shift

This position is for 1st shift

Equal Opportunity Employer:

Boeing is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, physical or mental disability, genetic factors, military/veteran status or other characteristics protected by law.
