Title: Data Analyst Coordinator
Place: Happy Valley–Goose Bay, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
Description:
Location: Happy Valley-Goose Bay, NL

Department: Human Resources

Employment Type: Full-Time

Reports To: Chief Executive Officer

Relocation to Happy Valley-Goose Bay, NL is required for this position.

About Mamu Tshishkutamashutau Innu Education

Located in Happy Valley-Goose Bay, Labrador, Mamu Tshishkutamashutau Innu Education (MTIE) is dedicated to supporting the educational journey of the Innu Nation. With two schools in Natuashish and Sheshatshiu, MTIE serves approximately 1,000 students from Kindergarten to Grade 12, fostering a culturally grounded and student-centered learning environment.

MTIE is committed to building a collaborative and supportive workplace where staff are empowered to contribute to the success and well-being of students and the broader Innu community.

About The Role

We are seeking a Data Analyst Coordinator to join our team. Reporting to the Chief Executive Officer, the Data Analyst Coordinator supports organizational planning, accountability, and continuous improvement through the collection, analysis, interpretation, and reporting of data.

Working closely with Directors, school administrators, project leads, and other stakeholders, the Data Analyst Coordinator develops meaningful insights, dashboards, and reporting tools that support evidence-based decision making across the organization. This role plays a key part in helping MTIE measure progress, evaluate programs, and strengthen outcomes for students and communities.

This position is based in Happy Valley-Goose Bay, NL, a regional hub in Labrador offering essential services, strong community infrastructure, and access to outdoor recreation.

Key Responsibilities


Collect, organize, and maintain data from multiple sources, including student information systems, attendance records, assessments, operational data, and program reports
Analyze student, school, program, and organizational data to identify trends, patterns, opportunities, and areas for improvement
Develop dashboards, visualizations, and reporting tools that support evidence-based decision making
Prepare regular and ad hoc reports for the Chief Executive Officer, Directors, Board of Trustees, funding agencies, and external partners
Support program evaluation and performance measurement using both quantitative and qualitative data
Collaborate with school administrators, Directors, project leads, and other stakeholders to identify key performance indicators and reporting requirements
Support strategic planning, resource allocation, and organizational improvement initiatives through data-informed analysis
Provide guidance and support to staff in the interpretation and use of data


Requirements

Qualifications

Education


Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Statistics, Information Systems, Business Analytics, Education, or a related field
Graduate education in a related discipline is considered an asset
Relevant certifications in data analytics, business intelligence, or project management are considered assets


Experience


Minimum 3 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, or performance measurement
Experience working in an education, public sector, Indigenous, or community-based environment is considered an asset
Experience working with student information systems, databases, and reporting platforms is preferred


Knowledge and Skills


Strong analytical, research, and problem-solving skills
Ability to translate complex data into clear and meaningful insights for diverse audiences
Experience developing dashboards, visualizations, and performance reports
Knowledge of data governance, privacy, and information management principles
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail
Proficiency with data analysis tools, spreadsheet applications, database systems, and Google Workspace
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Demonstrated cultural competence, including experience working with Indigenous communities and awareness of Innu culture and values; fluency in Innu-Aimun is considered an asset


Key Competencies


Analytical thinking
Attention to detail
Communication and collaboration
Strategic planning
Problem solving
Organizational awareness
Integrity and accountability


Benefits

Relocation and Regional Benefits


Relocation allowance of $2,500
Labrador living allowance
Annual travel allowance for personal use


Compensation And Benefits


Competitive salary
100% employer-paid premiums for group insurance benefits
Registered Pension Plan (RPP) with employer matching up to 5.5% of base salary
Employer-funded RRSP contributions of 7% of base salary


Professional Development


Professional development opportunities
Tuition assistance may be available for approved programs of study, subject to organizational policy and approval


Work-Life Balance


Annual fitness and wellness allowance
Generous leave entitlements
Extended office closures during Christmas and Easter

Title: Decision Science Analyst
Description:
Come join Pattison Food Group Ltd., a Jim Pattison business, and Canada’s largest Western-based provider of food and health products.

Pattison Food Group Ltd. is a certified Great Place to Work® and consists of Save-On-Foods and other well-known grocery banners. With nearly 300 retail locations, we proudly employ nearly 30,000 team members.

We have an exciting opportunity for a full-time Decision Science Analyst to join our More Rewards team in Langley, BC.

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Customer Data & Analytics, you will be responsible for leveraging all aspects of data and analytics to support large-scale projects, experimentation, and continuous improvement initiatives that guide decision-making and deliver high-quality insights. In this role, you will support the development and management of customer data and insights to support the More Rewards Loyalty Program, Marketing, Retail Operations and Merchandising.

You will be responsible for:


Collaborating with stakeholders to understand business questions, processes and KPIs.
Formulating testable hypotheses aligned with business questions and conducting statistical experiments in a live environment.
Writing SQL scripts to automate data pipelines and workflows.
Using Python scripts in a Jupyter Notebook environment for exploratory data analysis, data wrangling and statistical testing.
Developing and maintaining dashboards and visualizations in Power BI to convey insights, report on KPIs and test results.
Learning cloud-based analytics environments and big data tools to build analytics workflows on large datasets.
Discovering unique and creative insights to identify new opportunities for driving business value.
Becoming an expert in the relevant datasets within your analytics domain.
Supporting the Data & Analytics team with key projects.



You have:


A degree in a STEM discipline with 3+ years of relevant experience in data and analytics.
Advanced Python coding skills and understanding of SQL.
Experience with additional programming languages is an asset.
Experience using experimental design principles such as A/B and multivariate testing and working with business stakeholders to formulate testable hypotheses.
An understanding of advanced analytics and data science methods, such as anomaly detection, forecasting, and supervised & unsupervised learning.
Working knowledge of cloud environments, such as Azure, GCP or AWS. Experience with big data tools such as Spark and Databricks is an asset.



You are:


A confident communicator, both verbally and written, with strong interpersonal skills and the ability to communicate a story with data and insights to non-technical stakeholders.
A problem solver with strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
Effective in task management and delivering high-quality work.
Capable of multitasking and thriving in a fast-paced environment.
Self-motivated with the ability to work independently and collaboratively.



What we offer:

In addition to a highly competitive salary, we offer a great range of benefits, a company pension plan, free parking, exclusive team member offers, and opportunities for development and career progression.

If you’re looking to join a team that puts people first, you’re in the right place. Apply online today!

Compensation Details:

$83,394.00 - $104,243.00

The compensation offered for this position will take into consideration location, education, skills, experience and other factors.

Title: Data & Automation Analyst
Description:
Data & Automation Analyst (on-site, Calgary)




POSITION SUMMARY:

The Data & Automation Analyst plays a key role in delivering data-driven solutions by combining automation, data engineering, analytics, and emerging AI capabilities. This position focuses on translating business needs into scalable data and automation solutions while supporting the growth of a high-performing analytics function.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:




1. Automation & Digital Solutions

• Design, develop, and implement automated workflows to improve operational efficiency

• Leverage modern automation tools and low-code platforms to streamline business processes

• Support AI and advanced analytics initiatives, including use case development and implementation

• Identify and deliver opportunities for process optimization and digital transformation




2. Data Engineering & Management

• Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from multiple structured and unstructured sources

• Ensure data quality, integrity, and consistency across systems

• Develop and maintain scalable data models to support analytics and reporting

• Collaborate on data architecture and integration across enterprise platforms




3. Analytics & Business Intelligence

• Design and develop interactive dashboards and reporting solutions using modern BI tools

• Translate business requirements into actionable insights and analytical frameworks

• Deliver data-driven recommendations that support operational and strategic decision-making

• Connect analytics outputs to measurable business outcomes




4. Solution Design & Delivery

• Conduct requirements gathering and analysis with stakeholders

• Design end-to-end data, reporting, and automation solutions

• Translate complex and ambiguous business problems into structured, scalable data solutions




5. Team Support & Mentorship

• Provide guidance and mentorship to junior analysts

• Review deliverables to ensure quality and consistency

• Contribute to the development of analytics, automation, and AI capabilities within the team




6. Stakeholder Engagement

• Partner with cross-functional teams to deliver data and automation solutions

• Communicate technical concepts and insights effectively to non-technical audiences

• Act as a trusted advisor for data-driven initiatives




7. Training & Enablement

• Deliver knowledge sharing sessions and training on analytics tools and best practices

• Support adoption of data, automation, and reporting solutions across the organization




ROLE REQUIREMENTS (Technical Emphasis)

• Strong experience with data analytics, automation, and business intelligence solutions

• Hands-on expertise with Microsoft Power Platform, including Power BI and Power Automate

• Strong proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, and analysis

• Experience with data modeling, ETL processes, and data integration across multiple systems

• Familiarity with Microsoft 365 ecosystem and integration with data and automation tools

• Exposure to Azure data services or cloud-based data platforms is considered an asset

• Experience building interactive dashboards and reporting solutions

• Working knowledge of automation tools and low-code/no-code platforms

• Exposure to AI and machine learning concepts, including practical application in analytics workflows

• Strong understanding of data governance, quality, and reporting best practices

• Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions and analytical outputs

• Strong problem-solving, critical thinking, and analytical skills

• Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage both technical and business stakeholders

• Demonstrated ability to work in fast-paced, collaborative environments




REQUIRED EXPERIENCE / EDUCATION

• Post-secondary education in data analytics, computer science, information systems, or a related field, or equivalent experience

• 2+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or data engineering roles

• Experience mentoring or supporting junior team members is considered an asset




COMPENSATION

• Salary range: $75,000 – $85,000

Title: Demand Planning Analyst
Place: St John’s, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
Description:
Location: Canada (Remote or In-Person)

About The Role

We are seeking a highly analytical and organized Demand Planning Analyst to lead our forecasting plans, improve supply confidence, and drive cross-functional alignment across brands and sales channels.

This role is responsible for developing and maintaining demand forecasts using historical sales data, market trends, promotional activity, and strategic business insights. The Demand Planner will partner closely with Sales, Marketing, Operations, Finance, Product Development, and Procurement to support business planning, budgeting, new product launches, and sourcing initiatives. This individual will proactively monitor forecast performance, inventory health, and production schedules, communicate risks and opportunities to leadership, and continuously improve planning tools, reporting, and ERP data integrity.

Responsibilities


Develop and maintain accurate demand forecasts across brands and sales channels using historical sales data, market trends, promotional activity, and strategic insights.
Partner cross-functionally with sales, marketing, and operations to align forecasts with business plans and customer demand.
Work closely with Finance to support the Planning and Budgeting rollup.
Assist Product Development team during new product launches by determining opening order forecasts and sales channel allocation.
Monitor forecast accuracy, inventory health, and production schedules to identify stockout risks proactively.
Steward forecast performance to leadership by highlighting trends, insights, and risks and translate into action plans to improve operations.
Support Procurement initiatives by providing analyses for ad-hoc sourcing proposals, awards, and commitments.
Maintain data integrity in ERP and planning systems, improve forecasting tools, reporting, and processes through ongoing continuous improvement.


What We’re Looking For


Minimum 3 years’ experience in supply/demand planning and forecasting, ideally in a fast-moving CPG industry.
Direct experience with developing and stewarding forecasting models that manage more than $1M of monthly sales
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Data Science, or a related field.
Strong analytical and quantitative skills, with experience in data visualization.
Advanced knowledge in Excel (nested formulas, fuzzy lookups, PowerPivot/Query)
Experience using Python, R, Power BI, or SQL is preferred, but not required.
Strong communication skills, both verbally and written.
Previous experience supporting multiple stakeholders.
Familiarity with ERP systems.


Who Thrives Here


People who take initiative, spot problems early, and bring solutions before being asked.
Detail-oriented, highly organized, and comfortable managing multiple priorities simultaneously.
Thrive in a test-and-learn environment where experimentation and iteration are core to success.
People who are data-driven and are confident in their thinking but can adapt and adjust their thinking in the presence of new information.


What We Offer


You’ve got the skills for the job and deserve proper compensation! We offer competitive salaries so you can bring your best self to work.
We offer health insurance, including dental, EFAP, and Group RRSP with employer matching
Our Healthy Living Program reimburses health & wellness expenses up to $500/year
High-performing people need a healthy balance. Take advantage of our office gym!
Live the brands you work on. Free monthly supplement orders from our four brands, Jacked Factory, Transparent Labs, Nested Naturals and Cira Nutrition.
Our team never stops learning. We provide self-development assistance so you can continue to grow your career in, and out of the office.


If you are looking for a role that will allow you to grow within a dynamic team, we encourage you to apply! Send your application directly on LinkedIn or by emailing jobs@nutraholdings.com with the subject line “Demand Planning Analyst”

The successful candidate must be flexible and open to occasional business travel.

All qualified candidates are encouraged to apply, however, preference will be given to Canadian Citizens and Permanent Residents.

Title: Content Analyst
Description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

The team:

We are a unified, collaborative marketing team focused on creating meaningful client connections that drive business growth. We combine deep insights with strong partnerships to deliver timely, relevant, and impactful experiences for our clients. Our team is committed to continuous learning, innovation, and excellence in everything we do. We align closely with distribution partners to generate demand and unlock new opportunities across the business. Above all, we measure and optimize our efforts to ensure we consistently deliver value, drive engagement, and strengthen loyalty to Invesco.

The role:

The Content Enablement Content Analyst is a key role within Invesco’s Global Content Enablement team, supporting the Americas & EMEA Marketing organization. This position executes the content strategy and roadmap to enhance the quality, accessibility, and usability of content across the EMEA region. The role focuses on building and managing content infrastructure, ensuring accurate categorization, distribution, and access across platforms and audiences. It partners closely with business and technology stakeholders to deliver consistent, well-governed content solutions and ensure requirements are clearly defined and tested. This hands-on role is critical to improving content quality and driving engagement, ultimately enabling a stronger client and investment experience.

Responsibilities of the role:

Provides analytics support primarily for EMEA content domains, with additional support for North America.
Partners closely with EMEA Content Product Owners to deliver analysis across development, deployment, and research initiatives.
Defines and standardizes content quality metrics, building reporting and dashboards to provide visibility into content performance for senior leadership.
Collaborates with EMEA and NA domain experts to develop and maintain global taxonomy, aligning stakeholders to standardized definitions and fields.
Bridges business and technology teams to ensure solutions meet requirements and align with strategic priorities.
Maps business processes, develops expertise in content sources, and provides recommendations to improve content effectiveness.
Translates stakeholder needs into user stories and supports execution of content initiatives, including testing, impact analysis, and cross-team coordination.
Leads governance and documentation efforts, including content quality monitoring, process improvement, issue escalation, and maintaining records in tools such as Jira and Confluence.


Requirements of the role:

Bachelor’s degree, 5+ years in technology platforms, project management, or content development and distribution.
Experience with CMS and/or DAM platforms, content lifecycle management, and content migrations, including API-based integrations.
Familiarity with AI-assisted content analysis and UX or digital marketing concepts.
Strong skills in workflow/process mapping, with the ability to manage multiple priorities, meet deadlines, and operate effectively in ambiguous environments.
Advanced technical proficiency, including SQL and Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables; VBA a plus), along with strong desktop tool capabilities.
Strong analytical and statistical reasoning skills, with ability to organize, analyze, and communicate large data sets with accuracy and attention to detail.
Knowledge of content categorization (campaign, program, asset class, product), with preferred experience in investment or asset management content.
Client-focused, execution-oriented professional with strong communication, organizational, relationship-building, and influencing skills, plus a data-driven approach to measuring content performance and impact.


The salary range for this position is $55,000 to $61,000 per year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success and we strive to provide employees with a competitive total rewards package which includes:

Hybrid working environment
RRSP Contributions
Health & Wellness Benefits
Work flexibility Programs
Parental Leave Benefits
Study Support


The above information on this description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this role. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job. The job holder may be required to perform other duties as deemed appropriate by their manager from time to time.

Invesco is committed to fair and accessible employment practices. If selected for an interview, we will work with you to ensure that your interview is accessible and accommodation is provided. Please contact us at accessibility@invesco.ca or 1.800.874.6275 to let us know if you require accommodation for an interview due to a disability.

Title: Systems Analyst II - Data Analysis and Migration
Description:
Description

Join our team

Do you thrive in a fast-paced, team centric environment? Are you a detailed-oriented individual who loves solving problems by leveraging creative solutions? Do you want to be in a space where your knowledge and experience are used and fostered? Read on as this is the job for you!

As a key member of the TELUS Health team, you will be joining a growing team of Analysts in transforming and converting health data for Canadian physicians on a TELUS EMR (Electronic Medical Record) platform. This exciting opportunity will allow you to leverage your experience and skillset within an innovative and collaborative environment while performing meaningful work that is crucial to the success of our TELUS Health EMR platforms.

Here’s How

As part of the team, you’ll be responsible for:



Extracting data from various source systems and data stores by performing light business coding (ex. Java, Python, C#, etc.)
Connecting with our customers to conduct requirement gathering and performing data validations
Mapping and transforming data using various techniques (ex. XSLT, MapForce, scripting, etc.) based on customer input and requirements
Performing ad-hoc queries (ex. SQL, LINQ, etc.) and reports to analyse customer data before and after migration
Planning and performing go-live transformation and validation to ensure no data is lost or corrupted
Executing production data migrations (requires occasional evening/weekend work)
Collaboration with various teams across the business to identify roadblocks and resolve issues, to ensure timely completion for each project
Contributing to on-going development and refinement of data migration tools and processes
Tracking issues, communicating project status, and managing project risk



What You Bring

Required Experience



Graduation from a post-secondary institution with a Diploma specializing in related technologies
1 year minimum experience working on data transformation, development, or school related projects



Technical Knowledge



SQL/TSQL
Data Transformation Scripting and Mapping (ex. XML, XSL/XPath)
Familiarity with databases such as MSSQL, Oracle, MySQL, etc.
Scripting (ex. Python/Groovy) or development (ex. Java/C#)
Linux/Unix and Groovy an asset
MapForce an asset



Functional Skills



Detail oriented
Avid problem solver
Ability to communicate both verbally and written in English, French is an asset
Ability to multi-task and manage numerous concurrent projects
Strong analysis and problem solving skills with the ability to identify root cause
Ability to communicate in an effective and respectful manner with customers and fellow team members
Self-directed in completing project-based work



Great to Have



Agile & Project Management skills
Understanding of Network Infrastructure
Electronic Medical Records (EMR), Medical or clinical workflow experience
Experience with HL7, CoPD and CDS/CMS XML standards
Experience with the Atlassian tool suite (ex. Jira, Bitbucket, Confluence)

Title: Analytics Data Engineer
Description:
✅ Analytics Data Engineer

📍 Waterloo, ON (4 days onsite)

📅 12‑month contract (extendable)

📌 T4 employment - $60 - 75/HR

Overview

This analytics data team is responsible for transforming large‑scale operational and telemetry‑style data into trusted, actionable insights that directly influence engineering decisions, product strategy, customer experience, and executive direction.

This role sits at the intersection of analytics, data engineering, and business strategy . You will own the end‑to‑end lifecycle of data - from validating and understanding raw data sources, to structuring and analyzing datasets, to delivering insights that stakeholders can confidently act on.

You will work closely with engineering, product, and business teams to ensure data accurately reflects real‑world behavior and supports meaningful decision‑making. Success in this role comes from the ability to work through ambiguity, validate data rigorously, and translate technical findings into clear business outcomes .

This is not a purely pipeline‑driven role. The team is looking for someone who understands what the data means and how it drives decisions , not just how it is built.

Key Responsibilities

🔹 Analytics, Data Quality & Insight Delivery (~60%)


Analyze large, complex datasets to uncover trends, patterns, and key performance drivers
Validate and ensure data quality and integrity across the full analytical lifecycle
Translate loosely defined business problems into structured analytical approaches
Apply analytical and statistical techniques (e.g., trend analysis, variation analysis, basic hypothesis testing)
Deliver insights through clear visualizations, dashboards, and stakeholder-ready narratives
Partner with stakeholders to ensure data reflects real-world business and product context



🔹 Data Engineering & Pipeline Development (~30%)


Design, build, and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines across structured and semi-structured data sources
Work with APIs and large-scale data ingestion processes
Transform raw data into clean, analytics-ready datasets
Contribute to cloud-based data workflows (GCP preferred; AWS/Azure experience transferable)
Support scalable and maintainable data architecture for long-term analytics



🔹 Collaboration & Data Enablement (~10%)


Partner with cross-functional teams to gather requirements and deliver solutions
Maintain shared data models, documentation, and analytics frameworks
Ensure data solutions are reliable, reusable, and scalable
Communicate findings effectively to both technical and non-technical audiences



Required Qualifications


5+ years of experience working with SQL and Python for data analysis and transformation
Strong experience working with large and complex datasets, including data validation and troubleshooting
Proven ability to translate business questions into analytical solutions and insights
Experience building or supporting data pipelines / ETL workflows
Strong understanding of data quality, reconciliation, and root cause analysis
Experience working directly with stakeholders and delivering insights
Exposure to cloud data platforms (GCP preferred; AWS/Azure also acceptable)
Experience with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, etc.)



Nice to Have


Experience with statistical modeling, experimentation, or predictive analysis
Experience working with high-volume or event-based datasets (e.g., telemetry, IoT)
Exposure to GCP tools such as BigQuery, Dataflow, or dbt



Work Environment


Hybrid model: 4 days onsite in Waterloo
Fast-paced, highly collaborative team working closely with business stakeholders



✅ What Success Looks Like


You understand not just how data is built, but what it represents and how it drives decisions
You ensure data is accurate, trusted, and validated before use
You can take ambiguous problems and turn them into clear analytical outcomes
You communicate complex findings in a way that enables real business action



Job Type & Location

This is a Contract position based out of Waterloo, ON.

Pay And Benefits

The pay range for this position is $70.00 - $80.00/hr.

Workplace Type

This is a hybrid position in Waterloo,ON.

À propos de TEKsystems et TEKsystems Global Services

Nous sommes un fournisseur de services aux entreprises et de technologies. Nous accélérons la transformation de nos clients. Notre compétence en stratégie, conception, exécution et opérations libère la valeur de l’entreprise par un éventail de solutions. Nous sommes une équipe de 80 000 personnes qui collaborent avec plus de 60 000 clients, notamment 80 % du Fortune 500 en Amérique du Nord, Europe et Asie, qui collaborent avec nous dans le cadre de nos capacités full-stack et notre rythme. Nous sommes des penseurs stratégiques, des collaborateurs pratiques qui aident les clients à exploiter le changement et maîtriser le dynamisme de la technologie. Nous bâtissons le futur en livrant les résultats et en créant un impact positif dans nos communautés mondiales. TEKsystems et TEKsystems Global Services sont des entreprises d’Allegis Group. Découvrez d’autres informations à TEKsystems.com.

Utilisation de l’intelligence artificielle (IA): Nous pouvons utiliser l’intelligence artificielle (IA) pour soutenir certaines étapes de notre processus d’embauche, notamment la recherche, la présélection et l’évaluation des candidatures. L’IA aide à analyser les candidatures et les qualifications, mais les décisions finales sont prises par notre équipe de recrutement. We’re a leading provider of business and technology services. We accelerate business transformation for our customers. We’re strategic thinkers, hands-on collaborators, helping customers capitalize on change and master the momentum of technology. We’re building tomorrow by delivering business outcomes and making positive impacts in our global communities. TEKsystems and TEKsystems Global Services are Allegis Group companies. Title: Intermediate Mining Data Analyst
Description:
Be part of something incredible. Metals are necessary for our world, but mining historically faced issues with waste, sustainability, and inefficiencies. MineSense is changing that. Our solutions reduce mining waste and environmental impacts while increasing profits and efficiencies. Backed by the best in venture capital and government funds, and recognized by tech industry and sustainability programs, MineSense is doing what many said could not be done: improving mining for people and planet.







We are looking to add an Intermediate Mining Data Analyst to our GeoScience team. The role will report to the Manager, GeoScience and be based out of the Vancouver office. The ideal candidate will have a strong interest in detailed data analysis and statistics within the mining industry. You will work with clients globally, to incorporate MineSense solutions into their ore control processes and show them the value of using our systems. If you enjoy working with data, collaborating with multidisciplinary groups, and have a mining, geology, or geophysics background, we want to hear from you!













Job Responsibilities:




Helping new and existing clients leverage MineSense technology in their grade control engineering processes, which includes developing client facing reports and presentations.
Collaborating with internal teams to develop prediction models by correlating mine grade measurements or estimates with our sensor results to identify patterns, trends or other data features that relate to grade, texture, mineralogy, or lithology.
Working with the Field Service and Product Development team to further enhance our products and services through system data analysis and data quality assurance/control.
Determining and tracking data model accuracy and precision through comparison with sampled assay grades or grade estimates.
Reporting and presenting data results/trends to internal teams, as well as external clients.
Working closely with the Data team to create queries, scripts, and reports while ensuring data is well organized and maintained.
Building and maintaining good working relationships with our clients.
Promotes a positive company culture through sharing of information and supporting the learning and development of all colleagues.
Additional duties as occasionally required by your manager.




Qualifications:

Degree in Earth Sciences (Geology, Geophysics), Geostatistics, Data Analytics, Mining Engineering or related field.
Minimum of 2-4 years of relevant working experience, where experience in mining is preferred.
Experience performing statistical analysis or data science in a work setting is preferred.
Mining experience with in-grade control or working with ore control models is preferred.
Experience with data analysis tools such as python or other statistical packages.
Experience with SQL is preferred.
Experience and passion for solving analytical problems involving diverse data sets using quantitative approaches to generate insights.
Attention to detail and an interest in problem solving.
Oral and written proficiency in English is required.
Additional oral and written proficiency in Spanish is strongly preferred.
The ability to travel and support global offices is required (10-30%).
Legally able to work in Canada.




The salary offered to the successful candidate will be based on their experience, knowledge and skills they bring to MineSense, market data based on their location as well as internal equity. Keeping this mind, the salary offered can be outside of the range posted.




Our total rewards package offers market-researched benefits, promotes internal equity, and supports team members at all levels of our organization, and includes the following:




Our total rewards package includes:

· A competitive salary and bonus.

· Matching RRSP or 401K program (for Canadian and US employees)

· A comprehensive benefit program including an employee assistance program.

· Learning and career development programs and resources (live sessions and on-demand)

· Transit Subsidy (for roles based in Vancouver, BC)

· Hybrid work (for roles that do not require to be onsite)

· 3 weeks' vacation or more depending on the position

· Global and local company organized social events and celebrations to connect with your team




Here's what you can expect as a part of the MineSense Team:




When you join MineSense, a trailblazing Canadian mining technology company, you become a part of our purpose to maximize global metals and minimize global impacts. We’re not just a company; we’re a Community of Innovators who forge a path toward a sustainable future. Here’s why we want you to be excited:




Purpose-Driven: At MineSense, we don’t just clock in—we live our purpose. We’re on a mission to revolutionize the mining industry, and every day, we’re one step closer to a cleaner, greener world.

Values That Resonate: Our core values—Innovation, Belonging, Resilience, and Adding Value—aren’t just buzzwords. They’re the bedrock of our culture. When you step into our team, you’re not just an employee; you’re a steward of these values.

Impactful Work: Imagine contributing to a cleaner, more sustainable planet. At MineSense, you’re shaping the future of cleantech and developing cutting-edge technology that literally hasn't been developed before. Your work matters, and it echoes beyond our walls.

People-Centric: We’re not just about profits; we’re about people. Our organization cares deeply about its employees. When you join us, you become part of a community that supports growth, well-being, and success.




Ready to make your mark? Join MineSense and be part of something bigger than yourself.




MineSense is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing accommodation to individuals with disabilities. Should you require accommodation during the recruitment or hiring process, please feel free to indicate your needs while applying. Information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially.

All applications are welcome, even if you don't meet all the requirements, so please do not hesitate to apply.







We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At CI, we see a great place to work as one that is a safe place for everyone to have a voice, where people are empowered to take ownership over meaningful work, where there is an opportunity to grow through stretching themselves, where they can work on innovative products and projects, and where employees are supported and engaged in doing so.

We are seeking experienced Data Analysts to support a large-scale data migration and integration initiative. You will be responsible for investigating existing data structures, analyzing business logic hidden in queries and reports, mapping source data to target systems, and collaborating with business stakeholders to ensure accuracy and completeness.

This role is highly investigative and requires a blend of technical analysis and business communication skills. You should be comfortable navigating undocumented environments, reverse-engineering logic, and documenting findings clearly for both technical and business audiences.

Key Responsibilities


Source System Analysis
Investigate source systems and databases to understand data structures, relationships, and logic.
Analyze existing SQL queries, reports, and stored procedures to reverse engineer business rules.
Data Mapping & Transformation
Map source fields to target model requirements.
Identify gaps, data quality issues, and transformation needs.
Work closely with data engineers to validate ETL/transformation rules.
Stakeholder Collaboration
Partner with business SMEs and stakeholders to clarify definitions, logic, and requirements.
Translate technical findings into clear documentation for non-technical stakeholders.
Documentation & Knowledge Transfer
Produce detailed data dictionaries, mapping documents, and gap analysis reports.
Capture undocumented business logic and processes uncovered during investigation.
Validation & Testing Support
Support data validation and reconciliation efforts during development and UAT.
Assist in resolving discrepancies between source and target systems.

Required Skills & Experience


Strong SQL skills, with ability to read, analyze, and write complex queries.
Proven experience in data migration, data warehousing, or system integration projects.
Background in analyzing large datasets.
Strong data profiling, mapping, and reconciliation skills.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities in ambiguous environments.
Strong communication skills to work with both technical teams and business stakeholders.
Experience creating data dictionaries, mapping documents, and gap analysis reports.
Experience with dashboard tools such as Sigma or equivalent
Experience with code repository systems – Bitbucket or Git


Nice to Have


Experience with Snowflake or other modern cloud data platforms.
Familiarity with ETL/ELT processes and tools.
Exposure to financial services or other regulated industries


This opportunity is for an existing vacancy with the company. The anticipated base salary range for this position is $55,000 - $95,000. Exact salary depends on several factors such as experience, skills, education, and budget. Salary range may vary based on geographic location. In addition to base salary, this position is eligible for participation in a bonus program. In addition, The Company offers a variety of benefits to eligible employees, including health insurance coverage, wellness programs, life and disability insurance, retirement savings plans, paid leave programs, education-related programs, paid holidays and vacation time, and many others. Many of these benefits are subsidized or fully paid for by the company.

This role follows our in‑office work model and requires employees to be onsite four days per week to support collaboration and business needs.

CI Financial is an independent company offering global wealth management and asset management advisory services through diverse financial services firms. Since 1965, we have consistently anticipated and responded to the changing needs of investors. We are driven by a commitment to provide individuals and institutions with the highest-quality investments and advice. Our commitment to the highest levels of performance means that whatever their position, CI employees must be comfortable in a fast-paced environment that will stretch them to tap into their highest potential. Employees with a healthy dose of ambition, a desire to commit to a curious mindset for continuous learning, and a willingness to go the extra mile thrive at CI.

A Supportive Environment for Success

We offer an in-office environment, competitive benefits, and a supportive workplace to help our employees thrive both personally and professionally.

What We Offer


Modern HQ location within walking distance from Union Station
Training Reimbursement
Paid Professional Designations
Employee Savings Plan (ESP)
Corporate Discount Program
Enhanced group benefits
Parental Leave Top–up program
Paid time off for Volunteering


We are focused on building a diverse and inclusive workforce. If you are excited about this role and are not confident you meet all the qualification requirements, we encourage you to apply to investigate the opportunity further.

Please submit your resume in confidence by clicking “Apply”. Only qualified candidates selected for an interview will be contacted. If you require accommodations in order to apply for any job opportunities, require this posting in an additional format, or require accommodation at any stage of the recruitment process please contact us at accessible.recruitment@ci.com, or call 416-364-1145 ext. 4747.

Title: People Data Analyst
Description:
Slalom is a fiercely human business and technology consulting company that leads with outcomes to bring more value, in all ways, always. From strategy through delivery, our agile teams across 52 offices in 12 countries partner with clients to co-create powerful customer experiences, modern ways of working, and meaningful impact.




What sets us apart? We believe work should be challenging and fulfilling, not perfect, but possible. That’s why we prioritize purpose, flexibility, connection, and recognition, so our people can thrive and love what they do, most days.


Job Title:




People Data Analyst


So, what will I do?
Serve as the primary analytics partner for Talent Acquisition while contributing to Slalom's broader People Analytics strategy through direct collaboration with the Global People Team and People Health product organization.
Partner with Talent Acquisition leaders, People Business Partners, Talent and People Experience, Total Rewards, Staffing, Workforce Planning, HR Product teams, and business stakeholders to define reporting requirements, establish key performance indicators, and deliver actionable insights that support strategic workforce decisions.
Support the People Health product hub by translating Talent Acquisition and broader people data into integrated views that enable leaders to understand workforce health, talent effectiveness, organizational trends, and employee outcomes across the talent lifecycle.
Develop executive-level dashboards and reporting solutions that combine recruiting, workforce, and people data to provide a comprehensive view of organizational performance and talent health.
Analyze recruiting funnel performance, sourcing effectiveness, workforce trends, talent movement, retention indicators, and people metrics to identify opportunities for optimization and continuous improvement.
Lead data domain integration efforts by partnering across People systems, data platforms, and business functions to improve data consistency, reporting alignment, and enterprise-wide visibility into people data.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate, transform, and model data from Avature, HR systems, workforce planning tools, data warehouses, and business intelligence platforms to support scalable analytics solutions.
Establish and promote reporting governance, data quality standards, and analytics best practices to improve trust, consistency, and usability of people data across the organization.
Translate complex datasets into compelling visualizations, narratives, and recommendations that enable leaders to make informed, data-driven decisions regarding talent strategy, workforce planning, and organizational effectiveness.

And, what will I bring?
Skills
5+ years of experience in People Analytics, Talent Acquisition Analytics, Workforce Analytics, Business Intelligence, or a related analytical discipline.
Advanced experience building reporting and dashboard solutions within Avature and leveraging business intelligence tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms.
Experience integrating and analyzing data across multiple HR, recruiting, workforce planning, and business systems to deliver enterprise-level insights.
Strong understanding of recruiting, talent, workforce, and people metrics, including the ability to connect data across multiple domains to support strategic decision-making.
Experience supporting data governance, data quality, reporting standardization, and analytics modernization initiatives.

Competencies
Strategic thinker who can connect Talent Acquisition metrics to broader workforce and organizational outcomes.
Strong relationship builder who effectively partners with stakeholders across Talent Acquisition, People Business Partners, Talent and People Experience, Total Rewards, Staffing, Workforce Planning, HR Product, and business leadership teams.
Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to identify patterns, opportunities, and risks within complex datasets.
Effective communicator and storyteller who can translate technical findings into actionable business recommendations.
Continuous improvement mindset with a passion for leveraging data to improve workforce experiences, talent outcomes, and organizational effectiveness.


Hybrid/In Office

We are hybrid, but there are expectations that if your team leader or business requirements require you onsite, you are able to meet those expectations and requirements.




Slalom is committed to fair and equitable compensation practices. For this position, the expected base salary pay range is $110,000 to $140,000. In addition, there is potential for a discretionary bonus based on individual and company performance. Actual compensation will depend upon an individual's skills, experience, qualifications, and other relevant factors.


Slalom Employee Perks
Culture & fun!
Lunch & learns
Health & wellness activities
Holiday parties
Employee community groups
Social clubs & activities
Vacation (20 days prorated based on start date & wellness/sick days)

Tangible Perks
Competitive compensation package
$100 cell phone monthly reimbursement
Registered Retirement Program (RRSP) & Employer Matching
Tax-Free Savings Account (TFSA)
Annual Thrive Well-Being Gift Card
Family Planning/Fertility Benefit
Maternity/Parental Leave Top-Up
Medical & Dental Benefits
Life and AD&D Insurance
Access to Supplementary Life and AD&D Insurance
Access to the Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and Inkblot Therapy
And more!



All benefits are subject to eligibility requirements.

Slalom is an inclusive, equal opportunity employer dedicated to building a diverse workforce. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates and will work to reasonably accommodate applicants' needs throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process. Please advise the Talent Acquisition team if you require accommodations during the interview process.

Please note that if you are hired at Slalom, you will be required to complete a background check.

  • Continuous improvement mindset with a passion for leveraging data to improve workforce experiences, talent outcomes, and organizational effectiveness.

Hybrid/In Office

We are hybrid, but there are expectations that if your team leader or business requirements require you onsite, you are able to meet those expectations and requirements.


Slalom is committed to fair and equitable compensation practices. For this position, the expected base salary pay range is $110,000 to $140,000. In addition, there is potential for a discretionary bonus based on individual and company performance. Actual compensation will depend upon an individual's skills, experience, qualifications, and other relevant factors.

Slalom Employee Perks
  • Culture & fun!
  • Lunch & learns
  • Health & wellness activities
  • Holiday parties
  • Employee community groups
  • Social clubs & activities
  • Vacation (20 days prorated based on start date & wellness/sick days)
Tangible Perks
  • Competitive compensation package
  • $100 cell phone monthly reimbursement
  • Registered Retirement Program (RRSP) & Employer Matching
  • Tax-Free Savings Account (TFSA)
  • Annual Thrive Well-Being Gift Card
  • Family Planning/Fertility Benefit
  • Maternity/Parental Leave Top-Up
  • Medical & Dental Benefits
  • Life and AD&D Insurance
  • Access to Supplementary Life and AD&D Insurance
  • Access to the Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and Inkblot Therapy
  • And more!

All benefits are subject to eligibility requirements.

Slalom is an inclusive, equal opportunity employer dedicated to building a diverse workforce. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates and will work to reasonably accommodate applicants' needs throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process. Please advise the Talent Acquisition team if you require accommodations during the interview process.

Title: Data Analyst, HR
Description:
Who We Are




For more than 130 years, The Salvation Army has served people in need in communities across Canada and Bermuda. Building on our roots as a world-wide Christian church, each year we help more than 2 million people, providing necessities such as food, clothing and shelter. In addition, we support people experiencing unemployment, addiction and family challenges. We continually adapt and innovate to meet emerging needs and live out our mission, vision and values of hope, service, dignity and stewardship. As a faith- and values-based organization, we hire and serve people of all backgrounds and walks of life - there is a place for everyone to belong here.




Mission Statement




The Salvation Army exists to share the love of Jesus Christ, meet human needs and be a transforming influence in the communities of our world.




Job Description:




Position Purpose:




The Data Analyst, HR exists to strengthen HR technology, analytics, and data integrity so that HR systems effectively support organizational goals. The role contributes by improving HR processes, ensuring accurate and reliable data through analysis, integration, and migration, and enabling robust reporting and analytics capabilities. By training and upskilling team members in Workday Reporting and partnering with stakeholders to define business needs, the role helps translate data into actionable insights. Ultimately, it empowers HR leaders to make informed, strategic decisions that enhance workforce planning and people outcomes across the organization.




This role is a 12 - month, temporary, fixed-term contract.




Key Accountabilities:




Elicit business, financial, and operational requirements across headquarters, divisions, ministry units, and frontline teams to ensure HR systems meet organizational needs.
Develop HR analytics capability by training team members on Workday reporting and creating high-quality, custom reports that support decision-making.
Assess HR system integrity through configuration reviews, interface evaluations, and data quality checks to improve reliability and consistency.
Collaborate cross‑functionally to define and recommend system enhancements, integrations, workflows, and release impacts that support digital transformation.
Establish reporting governance standards for Workday, including design, access, data integrity, and compliance, to ensure accurate and consistent reporting.
Consult on system releases and projects by providing insight on downstream impacts, risks, and requirements, and by escalating and resolving system issues as needed.
Prepare and execute testing and release management activities, including test plans, source-to-target data mapping, migration audits, and reconciliation reporting.
Oversee HR data assurance activities such as gap analysis, data extraction and conversion, validation controls, and integration mapping to ensure end‑to‑end data integrity.




Education and Experience Qualifications:




A university degree in computer science or an approved equivalent combination of education and experience.
7–10 years of experience leading and supporting data analysis and migration initiatives for cloud-based/SaaS HR system implementations.
3–5 years of hands-on experience in HR Reporting and Analytics, with a strong emphasis on statistical analysis and programming using R and/or Python.
Delivers advanced analytics and reporting solutions through strong expertise in Workday Reporting and Power BI.
Builds team capability by designing and delivering effective training, knowledge transfer, and practical guidance.
Navigates complex, multi-system and multi-entity environments while contributing to high-priority, high-visibility initiatives.
Collaborates effectively within Agile delivery teams on large-scale projects from analysis through implementation.
Utilizes advanced Microsoft Excel skills, with solid proficiency in Word and PowerPoint to support analysis and communication.
Demonstrates strong analytical, problem-solving, communication, adaptability, and continuous learning capabilities in fast-paced, changing environments.







Compensation:




The target hiring range for this position is $85,321.60 to $106,641.60 with a maximum of $127,982.40. Placement in the salary range will be based on factors such as market conditions, internal equity, and candidate experience, skills, and qualifications relevant to the role.




The Salvation Army will provide reasonable accommodation upon request. Please email Recruitment.Accommodations@salvationarmy.ca if you have a need for any accommodation and we will be pleased to discuss this with you.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Position Snapshot

Business area: Purina

Job title: Business Intelligence Analyst

Location: Mississauga ON located at 2500 Royal Windsor Dr, Mississauga ON L5J 1K8

Hybrid

Position Summary

We are seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst to join our dynamic team, reporting to the Senior Category and Shopper Insights Manager. This role is central to data management, with a strong focus on maintaining data integrity and enabling reporting capabilities that support efficient operations and informed business decisions. This position is an existing vacancy.

A day in the life

You will play a key role in automating data workflows, improving reporting processes, and maintaining the tools and structures that support efficient data delivery. You will help design and enhance dashboards, streamline recurring reporting, and ensure business teams have timely access to accurate, well-organized data and insights.

You will also:


Leverage advanced analytics, reporting, and database management tools to automate data preparation, improve reporting efficiency, and develop scalable data solutions.
Build and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations that convert complex data into clear, actionable business insights.
Support with the development of monthly reports that address business issues and opportunities.
Maintain data quality and consistency across reporting platforms by ensuring accuracy, reliability, and standardized formats.
Champion insights literacy by supporting tool training and support cross-functional teams with data management
Manage research budgets, supplier onboarding, and monitor spend against forecasts and POs


What will make you successful?


3+ years of experience in Customer Insights, Category Management, or Data Analytics
Hands-on experience with Python and Power BI is required
Proficiency with Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Word is required
Experience using syndicated data platforms (e.g., NielsenIQ, Numerator) is an asset
Post secondary education in Business Administration, Data & Analytics, Marketing, or related field.
Strong communication skills with the ability effectively convey findings in a clear and persuasive manner
Proven problem-solving skills with the ability to extract insights and formulate recommendations
Proactive self-starter with strong attention to detail, excellent interpersonal, communication, project management, and organizational skills


We have a friendly, supportive team with a coaching and mentoring environment. There are real opportunities for future development and progression – this really could be a move towards the exciting career you’ve always wanted.

At Nestle Canada, we are committed to transparency and fairness in our compensation and job posting practices. This position offers a competitive salary within the range specified above, in compliance with Ontario's pay transparency regulations.

Benefits:


Compensation Range: $81,000 CAD to $85,000 CAD
Comprehensive total rewards benefits package including Health and Dental benefits that start on day one of employment
Company matched pension plan
Three weeks of Vacation and five personal days (Personal Paid Holidays)
Excellent training and development programs, as well as opportunities to grow within the company
Access to Educational Assistance & Tuition Reimbursement
Bonus eligibility
Gender affirmation benefits to ensure access to necessary care, resources, and coverage for gender-affirming procedures
Fertility Services and Surrogacy Medical Coverage to support the pursuit of a path to parenthood regardless of medical conditions, gender or sexual orientation
Adoption benefits to remove some of the financial barriers associated with adoption
Up to 50% off – Nespresso Coffee Machine, Capsules and accessories
Access to the Discount Company store with Nestlé, Nespresso, and Purina products (Located across various Nestle offices/sites)
Bring your dog to work!


What You Need To Know

We will be considering applicants as they apply, so please don’t delay in submitting your application.

Nestlé Canada is an equal-opportunity employer committed to diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility. We welcome qualified applicants to bring their diverse and unique experiences as a result of their education, perspectives, culture, ethnicity, race, sex, gender identity and expression, nation of origin, age, languages spoken, veteran’s status, colour, religion, disability, sexual orientation and beliefs.

If you are selected to participate in the recruitment process, please inform Human Resources of any accommodations you may require. Nestlé will work with you in an effort to ensure that you are able to fully participate in the process.

Title: Revenue Strategy Analyst
Description:
About Us

AfterShip, a Great Place to Work Certified company, is transforming the global eCommerce landscape. Founded in 2012, AfterShip is a post-purchase SaaS company on a mission to build the world's leading automation platform for ecommerce merchants.

AfterShip unifies shipping & labels, order tracking, AI predictive delivery, and returns management into one system—giving merchants a single place to manage and automate everything that happens after checkout. By centralizing these workflows, AfterShip enables merchants to reduce customer support inquiries, deliver a more reliable and engaging customer experiences, and unlock incremental revenue at every post-purchase touchpoint.

AfterShip integrates seamlessly with eCommerce platforms including Shopify and TikTok Shop, and connects with more than 1,200 carriers worldwide. Today, over 20,000 businesses—including Samsung, Gymshark, Mejuri, Dr. Squatch, and Alo Yoga—rely on AfterShip to turn every post-purchase moment into an opportunity to build trust, reduce costs, and drive repeat purchases.

Built for a global market from day one, AfterShip operates with an engineering-driven, internationally distributed team. The company employs more than 450 people across 8 offices, spanning North America, Europe, and Asia, and representing over 20 cities worldwide.

Your Mission:

As a Revenue Strategy Analyst, you will be the analytical engine behind AfterShip's GTM strategy, turning data into the decisions that shape how we grow. Reporting to the Senior Manager, Revenue Operations, you will partner across Sales, Marketing, Partnerships, Customer Success, Finance, and Data to uncover insights, optimize revenue-driving processes, and accelerate company OKRs through rigorous, data-informed thinking.

This role is built for someone who thrives at the intersection of SaaS and eCommerce, leans into ambiguous business problems, and can translate complex datasets into clear narratives and strategic recommendations that leadership can act on. You will identify growth opportunities across GTM channels, sharpen reporting visibility, and drive high-impact operational initiatives that move revenue performance across the business.

This is a highly cross-functional role that rewards strong analytical thinking, commercial acumen, and communication skill. The ideal candidate is comfortable engaging executive stakeholders, navigating incomplete or evolving datasets, and designing AI-enabled workflows that turn analytics into a competitive advantage rather than a reporting function.

What You'll Do:


Analyze GTM channel and funnel performance to surface trends, growth levers, and operational gaps, then translate findings into prioritized recommendations
Partner with RevOps and GTM leadership to operationalize revenue strategies tied to company OKRs
Build executive-grade dashboards and self-serve reporting in Tableau and HubSpot, designed for decision-making
Run ad hoc strategic analyses across Sales, Marketing, CS, Partnerships, and Finance, including win/loss, churn, attribution, and segment profitability
Improve forecasting accuracy and pipeline predictability through analysis of conversion behavior, stage velocity, and deal slippage
Monitor core revenue metrics (pipeline coverage, conversion, velocity, NRR, GRR, CAC payback, quota attainment) and flag anomalies before they surface in QBRs
Translate complex datasets into clear executive narratives, owning the "so what" and the recommended action
Design scalable analytical frameworks and reporting processes that reduce one-off requests through better self-serve infrastructure
Build AI-enabled workflows to automate recurring analysis and accelerate insight delivery, treating AI as a system to design with
Investigate ambiguous datasets independently, scoping the question and shipping the answer without tight direction
Govern reporting consistency and data integrity across HubSpot, Tableau, and the data warehouse, ensuring shared metric definitions across teams


Who We're Looking For:


2 to 4 years of experience in Revenue Operations, Revenue Strategy, GTM Analytics, or related analytical roles, ideally within SaaS, eCommerce, or platform businesses
eCommerce business model experience strongly preferred (Shopify ecosystem, marketplace dynamics, or subscription commerce)
Required tooling: HubSpot (CRM, custom objects, workflows, reporting), Tableau (or equivalent BI), SQL
Preferred tooling: workflow automation platforms (n8n, Zapier, Workato, or Make), Python for data analysis, exposure to a cloud data warehouse (BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift)
Demonstrated experience designing AI-enabled workflows that solve operational problems (not just using AI for personal productivity), including familiarity with prompt design, agentic workflows, or MCP-based integrations
Hands-on experience analyzing GTM performance across the full funnel: pipeline coverage, conversion rates, sales cycle velocity, forecast accuracy, win/loss, NRR, CAC payback, and segment-level performance
Proven ability to build dashboards, run ad hoc analyses, and translate findings into clear recommendations for Sales, Marketing, Partnerships, CS, and Finance stakeholders
Strong storytelling and executive communication, including the ability to present trade-offs, not just findings, to leadership
Comfort navigating ambiguity, scoping problems independently, and shipping work without heavy hand-holding
Bias toward systems thinking: when you see a recurring manual task, your instinct is to redesign the process, not just complete it faster
Track record of cross-functional collaboration across Sales, Marketing, Partnerships, Customer Success, Finance, and Data


At AfterShip, we know great talent doesn't always fit every requirement. If you're passionate about our mission and believe you can make an impact, we encourage you to apply.

Why You Should Join Us:


Great Place to Work Certified: We've been recognized for our inclusive, values-driven culture that celebrates diversity and collaboration.
Innovative & Inclusive Culture: Started by our software engineer-turned-CEO, AfterShip is built on curiosity, creativity, and collaboration. We're a passionate, global team of problem solvers who put egos aside to innovate together. We take immense pride in fostering a culture that's inclusive, which has allowed us to surround ourselves with the industry's most talented professionals.
Ambitious Mission with Real Impact: Join us in transforming eCommerce by making buying and selling easier for everyone. It's one of the most dynamic spaces in tech, with limitless opportunities to innovate and grow.
Thrive & Grow: There's no ceiling to what you can achieve or learn here. We're committed to empowering your career while advancing together as a company.
Flexible Work Setup: We're a remote-first team, meaning by default that employees work from home or on a hybrid-flexible basis in our hub locations (i.e., Toronto, Austin, Barcelona). You're empowered to choose a work setup that works best for you and your team. With flexible hours depending on your time zone, you'll be able to have a schedule that fits your working style and the requirements of your role.


Perks:


Competitive compensation
Remote-first/hybrid-flexible work setups
Healthcare coverage offered from day 1
Retirement plans including company match
Unlimited PTO
Annual learning & wellness benefit
Monthly book perk
Career progression & professional development
In-office lunch and commuter benefits for those located in our hub locations


Salary range for this role: CAD$77,000 - $91,000

We are an equal opportunity employer and provide accommodations upon request throughout the recruitment process, in accordance with local legislation. Please let us know if you require any support, and we'll work with you to meet your needs.

We believe in hiring right over hiring fast. While timelines may vary, we're looking to fill this role as soon as possible.

Our hiring process uses AI to help with initial resume screening and to support interview note-taking. These tools help our team stay organized and fair, but all hiring decisions are made by people.

Title: Data Analyst I
Description:
SteerBridge Strategies is a modern technology company delivering innovative, mission‑focused solutions to the U.S. Government and private sector. Leveraging deep expertise in federal acquisition, digital transformation, and emerging technologies, we deliver agile, commercial‑grade capabilities that accelerate operational effectiveness and drive measurable mission success.

At the core of SteerBridge is our people—especially the veterans whose leadership, problem‑solving mindset, and commitment to excellence elevate every project we support. We don’t simply hire exceptional talent; we cultivate it, creating meaningful career pathways for veterans, military spouses, and professionals who share our passion for advancing technology and strengthening the missions we serve.




Position Overview

SteerBridge Strategies is seeking a highly skilled Data Analyst to support mission-critical operations in a secure, controlled data environment. This role focuses on advanced data analysis, automation, and reporting to drive informed decision-making at leadership levels. The ideal candidate will have strong SQL expertise, experience working with large datasets, and the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights.

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Insights
Analyze large, relational datasets to identify trends, inefficiencies, and performance gaps
Translate raw data into actionable insights to support leadership decision-making
Identify degraders, shortages, and operational inefficiencies




SQL Development
Write clean, efficient, and production-ready SQL (Amazon Redshift, Oracle SQL Developer)
Utilize advanced SQL techniques (CTEs, window functions, complex joins, aggregations)
Optimize queries for performance and scalability
Troubleshoot and resolve data discrepancies across multiple data sources




Data Quality & Validation
Implement automated validation checks (row counts, duplicates, completeness, integrity)
Ensure accuracy and consistency of data before reporting
Identify and resolve root causes of data inconsistencies




Automation & ETL Processes
Support and maintain ETL pipelines and data workflows
Build automated processes for data ingestion, transformation, and validation
Implement scheduling/orchestration tools (cron, AWS services, Airflow, or equivalent)
Ensure processes run without manual intervention




Scripting & Process Automation
Develop scripts using Python, Bash, or PowerShell
Automate data pulls, file processing, and pipeline validation
Integrate scripts into end-to-end workflows




Reporting & Dashboards
Develop and maintain automated reports and dashboards
Support daily, weekly, and monthly reporting requirements
Ensure reports are accurate, consistent, and delivered on schedule




Required Qualifications

Education & Experience

U.S. Citizenship required with the ability to obtain a Secret Clearance
Bachelor’s degree in a technical field (Data Analytics, Information Technology, Computer Science, or related)
Minimum 3+ years of professional experience in data analysis or a related field
Strong experience working in sensitive or controlled data environments
Advanced SQL proficiency (Amazon Redshift, Oracle preferred)
Experience with large, relational datasets and data structures
Strong understanding of joins, aggregations, and query optimization
Experience troubleshooting data discrepancies and ensuring data integrity
Familiarity with ETL processes and data pipelines
Experience building recurring reports and dashboards
Strong attention to detail and data validation skills




Interpersonal & Professional Skills

Strong communication skills with the ability to translate technical concepts for non-technical and operational stakeholders.
Ability to develop relationships with collaborators, program providers, community partners, and military personnel.
Able to successfully prioritize and manage multiple critical projects simultaneously with a high degree of accuracy.
Experience working on applied data projects involving diverse organizations to collect, analyze, and interpret data.




Preferred Qualifications

3–6 years of post-college professional experience
Experience with predictive analytics or modeling
Proficiency in Python or R for data analysis
Experience with automation tools and scheduling frameworks
Experience supporting executive-level decision-making




Core Competencies

Analytical Thinking

Collaboration & Teamwork

Attention to Data Quality

Problem Solving

Ownership & Accountability

Continuous Learning

Health insurance
Dental insurance
Vision insurance
Life Insurance
401(k) Retirement Plan with matching
Paid Time Off
Paid Federal Holidays




$81,000 - $95,000 a year

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Coframe

"You guys are insane." -- OpenAI Team

"Coframe makes you feel like you have a team of 100 people." -- Replit Team

At Coframe, we are building the world's first AI Growth Engineer. We envision a future where user interfaces aren't static code, but living organisms that adapt, evolve, and personalize themselves in real time. We’re looking for analysts who have worked on growth, experimentation, or conversion optimization teams. Experience working as a contractor or consultant across multiple clients is a major plus. You’ll work directly with customers and provide ad-hoc reporting, analysis, and insights for both internal teams and customers.

Why Coframe:


Elite Team: Our founder previously co-founded a unicorn with Tom Brady and guest lectures AI at Stanford. Our researchers created some of the world's most popular open-source code generation projects.
Already Crushing It: Growing at 120% QoQ, our AI Agents autonomously design, code, and deploy A/B tests, Landing Pages, and Personalization experiences, 10x faster than traditional methods. We've driven $200M+ in revenue for customers like Dropbox, Replit, and Intuit in the last 6 months.
Top VCs: Backed by Khosla Ventures, Nat Friedman (former GitHub CEO), and Rich Miner (Android co-founder). Join before our Series A.
OpenAI: The only martech company officially partnered with OpenAI, co-training a model specifically for our use case.


What You'll Do:


Respond directly to customer data and analytics requests
Build ad-hoc reports and answer business questions quickly
Align metrics across multiple analytics systems
Investigate and resolve data discrepancies
Build tools and automate recurring requests
Help turn one-off requests into internal products and processes


You May Be a Fit If:


Strong skills building queries, reports, and dashboards in complex data environments
Clear communication with non-technical teams internally and on the client side
Clear communication with data and technical teams on both sides
Ability to move fast while staying careful and accurate in conclusions
Strong adoption of AI tools, with good judgment about their strengths and limits
Good prioritization skills across many incoming requests


Bonus Points:


Experience with Optimizely, VWO, LaunchDarkly, or similar experimentation platforms.
Experienced with GA4, Segment, Amplitude, or Heap and understand how data flows from browser event to dashboard.
Familiarity with Google Tag Manager or Tealium.
Exposure to Shopify or SaaS product environments.
You manage competing priorities across multiple client engagements without losing quality.
Excited about AI-powered experimentation and autonomous agents.


Compensation

Base: $100,000 - $170,000

Equity: Meaningful early-stage equity.

Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors including experience and expertise.

Benefits:


Flexible (Unlimited) Paid Time Off
Medical, Dental, and Vision for you and your family
401k with match
Daily in-office meals
Wellness benefit and gym membership


Compensation Range: $100K - $170K
