Title: Data Analyst - Tempo determinato
Description:
Lavora con noi in Fiera Milano ✨

Il cuore pulsante degli eventi, delle manifestazioni fieristiche e dei congressi in Italia e nel mondo.

Con spazi innovativi, servizi all’avanguardia e una visione proiettata al futuro, siamo il partner strategico per le aziende che vogliono crescere, connettersi ed espandersi oltre i propri confini.

La nostra cultura aziendale mette le persone al centro: valorizziamo il talento, investiamo nello sviluppo continuo e creiamo un ambiente dinamico e inclusivo, in cui ogni idea può fare la differenza.




Data Analyst – Entra a far parte del nostro team!

Unisciti al team Data & Artificial Intelligence Office e vivi da protagonista l’energia di un contesto in continua evoluzione. Ti occuperai dell’analisi dei dati a supporto di eventi, fiere e iniziative commerciali, contribuendo a trasformare le informazioni in insight strategici per le decisioni aziendali. Avrai l’opportunità di lavorare su dataset reali e progetti ad alto impatto.

L’inserimento è previsto con contratto a tempo determinato di 12 mesi.




🎯 Le tue sfide principali:

Analizzare, strutturare e validare dataset a supporto di eventi, manifestazioni fieristiche e iniziative di business
Realizzare attività di reporting, dashboarding e analisi dati a supporto delle decisioni di business
Collaborare con stakeholder interni (Business Unit, Marketing e altre funzioni) per la comprensione dei fabbisogni analitici
Supportare la produzione di insight utili al monitoraggio delle performance e all’interpretazione dei dati




🙋 Chi stiamo cercando:

Laurea in discipline STEM, Economia o titoli affini
Conoscenza di base di:

-Analisi dati tramite Excel / strumenti XLS

-SQL e principi di modellazione dati

Esperienza di 3 anni in ruoli di data analysis
Attitudine analitica, precisione e capacità di lavorare per priorità
Capacità di lavorare in team e di interfacciarsi con interlocutori business




💼 Cosa troverai in Fiera Milano:

Formazione continua – catalogo formativo open source
Welfare & Benefit – flexible benefit, benessere a 360° e convenzioni aziendali
Bistrot aziendale e buoni pasto – punti di ristoro aziendali ed erogazione di buoni pasto durante il lavoro agile
Premio di risultato – riconoscimento di un contributo per i risultati di squadra
Mobilità interna – opportunità di sviluppo professionale
Work-life balance – flessibilità oraria & smart working
Onboarding dedicato – momento di benvenuto, seguito da affiancamento e ascolto durante il tuo percorso professionale




🤝 Perché unirti a noi?

Qui il tuo talento conta davvero: potrai sperimentare, innovare e contribuire a eventi e progetti di rilievo.




✅ Il nostro impegno per la diversità e l’inclusione

In Fiera Milano crediamo nelle pari opportunità: il nostro processo di selezione è aperto a chiunque, senza distinzioni. Valorizziamo la diversità ed evitiamo ogni forma di discriminazione, diretta o indiretta, riguardo a sesso, genere, età, origine etnica, orientamento sessuale, convinzioni e condizioni personali, politiche, sindacali e religiose, nel pieno rispetto della normativa vigente e del nostro Codice Etico.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Experian is a global data and technology company, powering opportunities for people and businesses around the world. We help to redefine lending practices, uncover and prevent fraud, simplify healthcare, create marketing solutions, and gain deeper insights into the automotive market, all using our unique combination of data, analytics and software. We also assist millions of people to accomplish their financial goals and help them save time and money.

We invest in people and new advanced technologies to unlock the power of data. As a FTSE 100 Index company listed on the London Stock Exchange (EXPN), we have a team of 22,500 people across 32 countries. Our corporate headquarters are in Dublin, Ireland.

Job Description

The Data Analyst delivers high quality analytical insights, data-driven decision support, and attribute development for Experian clients. You will represent Experian's advanced analytics capabilities during business development discussions and contribute in shaping analytical products built on bureau and alternative data.

You will report into the Director of Data Science and Gen AI.

What You'll Do


Participate in attribute design sessions, contributing analytical perspectives that improve predictive quality and business relevance.
Conduct exploratory data analysis (EDA) to evaluate data integrity, identify patterns, and extract behaviors that inform attribute creation.
Build analytical dashboards and insights reports using BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI to support other teams and clients.
Assess the quality and structure of source data; validate datasets for completeness and consistency.
Perform cross country data mapping and harmonization to support global products.
Develop scalable scripts in Python or Spark for data preparation, feature generation, and analytics workflows (engineering-heavy pipelines not required).
Maintain clear documentation, analytical specifications, and repository artifacts to support transparency and reproducibility.
Translate complex business questions into data-driven analytical solutions.
Support development of predictive models (regression and machine learning) and provide insights that guide model enhancements and feature selection.


Qualifications

What you'll bring:


3+ years of experience in a similar role.
Proficiency in Python for data analysis; familiarity with R or SAS.
Experience with Tableau or Power BI.
Expertise in exploratory data analysis, feature/attribute generation.
Understanding of consumer credit data, financial services data, or similar structured datasets.
Organizational, interpersonal, and presentation capabilities.


Additional Information

You will get:


Personal Development - career pathway for professional growth supported by learning and development programs and unlimited access to online educational training courses, learning materials and books.
Work environment - excellent work conditions with friendly environment, recognized team spirit, and fun and quality recreation time.
Social benefit package including life insurance, food vouchers, additional health insurance, monthly flex allowance and internet coverage, corporate discounts, marriage and childbirth / adoption allowance, Multisport card, Sharesave plan, Employee assistance program, а birthday gift and many other benefits!
Work-life balance - 25 days paid vacation, 1 additional day off for your birthday and extra 3 paid days for participation in Social responsibility event.
Opportunity for Flexible working hours and Home Office.


Experian is an Equal opportunity employer. Everyone can succeed at Experian and bring their whole self to work, irrespective of their gender, ethnicity, religion, colour, sexuality, physical ability or age. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know at the earliest opportunity.

This is a hybrid remote/in-office role.

Experian Careers - Creating a better tomorrow together

Title: Business & Data Analyst
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
¿Qué estamos buscando?




Actualmente nos encontramos en búsqueda de nuestro/nuestra futuro/futura Business Consultant & Data Analyst cuya misión es diseñar, analizar, controlar y optimizar los procesos operativos de las áreas bajo su responsabilidad dentro de la Dirección de Operaciones, aportando inteligencia de negocio y visión analítica para garantizar la eficiencia, la calidad del servicio y la escalabilidad de los procesos.




La persona seleccionada será responsable del análisis de datos operativos, el seguimiento de KPIs, cuadros de mando y SLA’s, con el objetivo de detectar desviaciones, anticipar tendencias y proponer mejoras continuas que contribuyan al cumplimiento de los objetivos operativos y presupuestarios de la compañía.




Asimismo, velará para que los procesos operativos sean robustos, eficientes, alineados con la normativa vigente y con los estándares de calidad establecidos, asegurando una correcta ejecución tanto por equipos internos como externos.







Hablemos del Proyecto...




Como Business Consultant & Data Analyst tu misión será:




Analizar la viabilidad de nuevas tareas o procesos dentro de la Unidad de Operaciones, elaborando informes en los que se recojan las necesidades, el impacto y el coste/esfuerzo asociado.
Analizar el comportamiento de los distintos servicios mediante datos, cuadros de mando e indicadores operativos para identificar oportunidades de mejora y anticipar posibles desviaciones.
Diseñar y documentar los procesos operativos, definiendo los flujos detallados y asegurando el cumplimiento normativo con el menor coste posible para la compañía.
Asegurar la correcta ejecución de los procesos por parte de personal interno y/o externo, garantizando la alineación con el modelo operativo definido.
Realizar el seguimiento de los KPIs operativos y promover acciones correctoras en caso de detectar desviaciones o ineficiencias.
Supervisar las auditorías de calidad y el cumplimiento de los niveles de servicio y estándares establecidos.
Mantener una interlocución continua con las áreas de negocio para la revisión de necesidades, definición de planes de acción y resolución de incidencias operativas.







¿Qué valoramos de tu candidatura?




Posees Licenciatura o Grado en Administración y Dirección de Empresas, Economía, Ingeniería, Matemáticas, Estadística o similar. Se valorará formación complementaria en análisis de datos, gestión de procesos, mejora continua (Lean, Six Sigma) u otras metodologías afines.
Tienes experiencia mínima de 3 años en posiciones similares en áreas de Operaciones, Procesos, Control de Gestión o Analítica. Experiencia demostrable en diseño, definición y optimización de procesos operativos.
Posees experiencia en el seguimiento de KPIs, elaboración de cuadros de mando y control de SLA’s. Valorable experiencia en entornos financieros, financieros no bancarios o de servicios.







¿Qué te ofrecemos?




Serás parte de un equipo de profesionales de primer nivel, con una excelente reputación en el sector.
Podrás disfrutar de una amplia oferta formativa.
Aprenderás de la mano de Managers de referencia de las distintas direcciones.
Ampliarás tu red de contactos a través de la colaboración y espacios de networking.
Contarás con opciones de desarrollo de carrera y movilidad dentro del Grupo. Somos un grupo bancario que sienta sus bases en los principios de meritocracia y el desarrollo de sus colaboradores.
Contratación indefinida y remuneración competitiva.







Banco Sabadell está comprometido en promover ambientes de trabajo en los que se trate con respeto y dignidad a las personas, procurando el desarrollo profesional de la plantilla y garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades en su selección, formación y promoción ofreciendo un entorno de trabajo libre de cualquier discriminación por motivo de género, edad, orientación sexual, religión, etnia o cualquier otra circunstancia personal o social.




Banco Sabadell forma parte de la red de empresas con el distintivo de “Igualdad en la Empresa” otorgado por el Ministerio de Igualdad.

  • Contarás con opciones de desarrollo de carrera y movilidad dentro del Grupo. Somos un grupo bancario que sienta sus bases en los principios de meritocracia y el desarrollo de sus colaboradores.
  • Contratación indefinida y remuneración competitiva.



Banco Sabadell está comprometido en promover ambientes de trabajo en los que se trate con respeto y dignidad a las personas, procurando el desarrollo profesional de la plantilla y garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades en su selección, formación y promoción ofreciendo un entorno de trabajo libre de cualquier discriminación por motivo de género, edad, orientación sexual, religión, etnia o cualquier otra circunstancia personal o social.


Banco Sabadell forma parte de la red de empresas con el distintivo de “Igualdad en la Empresa” otorgado por el Ministerio de Igualdad.

Title: Data Analyst Manager
Description:
Job Description



WHAT YOU’LL DO​

As a Data Analytics Manager you will lead and develop a team of Data Analysts within the In Season Optimization area, while driving both business delivery and transformation initiatives. This role combines strategic and operational responsibility with people leadership, where your focus will be to enable business impact through data.



The In-Season Optimization mission is to secure capabilities, processes, and insights to get the right product to the right place at the right time, supporting product allocation, availability, and pricing decisions across channels.



You will:



Lead, coach, and develop a team of Data Analysts to strengthen their capabilities and support their career growth
Drive the RUN and TRANSFORMATION agenda, ensuring strong delivery aligned with business goals
Identify and lead strategic areas of analysis to uncover opportunities and improve performance
Work hands-on with data to generate insights using modern analytics and business intelligence tools
Enable data-driven decision making through testing, quantitative analysis, and close collaboration with stakeholders
Oversee projects and initiatives, ensuring effective planning, prioritization, and delivery

​



WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH​



You will be part of the In Season Optimization area and collaborate closely with cross-functional stakeholders across product, tech, and business teams. Together with your team, you will play a key role in shaping how data supports business priorities and decision-making.

​



WHO YOU ARE​



We are looking for people with…​



​5–10 years of experience in people management within Data Analytics or similar fields
A university degree in Data Science, Engineering, or a related area, or equivalent experience
Strong experience within Supply Chain and understanding of end-to-end product flow
Solid experience in project and product management, combining strategic thinking with hands-on execution
Strong analytical and technical skills, with the ability to translate data into clear insights and actions​

And people who are…​



Strong leaders who inspire, develop, and support their teams
Collaborative and communicative, with the ability to work effectively across stakeholders
Proactive and opportunity-driven, identifying improvements through data
Structured and analytical, with a strong data-driven mindset



WHO WE ARE​



H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​



​



We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​



​



WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE ​

At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​



We offer all our employee’s attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. All our employees receive a 25% staff discount usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online. In addition to our staff discount, all our employees are included in our H&M Incentive Program – HIP. You can read more about our H&M Incentive Program here.
​30 days holiday
We offer a collective agreement which includes pension and life insurance
H&M Incentive Program
Wellness benefit 4000kr/year
Benify Benefits Portal
Community with activities





JOIN US​



Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.​



​



Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.​



​



​



*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.​

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At darwind, we are looking for a Data Consultant who enjoys bringing clarity to complexity and turning raw information into meaningful insights. You help organisations build strong data foundations, enabling better decision‑making and long‑term value creation. You will work in an environment that values autonomy, responsibility and professional growth.




What you will do

In this role, you support end‑to‑end data initiatives across business and technical teams. You will:

Collect, clean and transform data from various sources.
Build dashboards and visualisations using Power BI, Tableau or Excel.
Write and optimise SQL queries for reporting and analysis.
Support or develop data pipelines (ETL/ELT) and contribute to cloud‑based data environments.
Model datasets and design efficient, scalable data structures.
Identify patterns, trends and insights that support business decisions.
Collaborate with analysts, IT teams and business stakeholders.
Improve data quality, documentation and data governance practices.
Present findings clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.




Who you are

You bring structure, analytical thinking and clear communication. You also have:

A Master’s degree.
At least three years of experience in data management or a similar field.
Strong knowledge of SQL and at least one analytics or visualisation tool (Power BI, Tableau or Excel).
Understanding of data modelling, data structures and relational databases.
The ability to translate complex data into clear business insights.
A proactive, accurate and curious mindset.
Experience in cloud environments is considered an advantage.
Fluency in Dutch, with proficiency in French as a plus.




What we offer

We provide an environment where impact, ownership and development are central.

Competitive benefits package

Hospitalization insurance
Group insurance
Meal allowance
Your choice between a company car with EU fuel card or the federal mobility budget




Flexibility and work‑life balance

Hybrid work possibilities
Flexible working hours
Projects aligned with your skills and ambitions




Learning and growth

Continuous coaching
Technical and soft‑skills training
Opportunities to grow across different domains and technologies




Culture

A human, transparent and collaborative environment
Real ownership and influence on the projects you contribute to
A team that values initiative, authenticity and open communication




Ready for your next step?

If you want to work on projects where people, impact and growth truly matter, we would be happy to meet you.

Title: Data Analyst - Porto
Description:
Quadrante is looking to strengthen its team by hiring a Data Analyst for our Porto office.




As a leading engineering and consultancy group with a strong international footprint, Quadrante delivers high-value and innovative solutions across multiple markets. With more than 1.400 professionals worldwide, we are driven by excellence, collaboration, and a strong commitment to the highest professional and ethical standards.




In this role, you will contribute to transforming data into meaningful insights, supporting decision-making processes, and enhancing business performance across the organization.







Responsibilities:

Collect, process, and analyze data across different areas of the Group.
Assess data quality and ensure accuracy, consistency, and reliability of information.
Develop reports and dashboards to support business decision-making.
Continuously improve analytical models and data analysis processes.
Provide data-driven support to different business units, solving problems through data modeling and analytical techniques.




Profile:

Degree in Information Management, Information Systems, Computer Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field.
Up to 3 years of experience in similar roles.
Strong experience in SQL, Python, or equivalent programming languages (mandatory).
Experience with Business Intelligence tools (mandatory).
Advanced knowledge of English (mandatory).
Knowledge of Spanish will be valued.
Strong analytical thinking, communication skills, and team spirit.




What we offer

As part of the Quadrante Group, you will join an ambitious and collaborative team within a growing international organization.

We work every day to deliver high-quality solutions and foster the growth of our people through engaging challenges and diverse projects.

A dynamic and inclusive work environment, where knowledge sharing and teamwork are key.
The opportunity to work with diverse datasets and support multiple business areas.
A role with real impact, contributing directly to strategic decision-making.
A culture that values potential, continuous learning, and professional development.




Join the Quadrante Team!

Title: Sales Data Analyst
Description:
¿Por qué te estamos buscando?

Tu misión será garantizar la solidez, calidad y coherencia del modelo de datos comercial de los canales de Alimentación y Hostelería, asegurando una base única y fiable para la analítica, los modelos predictivos y la toma de decisiones comerciales basadas en datos de los que es responsable el departamento de Sales, Data & CRM HUB. Serás responsable de la gestión estructural del dato (modelo alimentación, censo hostelería, fuentes externas alimentación,…), así como de la analítica descriptiva avanzada y soporte a los proyectos de Sales AI departamentales, actuando como referente funcional del dato dentro del HUB.

Tu día a día en Mahou San Miguel


Desarrollo de analítica descriptiva avanzada: Realizar análisis para identificar palancas comerciales y oportunidades de mejora, sirviendo como base para la priorización de iniciativas analíticas y modelos predictivos.
Explotación y visualización del dato: Colaborar en la construcción de dashboards y entornos analíticos (CRM Analytics, Tableau, Palantir, etc.) y traducir los datos en insights accionables para negocio.
Definición de KPIs y métricas comerciales: Apoyar en la definición de indicadores de seguimiento que permitan medir el desempeño y orientar la toma de decisiones.
Gestión y gobierno del censo de Hostelería: Garantizar la calidad, consistencia y actualización del censo, velando por su correcta integración y alineación entre sistemas.
Liderazgo del modelo de datos de Alimentación: Gestionar el modelo de datos del canal y supervisar la integración de fuentes clave (sell-out clientes, Nielsen, Audit PDV, Focus y otras), asegurando su coherencia y fiabilidad.
Enriquecimiento y evolución del dato: Impulsar la incorporación de nuevas fuentes de información que potencien la analítica avanzada y el desarrollo de modelos predictivos y generativos.
Definición de reglas y calidad del dato: Establecer reglas de negocio, validaciones funcionales y controles de calidad, garantizando una base de datos robusta y confiable.
Soporte a proyectos de IA y analítica avanzada e interlocución transversal: Preparar y validar datasets, asegurar la consistencia de datos en modelos (captación, churn, NBA, CLTV, etc.), participar en la medición de impacto y actuar como nexo entre negocio, Data, IT y proveedores.


¿Qué requisitos debes cumplir?


Grado, Licenciatura, Diplomatura o FPII (Especialidad en Ingeniería, ADE, Economía o Matemáticas).
Nivel fluido de inglés conversacional.
Mínimo de 3-4 años de experiencia en análisis de datos, analítica de negocio o entornos BI.
Experiencia demostrable en la gestión de múltiples fuentes de datos y modelos comerciales.
Dominio de herramientas de visualización de datos (Tableau, Power BI o similares).
Capacidad analítica superior y orientación a resultados.
Se valorará positivamente la experiencia previa en el sector de Gran Consumo o Retail.


La diversidad, clave en nuestra estrategia

Nuestro compromiso con la diversidad, la inclusión y la igualdad de oportunidades está presente desde nuestro nacimiento en 1890. Nuestra visión, en este sentido, es “construir el futuro a través de la suma de personas únicas, impulsando la diversidad de forma activa como fuente de riqueza en la sociedad”. Por eso, esta oferta está dirigida a cualquier persona que reúna los requisitos exigidos para el puesto, independientemente de sus características.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
HTML:
Dale Consulting ricerca una figura di Junior Data Analyst per un importante cliente internazionale in ambito Banking, da inserire in un team strutturato e tecnologicamente avanzato.


| CHI SIAMO

Dale Group, società di Consulenza Informatica specializzata nel settore del Core Banking offre servizi ai principali players internazionali del settore IT.

Società dedita all’eccellenza e all'impegno costante per supportare le sfide dei propri clienti, fornendo loro soluzioni customizzate e contribuendo alla realizzazione e gestione di prodotti, servizi e strumenti di alto valore strategico.


| DI COSA TI OCCUPERAI

Entrerai in un contesto enterprise ad alta complessità con focus su analisi dati, supporto funzionale e collaborazione con i team di sviluppo per la corretta gestione dei flussi informativi e delle integrazioni applicative.


La figura affiancherà profili senior e team tecnici nelle attività di analisi, verifica e documentazione dei dati, contribuendo alla qualità e alla coerenza delle informazioni trattate dai servizi applicativi.


In particolare ti occuperai di:

  • Supportare l’analisi dei requisiti funzionali e dei flussi dati relativi a microservizi e API;
  • Collaborare alla definizione e verifica di data mapping e regole di valorizzazione dei campi;
  • Eseguire controlli sui dati, query di verifica e analisi di coerenza su basi dati relazionali;
  • Supportare le attività di test funzionale e validazione dei flussi applicativi;
  • Collaborare con team backend e frontend per identificare anomalie, incongruenze o dati mancanti;
  • Contribuire alla produzione e manutenzione della documentazione funzionale e tecnico-funzionale;
  • Affiancare il team nell’analisi di errori applicativi e nelle attività di troubleshooting.


| COSA RICHIEDIAMO

Mandatoria la Laurea Magistrale STEM (Informatica, Ingegneria informatica, Matematica e affini), con voto minimo di 105.


  • Esperienza, anche iniziale, in ruoli di data analysis, technical analysis, business analysis o supporto applicativo;
  • Buona conoscenza di SQL;
  • Familiarità con basi dati relazionali e analisi dei dati;
  • Interesse per il funzionamento di API, integrazioni applicative e microservizi;
  • Capacità di comprendere requisiti funzionali e tradurli in analisi tecniche operative;
  • Buona conoscenza di strumenti di produttività e documentazione;
  • Attitudine al lavoro in team e alla collaborazione con figure tecniche.


| COSA OFFRIAMO?

  • Inserimento su progetti internazionali in ambito bancario;
  • Coinvolgimento su architetture enterprise moderne;
  • Ambiente strutturato, solido e orientato alla qualità.


Modalità di lavoro: ibrida (3+2) – Napoli

Contratto a tempo indeterminato e RAL commisurata alla reale esperienza


L’offerta si rivolge a candidati ambosessi, nel rispetto del Codice delle pari opportunità D.Lgs. 11 aprile 2006, N. 198 e ss.mm.ii.

Title: Data Functional Analyst
Description:
Data Functional Analyst




About the Role: We are looking for a skilled Functional Data Analyst to join our “Data Team” within the B2B Supply Belgium business platform. This platform serves B2B customers in Belgium and consists of around 180 people, including 120 on the business side and 60 on the IS and Data side. You will work on various data use cases, including Metering Data Management, customer reporting, and Green Energy Reporting.




Key Responsibilities:

Act as a liaison between product owners and the technical Data Team, ensuring a clear understanding of business requirements.
Quickly familiarize yourself with the business domain (Metering Data Management) and analyze business requirements to interpret the specs accurately.
Translate business requirements into functional and technical specifications that guide Data Engineers in building data pipelines.
Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand business processes and document functional requirements.
Plan and organize functional testing and actively manage business validation processes.
Work with application and data source owners to map out functional data flows, using your technical knowledge and market expertise to ask relevant questions.




Required Skills and Experience:

Must-Have:

Experience in the Utilities sector, specifically with Metering/Allocation data.
Proven experience in Functional Analysis and data modeling.
Proactive, hands-on approach with the ability to work autonomously.
Strong communication and negotiation skills, able to engage with stakeholders effectively.
Experience working in Agile methodologies and familiarity with Agile tools such as Azure DevOps or Jira.

Nice-to-Have:

Knowledge of the Belgian Utilities Market (ATRIAS).
Previous experience with utilities market, metering, and allocation data management.
Familiarity with technical systems like SAP ISU.
Knowledge of data platform concepts.




Soft Skills:

Must-Have:

Strong ability to translate business requirements into clear and effective technical solutions.

Data Functional Analyst


About the Role: We are looking for a skilled Functional Data Analyst to join our “Data Team” within the B2B Supply Belgium business platform. This platform serves B2B customers in Belgium and consists of around 180 people, including 120 on the business side and 60 on the IS and Data side. You will work on various data use cases, including Metering Data Management, customer reporting, and Green Energy Reporting.


Key Responsibilities:

Required Skills and Experience:

Must-Have:

  • Experience in the Utilities sector, specifically with Metering/Allocation data.
  • Proven experience in Functional Analysis and data modeling.

  • Experience working in Agile methodologies and familiarity with Agile tools such as Azure DevOps or Jira.

Description:
We’re looking for the special, unique, and amazing YOU!




@ KCS IT, we look for the ones that stands out, for those that always wants to be better and fight for it, and for those who has the same values that we do: dedication, energy, integrity, transparency, flexibility, trust, honesty, hard work, proactivity, teamwork.




At KCS we stand for equality and value diversity. We create a safe, diverse environment where opportunities are equal for everyone! Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field;

Good level of english.




Why should you become part of our family?

Interesting Challenges Ahead: you can work for several clients from different sectors of activity

Title: Analista de Datos Junior
Description:
¿Quieres formar parte de la experiencia Exceltic, una empresa con estrategia centrada en las personas?




¿Buscas formar parte de una gran variedad de proyectos punteros en una empresa de ingeniería en fuerte expansión? ¡Queremos conocerte!




¡Ésta es tu oportunidad!👇




Estamos en búsqueda de un/a Analista de Datos Junior con experiencia en análisis de datos y desarrollo de métricas e indicadores, para formar parte de nuestro equipo.




🎯 ¿Cuál será tu misión?

Impulsar el análisis y la calidad del dato mediante el desarrollo de métricas, indicadores y cuadros de mando que contribuyan a la optimización de operaciones y la toma de decisiones estratégicas.




📝 En tu día a día te encargarás de:

Analizar y tratar datos operacionales y de negocio.
Diseñar y desarrollar cuadros de mando e informes.
Definir y realizar seguimiento de métricas e indicadores clave.
Supervisar y analizar la calidad de los datos.
Elaborar reportes y documentación asociada.
Colaborar con diferentes áreas para la mejora continua de procesos y reporting.




✅ Este rol es para ti si cuentas con:

Grado o Máster en Gestión Aeronáutica / Gestión de Operaciones y Tráfico Aéreo / ADE o equivalente.
Experiencia mínima de 6 meses en tratamiento y análisis de datos.
Experiencia en desarrollo de cuadros de mando, métricas e indicadores.
Conocimientos en análisis y control de calidad de datos.
Manejo de entornos Windows y herramientas ofimáticas.
Conocimientos en bases de datos, Power BI y Python.
Nivel de inglés B1.




💫 Vivir la experiencia Exceltic significa:

Tener una posición estable con contrato indefinido.
Participar en proyectos punteros donde puedas desarrollar tus habilidades.
Acceder a un plan de carrera personalizado y formación continua.
Disfrutar de un plan de retribución flexible (seguro médico, ticket transporte, restaurante y guardería).
Compartir el camino con un equipo de profesionales apasionados y comprometidos.




💬 "Acerca de Exceltic…"

En Exceltic somos una empresa líder en ingeniería y consultoría tecnológica. Desarrollamos nuestra actividad en diversos sectores como el ferroviario, aeronáutico, industrial, tecnológico y muchos más. Contamos con cerca de 600 profesionales apasionados, oficinas en Madrid, Barcelona, Valencia, Valladolid y Bilbao, Sevilla y proyectos en los cinco continentes.




¡Únete a nuestro equipo y construye el futuro con Exceltic! 🚀




✨ Tu próximo reto profesional puede empezar aquí.

Registra tu perfil en el portal de carreras de Exceltic y conecta con proyectos innovadores a nivel nacional e internacional:

https://careers.exceltic.com/login

Inetum is a European leader in digital services. Inetum’s team of 28,000 consultants and specialists strive every day to make a digital impact for businesses, public sector entities and society. Inetum’s solutions aim at contributing to its clients’ performance and innovation as well as the common good. Present in 19 countries with a dense network of sites, Inetum partners with major software publishers to meet the challenges of digital transformation with proximity and flexibility. Driven by its ambition for growth and scale, Inetum generated sales of 2.5 billion euros in 2023.

Creation of dashboards and reports to support decision-making.
Implementation of data visualization strategies.
Data Governance: asset cataloging, application of technical and functional rules.
Data Quality: execution of profiles and alerts for stakeholders.
Connections with Microsoft Fabric, Dynamics, and other sources.
Support to business teams in Purview, Data Governance, and Data Quality.
Onboarding and training

Experience in Data Governance and EDP projects.

Microsoft Purview (implementation of data quality rules)
Microsoft Fabric
Power BI
Azure
Databricks
AWS
Languages and tools:
SQL
Advanced Excel
Dashboard tools
Desirable knowledge:
SAS Enterprise Guide
DOMO
DAMAn framework
Experience in CI/CD and agile methodologies.


Additional Information

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Rotterdam (three days onsite).

Salary €65,000 to €80,000 plus benefits.




This is a great opportunity to step into a business-critical data analyst role where your insights will directly influence priorities, decision-making and growth. You will join at a moment of change, with the chance to shape how data is used across the organisation and take real ownership of analytical projects.




The Company

They are a growing organisation operating in a dynamic market environment, where data plays an increasingly important role in strategy and performance. The business values clear communication, initiative and practical insight over theory alone. With a lean data setup, they offer a close-knit environment where your work is visible and impactful. Collaboration with senior stakeholders is a core part of how they operate.




The Role

You will be responsible for delivering actionable insights and managing BI-focused data initiatives across the business.
You will work closely with stakeholders to understand needs, set priorities and translate questions into analysis.
You will take ownership of both larger analytical initiatives and smaller ad hoc projects.
You will support the evolution of reporting and dashboards using SQL-based data models and Power BI.
You will help reassess team focus and objectives following recent organisational changes.

Your Skills and Experience

Strong commercial experience in SQL for data analysis and data modelling.
Hands-on experience building reports and dashboards in Power BI.
Confidence working independently and managing analytical projects end to end.
Proven ability to engage with stakeholders and communicate insights clearly.
Exposure to Python or advanced analytics is beneficial but not essential.




What They Offer

A competitive salary between €65,000 and €80,000 with flexibility based on experience.
Recently updated salary bands with clear room for growth.
Hybrid working with a strong in-office collaboration culture in Rotterdam.
A high-impact role with autonomy, ownership and visibility at senior level.




How to Apply

If you are a Data Analyst looking for a role where you can truly influence how data is used, apply now to learn more.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Experian is a global data and technology company, powering opportunities for people and businesses around the world. We help to redefine lending practices, uncover and prevent fraud, simplify healthcare, deliver digital marketing solutions, and gain deeper insights into the automotive market, all using our unique combination of data, analytics and software. We also assist millions of people to realise their financial goals and help them to save time and money.

We operate across a range of markets, from financial services to healthcare, automotive, agrifinance, insurance, and many more industry segments.

We invest in people and new advanced technologies to unlock the power of data and to innovate. A FTSE 100 Index company listed on the London Stock Exchange (EXPN), we have a team of 23,300 people across 32 countries. Our corporate headquarters are in Dublin, Ireland. Learn more at experianplc.com.

Job Description

We are hiring for a fundamental role within our Marketing Product Development team. You will play an important role within the department, reporting into the Analytics Manager.

What you'll do?


Deliver spatial analytical projects which will involve analysing data sets, getting to the bottom of what is going on with the data;
Communicate your findings to different stakeholders;
Blend a knowledge of mathematical, analytical and spatial techniques with a natural curiosity for understanding and analysing data.


Qualifications

What you'll bring?

We are looking for a recent graduate or a professional with up to 1 year of experience who is eager to start their career in data analytics. You are curious, motivated to learn, and enjoy working with data to uncover insights.

Therefore, You'll Bring


A degree (or final year student) in Mathematics, Statistics, Data Science, Geography, Economics, or a related field
Experience with data analysis and interpretation
Numerical and analytical thinking skills
Interest in spatial data / GIS (Geographic Information Systems)


It Would Be Great If You Have Previous Experience Data Analysis And Interpretation And An Understanding Of Any Programming Languages And Applications Such As


ArcGIS, QGIS or other GIS software;
SQL;
SAS;
Python;
GIT;
Alteryx;


Additional Information

You will get:


Personal Development - career pathway for professional growth supported by learning and development programs and unlimited access to online educational training courses, learning materials and books.
Work environment - excellent work conditions with friendly environment, recognized team spirit, and fun and quality recreation time.
Social benefit package including life insurance, food vouchers, additional health insurance, monthly flex allowance and internet coverage, corporate discounts, marriage and childbirth / adoption allowance, Multisport card, Sharesave plan, Employee assistance program, а birthday gift and many other benefits!
Work-life balance - 25 days paid vacation, 1 additional day off for your birthday and extra 3 paid days for participation in Social responsibility event.
Opportunity for Flexible working hours and Home Office.


Experian is an Equal opportunity employer. Everyone can succeed at Experian and bring their whole self to work, irrespective of their gender, ethnicity, religion, colour, sexuality, physical ability or age. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know at the earliest opportunity.

This is a hybrid remote/in-office role.

Experian Careers - Creating a better tomorrow together

Title: Associate Data Analyst (SQL & Excel)
Description:
Sphera is a leading global provider of enterprise software and services that enables companies to manage and optimize their environmental, health, safety and sustainability. Our mission is to create a safer, more sustainable and productive world.

Sphera is a portfolio company of Blackstone, a U.S.-based alternative asset investment company that focuses on private equity, technology and innovation, and more. Blackstone businesses succeed through strong partnerships, a personalized approach and a commitment to exceptional performance with uncompromising integrity. Sphera and Blackstone are leaders in the Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) space.

We are guided by our core values of Customer Centricity, Accountability, Bias to Action, Innovation, and Collaboration. These values help us recruit the right talent to join our rapidly expanding team of around the globe. It is important to us that each and every Spherion is not only eager to challenge themselves and knows how to get work done but is an awesome addition to our company culture.

In this role, you will support customers using our Supply Chain Risk Management solution, helping them onboard successfully, manage critical supplier and risk data, and gain valuable insights through reporting and analytics.

You will work closely with customers and cross-functional teams to resolve queries, maintain data quality, and deliver meaningful dashboards and reports. Leveraging your expertise in SQL, Excel, and data modeling, you will play a key role in ensuring accurate, reliable data that enables customers to identify, assess, and manage supply chain risks effectively.

Responsibilities


Support customer onboarding activities and manage cases end-to-end, ensuring a smooth and high-quality experience
Handle customer data-related requests, including validation, correction, reporting, and data imports
Collaborate closely with internal teams to resolve customer queries efficiently and effectively
Generate insights by building SQL queries and extracting data from databases
Design and maintain dashboards for both customers and internal stakeholders
Manage data lifecycle activities: importing, extracting, validating, and maintaining data accuracy
Apply best practices in data reporting and analysis, ensuring strong data integrity, accuracy, and documentation
Work with multiple data sources, performing advanced extraction, transformation, and modeling


Requirements


2+ years of experience in data extraction, manipulation, modeling, and reporting
Advanced SQL skills and strong Excel expertise
Good working knowledge of Power Query
Experience building data models, projections, and pivot tables from multiple data sources
Strong English communication skills (spoken and written)
Native Polish language


Sphera is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all colleagues.

This job description is intended to convey information essential to understanding the scope of the job and the general nature and level of work performed by job holders within this job. This job description is not intended to be an exhaustive list of qualifications, skills, efforts, duties, responsibilities or working conditions associated with the position.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
En Volkswagen Group Services Barcelona estamos buscando un/ nuevo/a Compañero/a que dará soporte directo en análisis de datos de negocio al responsable de área. Proporcionará información estructurada, informes periódicos e insights relevantes sobre el rendimiento de los servicios. Su labor se centrará en el análisis de indicadores clave como enrollment, automatic service scheduling y la evolución de los servicios por mercado, país y modelo, así como en el mantenimiento de dashboards y herramientas de reporting en Power BI, asegurando la calidad, fiabilidad y disponibilidad de la información necesaria para la toma de decisiones del área.

Entre las funciones del puesto:

1. Soporte en el análisis de datos del servicio: Dar apoyo al responsable interno en el análisis de datos de servicios conectados (enrollment, automatic service scheduling, métricas de negocio adicionales, uso por mercado/modelo), facilitando la identificación de patrones y desviaciones relevantes.

2.Apoyo en la generación de insights accionables: Asistir al responsable interno en la extracción de conclusiones relevantes a partir de los datos, enfocadas en mejorar el negocio y la experiencia del cliente.

3. Apoyo en la definición y seguimiento de KPIs: Colaborar en la definición, mantenimiento y actualización de KPIs de segundo nivel orientados a negocio, asegurando consistencia de métricas.

4. Preparación de información para la toma de decisiones: Preparar y estructurar datos, informes y resúmenes ejecutivos como base para las decisiones del responsable del área.

5. Elaboración de informes periódicos y análisis puntuales: Ejecutar reportes recurrentes y análisis ad-hoc bajo demanda del responsable interno o de otros stakeholders del área.

6. Soporte en la gestión de Power BI y reporting: Mantener dashboards, modelos de datos y visualizaciones en Power BI bajo la dirección del responsable del área.

7. Apoyo en el control de calidad del dato: Colaborar en la revisión de fuentes de datos, identificación de inconsistencias y validación de resultados de reporting.

8. Coordinación con equipos técnicos y de datos: Alinear fuentes, definiciones de KPI y metodología con IT y equipos analíticos.

9. Preparación de presentaciones y documentación: Elaborar materiales de soporte para comités, reuniones y comunicaciones internas.




Qué esperamos de ti:

Grado universitario en Ingeniería Economía, Analítica de Datos o formación equivalente.
Valorable formación específica en gestión de proyectos data analytics o negocios digitales.
Valorable formación en Power BI y Business Intelligence (modelado de datos, DAX, visualización y gobierno del dato).
Análisis de datos, reporting avanzado o herramientas BI complementarias.
Mínimo 2 años en funciones de soporte analítico, reporting, gestión de datos o soporte operativo a áreas de negocio, preferiblemente en entornos digitales, tecnológicos o de automoción.
Inglés c1
Análisis e interpretación de datos orientados a negocio.
Definición y seguimiento de KPIs y métricas de rendimiento.
Gestión administrativa y presupuestaria básica (volúmenes, facturación, licencias).
Capacidad para estructurar información compleja y convertirla en reportes ejecutivos.
Coordinación en entornos transversales y trabajo colaborativo con múltiples áreas.
Ofimática avanzada: PowerPoint, Excel.
Business Intelligence y reporting: Power BI (nivel medio).
Gestión de trabajo y documentación: Confluence, Jira (valorable)
Conocimiento de metodologías de trabajo Agile (SAFe, Scrum)




Qué podemos ofrecerte:

Subvención diaria para la cantina.
Acceso gratuito a transporte de empresa.
Opción para participar en el Plan de Retribución Flexible, según la normativa interna de la empresa.
Acceso subvencionado a formación en idiomas.
Acceso a Plataforma de Formación E-learning.
Beneficios corporativos.
Descuentos en automóviles del grupo.
Hasta el 60% de teletrabajo

Title: Analista datos financieros
Description:
Da el siguiente paso en tu carrera y únete a CGI en España




Fundada en 1976, CGI se encuentra entre las mayores empresas de servicios de consultoría TI y de negocio del mundo. Nos basamos en conocimientos y resultados para ayudar a acelerar la transformación digital de nuestros clientes. A través de 21 sectores en 400 ubicaciones en todo el mundo, nuestros 94.000 profesionales proporcionan servicios de consultoría TI y de negocio integrales, escalables y sostenibles que entregamos localmente.




Buscamos Data Analyst para incorporación a proyecto de importante entidad bancaria internacional:




Your future duties and responsibilities

Extracción y análisis de datos

Desarrollo y optimización de dashboards

Automatización de reportes

Análisis de indicadores




Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

Conocimiento experto entorno google (google sheet, looker studio....)
Experiencia en haber tratado documentos de gran volumen de datos
Capacididad Analítica
Conocimientos financieros
Capacidad de automatización




¿Por qué elegir CGI en España?




Además de trabajar con tecnologías de vanguardia y equipos multidisciplinares, te ofrecemos:

Estabilidad y proyección

Contrato indefinido y paquete retributivo competitivo (ticket restaurante, tarjeta de transporte, seguro de vida y accidentes, etc.)
Participación desde el primer día en el Plan de Compra de Acciones global.
Plan de carrera flexible y adaptado a tus expectativas.

Crecimiento permanente

Formación continua, acceso a certificaciones oficiales y comunidades internas de conocimiento.
Oportunidades de trabajar con expertos globales y aplicar buenas prácticas internacionales localmente.

Conciliación real y beneficios

Trabajo híbrido o remoto según proyecto, y horarios flexibles.
Jornada continua los viernes, meses de verano con jornada reducida y días extra no laborables.
Programas de salud y bienestar a nivel local y global

Impacto social y sostenibilidad

CGI impulsa iniciativas globales y locales para generar impacto en las comunidades en las vivimos y trabajamos, desde proyectos medioambientales hasta educación digital. Formar parte de CGI significa trabajar con propósito y ayudar a construir un futuro más justo y sostenible.

CGI a nivel global: un partner de confianza para proyectos con impacto

CGI colabora con clientes privados y administraciones públicas en proyectos estratégicos y de alto valor que marcan la diferencia:

Partners tecnológicos de confianza en grandes transformaciones digitales: establecemos relaciones a largo plazo con organizaciones líderes, operando servicios gestionados que garantizan décadas de eficiencia y estabilidad.
Transformación digital para sectores complejos: ayudamos a clientes globales y locales a modernizar sus sistemas, cadenas de suministro y plataformas clave para operar en el ecosistema actual.
Reconocimiento internacional como consultora de referencia: Forbes nos continúa distinguiendo como una de las “Mejores Empresas de Consultoría de Gestión del Mundo” y la revista TIME nos ha vuelto a incluir en la lista de las “Mejores Empresas del Mundo”
Presencia en proyectos críticos de infraestructuras TI en múltiples países, como contratos relevantes con agencias y administraciones públicas en Norteamérica y Europa.

¿Qué significa esto para ti?

Trabajar en CGI en España no es un empleo más: es formar parte de una red global donde:

Contribuyes en proyectos reales que transforman sectores y mejoran procesos globales, marcando la diferencia a escala local y global
Aprendes continuamente gracias a una cultura que impulsa el crecimiento profesional.
Tu voz importa, tanto local como globalmente, porque nuestras soluciones se crean con talento diverso y colaborativo.
Podrás ser tú, innovar y crecer gracias a nuestra cultura basada en la colaboración, el respeto y los resultados




Únete a CGI en España y transforma tu carrera.




Juntos, como propietarios, transformemos nuestro conocimiento en acción.

La vida en CGI está arraigada en la propiedad, el trabajo en equipo, el respeto y la pertenencia. Aquí alcanzarás todo tu potencial porque...

Te invitamos a ser propietario de la compañía desde el primer día, mientras trabajamos juntos para hacer realidad nuestro sueño. Por eso nos llamamos asociados de CGI y no empleados. Nos beneficiamos de nuestro éxito colectivo y tenemos la oportunidad y la responsabilidad de contribuir activamente en la estrategia y dirección de nuestra compañía.

Tu trabajo crea valor. Desarrollarás soluciones innovadoras y establecerás relaciones con compañeros y clientes, al tiempo que accederás a capacidades globales para hacer realidad tus ideas, aprovechar nuevas oportunidades y beneficiarte de una amplia experiencia sectorial y conocimiento tecnológico.

Darás forma a tu carrera uniéndote a una empresa construida para crecer y durar. Contarás con el apoyo de managers que se preocupan por tu salud y bienestar y te ofrecerán oportunidades para perfeccionar tus habilidades y ampliar tus horizontes.

Únete a nosotros - una de las mayores firmas independientes de servicios TI y consultoría de negocio del mundo.




Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.

Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.

Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description

The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is an international organisation comprised of 38 member countries, that works to build better policies for better lives. Our mission is to promote policies that will improve the economic and social well-being of people around the world. Together with governments, policy makers and citizens, we work on establishing evidence-based international standards, and finding solutions to a range of social, economic and environmental challenges. From improving economic performance and creating jobs to fostering strong education and fighting international tax evasion, we provide a unique forum and knowledge hub for data and analysis, exchange of experiences, best-practice sharing, and advice on public policies and international standard-setting.

The OECD has earned a leading role in agriculture. The Trade and Agriculture Directorate (TAD) is the focal point for the OECD’s work on agriculture, both international and domestic, and it works to advance the Strategic Orientations of the Secretary-General, including on climate change mitigation and on open trade and markets, ensuring impact of the OECD Agriculture work in the international governance architecture, in co-ordination with the OECD Sherpa team.

TAD works covers a wide set of analysis of food and agriculture policy, agri-food trade and market and agricultural sustainability under the responsibility of two divisions, the Agriculture and Resource Policies (ARP) Division and the Agro-Food Trade and Markets (ATM) Division. TAD work involves a mixture of analytical and policy-oriented research conducted in a dynamic, multicultural environment by a team of highly-qualified and experienced staff.

TAD/ATM is looking for a Junior Data Analyst to contribute to its work on agricultural commodity markets and related analysis on agriculture and food policy issues. The Junior Data Analyst will prepare data for the OECD-FAO Agricultural Outlook, lead the Outlook projections for one commodity group and contribute to the ongoing development of the Outlook data process and the related agricultural market model (Aglink-Cosimo). Contributions to other data work of the TAD/ATM Division is going to be part of the regular work as well. The Junior Data Analyst will work under the oversight of the Head of Division in TAD/ATM.

Job Description

Main Responsibilities

Research, Analysis and drafting


Prepare the database for the Agricultural Outlook, improving the data process of Aglink-Cosimo and analysing of Aglink-Cosimo results, including the development and updating of relevant software, AI applications and modelling tools.
Contribute to the preparation of the OECD-FAO Agricultural Outlook with specific focus on one commodity chapter and to other publications designed for a policy audience.
Prepare data output, dashboards, presentations, and briefing material that are clear, concise and relevant for policy makers.
Ensure continuous awareness of emerging research (especially regarding data processes), exploratory work and evolving policies on agriculture commodity and food markets and trade, emanating from governments, other international organisations, non-governmental organisations, academia and the private sector.


Liaison, representation, and meetings


Participate to the organisation of workshops, webinars and events organised by the OECD on agriculture and food, as requested, including by interacting with prospective speakers.
Coordinating data collection with member countries and international commodity organisations.
Collaborate with other Divisions and Directorates and contribute to horizontal and multidisciplinary projects and outputs as needed.
Build and maintain a network of contacts with government officials, analysts and experts in international organisations, research institutions, universities and NGOs.


Other duties


Provide general support to the activities of the Division as required, in particular as requested by the Head of Division.


Qualifications

Ideal Candidate Profile

Academic Background


An advanced university degree in econometrics, applied economics or political sciences, with an emphasis on agriculture, food or environmental issues.


Professional Background


Minimum three years' experience in economic analysis on agriculture, food, environment or related topics.

Proficiency in analysis of quantitative information and experience with advanced analytical tools (Excel, GAMS, AI approaches and especially Python) is a requirement.

There are three possible levels for each competency. The level for each competency is determined according to the specific needs of each job role and its associated grade.
To learn more about the definitions for each competency for levels 1-3, please refer to OECD Core Competencies.


Languages


Fluency in one of the two OECD official languages (English and French) and knowledge of the other, with a commitment to reach a good working level.


Additional Information

Closing Date:


This vacancy will be filled as soon as possible, and applications should reach us no later than midnight 11 June 2026 (CEST).


Contract Duration


12-month fixed term appointment, with the possibility of renewal to a maximum of 36 months.


Please note that our Rules and Regulations stipulate that the mandatory retirement age is 67.

Depending on level of experience, monthly salary starts at 6 008 EUR, plus allowances based on eligibility, exempt of French income tax.

HTML:
Company Description

The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is an international organisation comprised of 38 member countries, that works to build better policies for better lives. Our mission is to promote policies that will improve the economic and social well-being of people around the world. Together with governments, policy makers and citizens, we work on establishing evidence-based international standards, and finding solutions to a range of social, economic and environmental challenges. From improving economic performance and creating jobs to fostering strong education and fighting international tax evasion, we provide a unique forum and knowledge hub for data and analysis, exchange of experiences, best-practice sharing, and advice on public policies and international standard-setting.

The OECD has earned a leading role in agriculture. The Trade and Agriculture Directorate (TAD) is the focal point for the OECD’s work on agriculture, both international and domestic, and it works to advance the Strategic Orientations of the Secretary-General, including on climate change mitigation and on open trade and markets, ensuring impact of the OECD Agriculture work in the international governance architecture, in co-ordination with the OECD Sherpa team.

TAD works covers a wide set of analysis of food and agriculture policy, agri-food trade and market and agricultural sustainability under the responsibility of two divisions, the Agriculture and Resource Policies (ARP) Division and the Agro-Food Trade and Markets (ATM) Division. TAD work involves a mixture of analytical and policy-oriented research conducted in a dynamic, multicultural environment by a team of highly-qualified and experienced staff.

TAD/ATM is looking for a Junior Data Analyst to contribute to its work on agricultural commodity markets and related analysis on agriculture and food policy issues. The Junior Data Analyst will prepare data for the OECD-FAO Agricultural Outlook, lead the Outlook projections for one commodity group and contribute to the ongoing development of the Outlook data process and the related agricultural market model (Aglink-Cosimo). Contributions to other data work of the TAD/ATM Division is going to be part of the regular work as well. The Junior Data Analyst will work under the oversight of the Head of Division in TAD/ATM.

Job Description

Main Responsibilities

Research, Analysis and drafting

  • Prepare the database for the Agricultural Outlook, improving the data process of Aglink-Cosimo and analysing of Aglink-Cosimo results, including the development and updating of relevant software, AI applications and modelling tools.
  • Contribute to the preparation of the OECD-FAO Agricultural Outlook with specific focus on one commodity chapter and to other publications designed for a policy audience.
  • Prepare data output, dashboards, presentations, and briefing material that are clear, concise and relevant for policy makers.
  • Ensure continuous awareness of emerging research (especially regarding data processes), exploratory work and evolving policies on agriculture commodity and food markets and trade, emanating from governments, other international organisations, non-governmental organisations, academia and the private sector.

Liaison, representation, and meetings

  • Participate to the organisation of workshops, webinars and events organised by the OECD on agriculture and food, as requested, including by interacting with prospective speakers.
  • Coordinating data collection with member countries and international commodity organisations.
  • Collaborate with other Divisions and Directorates and contribute to horizontal and multidisciplinary projects and outputs as needed.
  • Build and maintain a network of contacts with government officials, analysts and experts in international organisations, research institutions, universities and NGOs.

Other duties

  • Provide general support to the activities of the Division as required, in particular as requested by the Head of Division.

Qualifications

Ideal Candidate Profile

Academic Background

  • An advanced university degree in econometrics, applied economics or political sciences, with an emphasis on agriculture, food or environmental issues.

Professional Background

  • Minimum three years' experience in economic analysis on agriculture, food, environment or related topics.
  • Proven capacity to organize data collection, econometric analysis and mathematical programming.
  • Proven capacity for sound analytical and policy-orientated work; to effectively execute research and to synthesise and communicate quantitative information for audiences at governmental or intergovernmental level.

Tools

  • Proficiency in analysis of quantitative information and experience with advanced analytical tools (Excel, GAMS, AI approaches and especially Python) is a requirement.

Core Competencies

  • OECD staff are expected to demonstrate behaviours aligned to six core competencies which will be assessed as part of this hiring processes: Vision and Strategy (Level 1); Enable People (Level 1); Ethics and Integrity (Level 1); Collaboration and Horizontality (Level 1); Achieve Results (Level 2); Innovate and Embrace Change (Level 2).
  • There are three possible levels for each competency. The level for each competency is determined according to the specific needs of each job role and its associated grade.
  • To learn more about the definitions for each competency for levels 1-3, please refer to OECD Core Competencies.

Languages

  • Fluency in one of the two OECD official languages (English and French) and knowledge of the other, with a commitment to reach a good working level.

Additional Information

Closing Date:

  • This vacancy will be filled as soon as possible, and applications should reach us no later than midnight 11 June 2026 (CEST).

Contract Duration

  • 12-month fixed term appointment, with the possibility of renewal to a maximum of 36 months.

Please note that our Rules and Regulations stipulate that the mandatory retirement age is 67.

What the OECD offers

  • Depending on level of experience, monthly salary starts at 6 008 EUR, plus allowances based on eligibility, exempt of French income tax.

Selection Process

The OECD is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes the applications of all qualified candidates who are nationals of OECD member countries, irrespective of their racial or ethnic origin, opinions or beliefs, gender, sexual orientation, health or disabilities.

travailler pour plusieurs Squads, avec des interlocuteurs variés,

Comfortable bridging business and technical teams

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description




Inetum is a global leader in IT services, dedicated to delivering innovative solutions to our clients. We are committed to fostering a dynamic and inclusive work environment that values diversity, where creativity and collaboration can thrive. We are present in 19 countries and have more than 28,000 employees worldwide.

If you are looking for a dynamic, innovative, and technology-driven company, Inetum is the place for you! Come be part of Inetum!




Job Description




Identify business problems and propose data-driven solutions to support decision-making and growth
Retrieve, clean, prepare, and analyze complex datasets from multiple sources
Design and implement analyses using statistical methods and data models
Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations following best practices (Tableau, Power BI)
Build automated tools and solutions to deliver recurring insights efficiently
Contribute to data feed industrialization, ETL pipelines, and data warehousing processes
Translate business requirements into technical specifications for the development team
Communicate complex analytical results clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Collaborate closely with traders, sales teams, business sponsors, and technical teams




Qualifications




Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
Professional experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis using programming languages, preferably Python (Pandas)
Strong ability to analyze, synthesize, and simplify complex information
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus
Familiarity with Big Data technologies such as PySpark, Presto, and Airflow is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus
Fluent in English

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Decskill was founded in 2014 as an IT Consulting Company and their main mission is to delivery value through the knowledge. We enable companies to meet the chalenges of digital world by providing our clients with business models that ensure technological capacity, flexibility and agility. We are more than 500 consultants with offices in Lisbon, Porto and Madrid.




DECSKILL operates in 3 main areas:




DECSKILL TALENT, through which we provide our clients with an extension to their IT teams;




DECSKILL BOOST, through which we provide our client with software development models to increase capacity and optimize Time-to-Market, where we create and manage teams that deliver according to their needs, at the desired speed;




DECSKILL CONNECT through which we provide our client with consulting services, as well as the implementation and management of information technology infrastructures.




Our practice results in the creation of value for our customers, either by delivering qualified and value-added services, or through highly qualified and motivated professionals, as well as technology solutions that allow us to operate and transform the business of our customers.




We are looking for a Data Analyst:




RESPONSABILITIES:

As a Data Analyst, you will play a vital role in our team.
Working directly with one or more of the bank's business areas, you will provide valuable insights to drive business growth, understading their needs, proposing and implementing data-driven solutions (analysis, visualization and automation). You will exercise a lot of problem identification and problem solving skills.
You will have the opportunity to take ownership of business essential analytical projects that involve retrieving, preparing and analysing complex datasets using data/statistical models and best practices in data visualization for the reporting creation, with the goal to produce insights and automated tools that will guide strategic solutions
You will move between technical and non-technical areas and should be able to communicate well with different types of professionals.
It is very important to be able to clearly and concisely understand stekeholders’ needs and translate these to our technical team, as well as present the results of complex analysis to our stakeholders.




TECHNICAL SKILLS:

Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computing, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Ability to analysze, synthetize and simplify.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
Knowledge in Big Data technology such as Pyspark, Presto and Airflow is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus




IDIOMAS:

Inglês - Avançado- (Obrigatório)
Francês - Noções - PLUS




If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to carolina.mustra@decskill.com with reference “CM/Data Analyst”

HTML:
Decskill was founded in 2014 as an IT Consulting Company and their main mission is to delivery value through the knowledge. We enable companies to meet the chalenges of digital world by providing our clients with business models that ensure technological capacity, flexibility and agility. We are more than 500 consultants with offices in Lisbon, Porto and Madrid.


DECSKILL operates in 3 main areas:


  • DECSKILL TALENT, through which we provide our clients with an extension to their IT teams;


  • DECSKILL BOOST, through which we provide our client with software development models to increase capacity and optimize Time-to-Market, where we create and manage teams that deliver according to their needs, at the desired speed;


  • DECSKILL CONNECT through which we provide our client with consulting services, as well as the implementation and management of information technology infrastructures.


Our practice results in the creation of value for our customers, either by delivering qualified and value-added services, or through highly qualified and motivated professionals, as well as technology solutions that allow us to operate and transform the business of our customers.


We are looking for a Data Analyst:


RESPONSABILITIES:

  • As a Data Analyst, you will play a vital role in our team.
  • Working directly with one or more of the bank's business areas, you will provide valuable insights to drive business growth, understading their needs, proposing and implementing data-driven solutions (analysis, visualization and automation). You will exercise a lot of problem identification and problem solving skills.
  • You will have the opportunity to take ownership of business essential analytical projects that involve retrieving, preparing and analysing complex datasets using data/statistical models and best practices in data visualization for the reporting creation, with the goal to produce insights and automated tools that will guide strategic solutions
  • You will move between technical and non-technical areas and should be able to communicate well with different types of professionals.
  • It is very important to be able to clearly and concisely understand stekeholders’ needs and translate these to our technical team, as well as present the results of complex analysis to our stakeholders.


TECHNICAL SKILLS:

  • Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computing, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields
  • Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
  • Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
  • Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
  • Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
  • Ability to analysze, synthetize and simplify.
  • Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
  • Knowledge in Big Data technology such as Pyspark, Presto and Airflow is a plus
  • Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus


IDIOMAS:

  • Inglês - Avançado- (Obrigatório)
  • Francês - Noções - PLUS


If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to carolina.mustra@decskill.com with reference “CM/Data Analyst”


Thank You! 😊


Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being

Title: Data Analyst TRAINEE
Description:
Job Summary

We are looking for a Data Analyst Intern with 1–2 years of prior experience in data analytics, data management, or business analytics. He or she will work closely with cross-functional stakeholders (Data Engineering, Cybersecurity, IT, Business Domains, Technological Centers) and support key initiatives to drive data governance, data quality, standardize definitions, and data trust.




Key Accountabilities

Data Quality

•Assist in identifying and documenting data quality framework, ensuring correct tracking, classification, and follow-up.

•Help maintain standardized definitions, taxonomies, and controlled vocabularies across domains.

Data Catalog Management

•Contribute to promote the adoption and awareness of the company’s Data Catalog.

•Update glossary terms and domain ownership and information management.

Collaboration & Enablement

• Support Data & Digital Innovation to document and communicate data governance processes

• Document and manage content in different formats for presentations and communication strategy. Collaborate with other Data and business teams to understand data issues and support resolution.

•Contribute to training materials, onboarding guides, and communication updates.




Minimum Knowledge & Experience required for the position:

•Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Information Systems, Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, Business Analytics, or related discipline (completed or in final stage).

•1–2 years of experience (through full-time roles, internships, or academic projects) in Data analysis, data management, or business intelligence.




Skills & Capabilities:

Basic understanding of:

•Data governance and or Data management tools & concepts (data lineage, metadata, data quality, RBAC, data privacy).

•SQL and/or Python

•Data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, Qlik).

Preferred Skills

•Cloud Data Platforms knowledge (Azure, AWS, GCP) and modern data architectures.

•Strong communication and documentation skills. Writen and verbal english communication skills

•Detail-oriented mindset and curiosity for understanding data end to end

•Ability to collaborate in a multi-disciplinary environment and complex organization matrix
