Title: Data Analyst (Data Partner) – m/f/d
Description:
✨ About Voyage Privé



Born in France in 2006, Voyage Privé has grown from an ambitious startup into becoming the Europe's leading travel tech platform. Operating across 9 markets with tens of millions of users, we're not just another e-commerce success story - we're a tech powerhouse revolutionizing online travel.  



What makes us unique? A mission-driven culture where performance meets impact. Our innovative campus brings together tech talent, professional athletes, students, and artists, creating an ecosystem where digital innovation drives both business growth and positive change.  



We're now at an inflection point, upgrading our entire technical foundation with cloud architecture, AI, and real-time systems to become a reference and top-of-mind platform for luxury travel, known by travelers for its for excellent offer and customer experience, and by our providers as a high-performance business development partner. 





Job Description



🎯 Your Mission



As a Data Partner, you’ll play a key role in shaping the future of your domain squad. You’ll work closely with Product Managers, Engineers, Designers, and Business Representatives to deliver measurable impact and help us scale efficiently.



Your key responsibilities will include:



Lead the data agenda in your domain to drive business decisions, uncover hidden opportunities, and resolve performance bottlenecks.
Collaborate with Analytics Engineers, Data Scientists, and the Data Platform team to ensure scalable, consistent, and actionable analytics.
Analyze and optimize domain-specific processes, dashboards, and metrics for continuous improvement and clarity.
Ensure high quality and operational excellence across data models, semantic layers, and analytics delivery.
Proactively identify opportunities for advanced analytics, domain-specific algorithms, and data-driven innovations.
Promote a culture of data literacy and decision-making within the domain.


Qualifications



💡 What We’re Looking For



We’re looking for builders who move fast, think big, and care deeply about creating impact that lasts.



Your profile:



Proven experience of 5+ years in data analytics, business intelligence, or strategy, ideally embedded in product or business teams.
Solid understanding of SQL, BI tools (Tableau, Looker, Power BI, Metabase), and data modeling concepts.
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.
Strong communication and collaboration abilities; able to translate strategic goals into actionable insights.
Comfortable in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.
Data-driven, structured, and always looking for ways to improve.
Communicate well in English and French.


Additional Information



⚡ Our Recruitment Process 



We believe in a fast, transparent, and human recruitment process. 



Here’s what you can expect: 



Call with a Talent Acquisition Partner (30–45 min): Get to know each other! We’ll share more about the role, the team, and our culture.
Manager Interview (60 min): Meet your future manager to deep dive into your experience, missions, and ways of working.
Business Case: A practical exercise to showcase your analytical mindset, creativity, and strategic thinking. You’ll then have a 45-minute debrief session to discuss your approach and insights.
Onsite interview – Manager Insights & Analytics (60 min) + Chief Digital Officer (30 min) – Meet the leadership team to align on expectations, culture, and long-term impact.

📍Location : Aix en Provence or remote, France

📅 Start Date : The sooner, the better

📄 Contract Type : Full-time / Permanent 



❤️ You'll Love Joining Us



Our HQ in the South of France offers an exceptional environment - natural, cultural, and digital - on a modern and eco-responsible campus.



🌴 Prefer flexibility? We offer a hybrid model for all other positions with 3 mandatory on-site days per week plus 4 fully remote weeks per year.



🤝Put meaning back into your work and join a unique ecosystem that connects worlds often far apart: business, sports, education, and social impact, through projects like Ecole des XV, Provence Rugby, VP Green, Les Tremplins, and Chez Pierre.



💪 Forget your gym subscription! Access our large on-site fitness center morning, noon, and night - or challenge your colleagues to a padel match on our private court.



🎉 Live to the rhythm of Voyage Privé’s signature mix of business and fun: Company Breaks, Carnival, Annual Convention, meetups and talks… plus free tickets to every Provence Rugby home match and live music nights at the Dalida Institute.



✈️ And because travel is in our DNA : enjoy up to 20% off our exclusive getaway offers.



Join us and make your next career move a journey worth taking. 🌍

En el Grupo TÜV SÜD nos apasiona la tecnología. Las innovaciones transforman nuestra vida cada día y estamos comprometidos a acompañar ese progreso. Probamos, auditamos, inspeccionamos y asesoramos. Nunca dejamos de desafiarnos para garantizar la seguridad de las personas y del entorno. Vivimos la tecnología, aspiramos a la excelencia profesional y buscamos dejar una huella significativa.

Tomamos el futuro en nuestras manos. Somos el Grupo TÜV SÜD.

En TÜV SÜD nos apasiona la tecnología. Las innovaciones afectan a nuestra vida cotidiana de innumerables maneras, y nos dedicamos a formar parte de ese progreso. Probamos, auditamos, inspeccionamos y asesoramos. Nunca dejamos de desafiarnos a nosotros mismos por la seguridad de la sociedad y sus ciudadanos. Respiramos tecnología, nos esforzamos por alcanzar la excelencia profesional y dejamos huella. Tomamos el futuro en nuestras manos. Somos TÜV SÜD.

Buscamos

Un auxiliar administrativo/a de ITV, para llevar a cabo todas las tareas asignadas al puesto

Requisitos Necesarios


Grado medio Administración.- Manejo de las herramientas informáticas.
Sentido de la responsabilidad, seriedad y alta motivación para el trabajo.
Habilidades en la atención al cliente (capacidad comunicativa, empatía, don de gentes, escucha activa, etc)


Qué Ofrecemos


Contratación a través de la empresa directamente.- Puesto por sustitución- Jornada completa. 40 horas semanales.
Horario: Horarios rotativos de mañana, tarde y partida, de Lunes a Sábado.
Zona de trabajo: Puertollano.
La posibilidad de trabajar en una empresa multinacional de gran proyección internacional.


Información Adicional

¡Únete a la familia TÜV SÜD!¿Por qué trabajar con nosotros?


Porque somos una empresa de gran proyección a nivel nacional e internacional.
Con posibilidades de participar en proyectos relevantes.
Te ofrecemos formación en diferentes campos.
Una multinacional con oportunidades de promoción interna.
Y te garantizamos tu desarrollo profesional en un gran ambiente de trabajo.


En TÜV SÜD tenemos empleados de más de 100 países diferentes. Personas de diferentes orígenes, habilidades y que persiguen diferentes objetivos vitales. Nuestra fuerza proviene de estas innumerables y variadas perspectivas.

Nos comprometemos a ser un lugar de trabajo inclusivo y diverso dando la bienvenida a personas de todos los orígenes. Queremos que la Diversidad y la Inclusión (D&I) sean los cimientos de nuestra empresa y creen un entorno en el que todos nuestros empleados puedan confiar en que serán tratados con respeto, independientemente de su sexo, nacionalidad, origen étnico, fe, creencias, discapacidades, edad, orientación sexual o identidad. Por ello, se espera que nuestros empleados se comporten en todo momento de forma coherente con el Código Ético de TÜV SÜD y los valores de la empresa.

Creemos firmemente que integrar la D&I en el corazón de lo que hacemos contribuirá intrínsecamente al éxito de TÜV SÜD. Haga clic aquí para saber más sobre la Diversidad en TÜV SÜD

Title: Asesor(a) comercial - Málaga
Description:
🌟 Somos Impress 🌟

¡Creemos que todos merecen una sonrisa que les haga feliz! 😃




🏥 Somos la mayor cadena de clínicos de ortodoncia con procesos totalmente digitales de Europa. Hemos revolucionado el sector de la ortodoncia invisible con el mejor equipo y utilizando la última tecnología para el diagnóstico, tratamiento y seguimiento de todos los casos.💼




Buscamos un(a) Comercial apasionado(a) para nuestra clínica en Málaga.

¿Te unes a nosotros? 🦷




Responsabilidades 🦷😁:




Ser el rostro de Impress, saludar a los clientes, explicar nuestro producto, opciones de financiamiento y guiar a los clientes a través de los plazos y expectativas.
Cumplir con tus objetivos de ventas, asegurar que alcances tus metas de conversión de ventas y proporcionar atención al paciente de primera clase para que todos los pacientes estén emocionados de comenzar el tratamiento.
Cumplir o superar tus KPIs mensuales. Resolver problemas o cualquier inconveniente relacionado con los pacientes en colaboración con el equipo de la clínica o equipos de la sede según sea necesario.
Conectar y formar una conexión genuina con nuestros clientes.
Responder a todas las llamadas y correos electrónicos con un sentido de urgencia.
Hacer seguimiento a los clientes potenciales y mantenerlos en un pipeline para futuras ocasiones.
Automotivación y hambre por el éxito. Nunca aceptar resultados inferiores a "Impresionante"




Requisitos 🏥🤗:




+2 años de experiencia trabajando en el área comercial.
Se valora una experiencia previa en sectores como : automoción, dental, seguros, educación.
Confianza al presentar nuestro producto y abordar con precisión las preguntas e inquietudes de un cliente.




Por qué elegirnos: 🙋 ♀️🙋 ♂️




¡Formar parte de un equipo internacional y multicultural!
Un entorno de trabajo dinámico y colaborativo
Descuentos en la compra de nuestros tratamientos
Cultura positiva: ¡celebramos cada éxito!




En Impress cultivamos una cultura de inclusión y diversidad. Celebramos las fortalezas individuales de nuestros empleados, puntos de vista y experiencias y animamos a todos los candidatos a aplicar, sin distinción de raza, color, religión, identidad de género, orientación sexual, edad, origen nacional, discapacidad o cualquier otro factor.




Nuestras diferencias nos permiten ser un equipo mejor, que toma mejores decisiones, impulsa la innovación y ofrece mejores resultados.

🌈💪

Title: IT Support Konsulent
Company: Soft Supply A/S – matching IT all
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
IT Support Konsulent søges – København




Vi oplever stigende efterspørgsel og søger derfor IT Support Konsulenter til forskellige opgaver hos vores kunder i København og omegn.




Opgaverne varierer, men vil typisk være inden for 1st og 2nd line support. Du kommer til at arbejde med forskellige virksomheder og brugere, så det er en fordel, hvis du trives med variation og kan lide kontakt med mennesker.




Der er tale om en rolle, hvor du både skal kunne arbejde selvstændigt og samtidig være den person, brugerne kan regne med, når IT’en driller. Nogle dage sidder du med klassiske supportsager, andre dage er du ude hos kunder og hjælper med opsætning, fejlsøgning eller onboarding af nyt udstyr.




Opgaver kan bl.a. være:

• Bruger- og supportsager (1st/2nd line)

• Fejlsøgning på hardware og software

• Klargøring og opsætning af PC’er og udstyr

• Onsite support hos kunder rundt omkring i København

• Installation af arbejdspladser, printere og øvrigt IT-udstyr

• Oprettelse af brugere, adgangsrettigheder og generel support

• Dialog med brugere og håndtering af IT-relaterede udfordringer




Vi forestiller os, at du:

• Har min. 5 års erhvervserfaring med IT-support

• Er serviceminded og god til at tale med brugere

• Arbejder selvstændigt og tager ansvar

• Er mødestabil og trives i en rolle med forskellige opgaver

• Har et godt overblik og kan bevare roen, når der er travlt

• Rollen kræver, at du kan kommunikere flydende på dansk – både mundtligt og skriftligt




Vi tilbyder spændende opgaver hos forskellige kunder, hvor du får mulighed for at arbejde i forskellige IT-miljøer og være en del af både kortere og længerevarende opgaver.




Hvis det lyder som noget for dig, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte Marc Simonsen for yderligere information. Alle henvendelser behandles fortroligt, og vi venter gerne på den rette kandidat.

Title: Asistente Financiero
HTML:
Estamos a crescer e queremos crescer contigo!

Procuramos pessoas com ambição, atitude positiva e vontade de fazer a diferença. Se valorizas soluções em vez de problemas e queres fazer parte ativa do desenvolvimento de um projeto em expansão, vem conhecer-nos. Acreditamos que o teu sucesso é o nosso sucesso – e vice-versa.


Principais funções e responsabilidades:


Organização, classificação e lançamento de documentos contabilísticos na área logística, com integração na contabilidade;


Reconciliações bancárias;


Apoio na elaboração de relatórios mensais e semanais para a administração;


Gestão e acompanhamento de contas correntes de fornecedores e clientes;


Preparação de listas de pagamentos;


Apoio nos inventários mensais;


Colaboração em tarefas administrativas nas áreas de receção, financeira e recursos humanos;


Arquivo e conferência de dados contabilísticos.


Perfil pretendido:


Experiência mínima de 1 a 2 anos nas áreas de contabilidade ou finanças;


Valorizamos conhecimentos do software Primavera;


Bons conhecimentos de Microsoft Office;


Conhecimentos de inglês (falado e escrito);


Capacidade de análise, planeamento e organização;


Disponibilidade imediata.


Se procuras um novo desafio e queres evoluir profissionalmente, envia o teu CV para belinda@staunchltd.com

Title: Técnico/a de Producción Editorial
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
¿Te gustaría formar parte de un proyecto innovador con impacto social? ¡En Innovamat estamos buscando a alguien como tú! 😎

Sobre nosotros 🚀

Innovamat es una organización educativa centrada en el aprendizaje de las matemáticas. Su propósito es contribuir a mejorar cómo se aprende esta ciencia, ofreciendo recursos, formación e investigación a la comunidad educativa.

Fundada en 2017 en Barcelona, Innovamat cuenta con 27 000 docentes de más de 2 600 centros educativos de España, Estados Unidos, México, Italia, Chile, Ecuador, Colombia, Brasil y Perú, que hacen que más de 600 000 alumnos aprendan matemáticas fomentando la comprensión profunda y el desarrollo competencial.

Tu misión 🎯

Formarás parte del equipo de Producción dentro del departamento de Supply Chain de Innovamat. Tu misión será asegurar la correcta implementación de nuestros materiales y procesos en todos los proyectos. Serás una pieza clave en la coordinación operativa, garantizando que las entregas se realicen con calidad, en tiempo y forma. Concretamente en el cálculo de costes de producción y serás el contacto directo con nuestros proveedores. Además, colaborarás estrechamente con equipos internos y externos para anticiparte a posibles retos y proponer soluciones que optimicen la producción.

Tu día a día en Innovamat 🏁


Coordinar y supervisar el ciclo de producción de materiales y recursos, asegurando su disponibilidad y calidad.
Detectar necesidades operativas y transformar los retos en soluciones efectivas.
Trabajar estrechamente con equipos internos (Didáctica, Contenido, Operaciones, Tech…) para garantizar la coherencia y excelencia del producto final.
Interpretar y documentar requerimientos de producción en nuestras herramientas de gestión (por ejemplo, Airtable, Google Sheets).
Hacer seguimiento de los KPIs de producción y proponer mejoras para optimizar procesos.
Participar en reuniones de coordinación con diferentes áreas, garantizando la alineación en plazos y calidad de entregables.
Contribuir a la mejora continua de procesos, aportando ideas y propuestas que refuercen la eficiencia del equipo.
Participar en formaciones internas sobre metodologías de trabajo, herramientas de gestión y sector educativo.
Formarás parte de un equipo ágil, caracterizado por la corresponsabilidad, la colaboración y la orientación a resultados.


Qué buscamos en ti 🔍


Experiencia previa en producción editorial, digital o de materiales educativos.
Pasión por la organización, la eficiencia y la mejora de procesos.
Dinamismo y proactividad en la resolución de problemas, con capacidad de aprendizaje y adaptación.
Visión estratégica para entender el impacto de la producción en el conjunto de la compañía.
Capacidad de liderazgo transversal y coordinación con distintos equipos.
Buenas habilidades de comunicación, documentación y cálculo de costes.
Ganas de trabajar en equipo, entusiasmo y curiosidad.
Capacidad para desenvolverte bien en entornos de presión y con plazos ajustados.
Ser sistemático/a y ordenado/a para mantener actualizadas nuestras herramientas de gestión.


Será genial si además… 💫


Has trabajado en startups o entornos dinámicos.
Manejas herramientas de gestión de proyectos (Airtable, Google Sheets).


Qué ofrecemos 🤗

We aim high: Nos marcamos objetivos ambiciosos y trabajamos con determinación y pasión por transformar la educación. Aquí podrás crecer tanto como quieras, rodeado de personas que te impulsan, te inspiran y te ayudan a dar siempre tu mejor versión.

We are owners: Desde el primer día tendrás impacto real. No venimos solo a ejecutar, sino a pensar, decidir y construir. Confiamos en ti para tomar la iniciativa, proponer ideas y ver cómo se convierten en realidad.

We move fast: Trabajamos en un entorno dinámico donde la agilidad y la adaptabilidad marcan la diferencia. Sentirás el ritmo, la energía y la emoción de aprender rápido, actuar con criterio y hacer que las cosas pasen.

We are eager to learn: Somos curiosos, nos cuestionamos y compartimos lo que aprendemos. Cada reto es una oportunidad para mejorar y seguir creciendo, tanto a nivel individual como en equipo.

We are humble: Fomentamos una cultura de confianza y feedback donde todos tenemos voz. Escuchamos activamente, colaboramos y valoramos las ideas por su impacto. Aquí encontrarás un equipo siempre dispuesto a ayudar, compartir y celebrar los logros juntos.

¿Te gustaría formar parte de un proyecto innovador con impacto social? ¡En Innovamat estamos buscando a alguien como tú! 😎

Sobre nosotros 🚀

Innovamat es una organización educativa centrada en el aprendizaje de las matemáticas. Su propósito es contribuir a mejorar cómo se aprende esta ciencia, ofreciendo recursos, formación e investigación a la comunidad educativa.

Fundada en 2017 en Barcelona, Innovamat cuenta con 27 000 docentes de más de 2 600 centros educativos de España, Estados Unidos, México, Italia, Chile, Ecuador, Colombia, Brasil y Perú, que hacen que más de 600 000 alumnos aprendan matemáticas fomentando la comprensión profunda y el desarrollo competencial.

Tu misión 🎯

Formarás parte del equipo de Producción dentro del departamento de Supply Chain de Innovamat. Tu misión será asegurar la correcta implementación de nuestros materiales y procesos en todos los proyectos. Serás una pieza clave en la coordinación operativa, garantizando que las entregas se realicen con calidad, en tiempo y forma. Concretamente en el cálculo de costes de producción y serás el contacto directo con nuestros proveedores. Además, colaborarás estrechamente con equipos internos y externos para anticiparte a posibles retos y proponer soluciones que optimicen la producción.

Tu día a día en Innovamat 🏁

  • Coordinar y supervisar el ciclo de producción de materiales y recursos, asegurando su disponibilidad y calidad.
  • Detectar necesidades operativas y transformar los retos en soluciones efectivas.
  • Trabajar estrechamente con equipos internos (Didáctica, Contenido, Operaciones, Tech…) para garantizar la coherencia y excelencia del producto final.
  • Interpretar y documentar requerimientos de producción en nuestras herramientas de gestión (por ejemplo, Airtable, Google Sheets).
  • Hacer seguimiento de los KPIs de producción y proponer mejoras para optimizar procesos.
  • Participar en reuniones de coordinación con diferentes áreas, garantizando la alineación en plazos y calidad de entregables.
  • Contribuir a la mejora continua de procesos, aportando ideas y propuestas que refuercen la eficiencia del equipo.
  • Participar en formaciones internas sobre metodologías de trabajo, herramientas de gestión y sector educativo.
  • Formarás parte de un equipo ágil, caracterizado por la corresponsabilidad, la colaboración y la orientación a resultados.

Qué buscamos en ti 🔍

  • Experiencia previa en producción editorial, digital o de materiales educativos.
  • Pasión por la organización, la eficiencia y la mejora de procesos.
  • Dinamismo y proactividad en la resolución de problemas, con capacidad de aprendizaje y adaptación.
  • Visión estratégica para entender el impacto de la producción en el conjunto de la compañía.
  • Capacidad de liderazgo transversal y coordinación con distintos equipos.
  • Buenas habilidades de comunicación, documentación y cálculo de costes.
  • Ganas de trabajar en equipo, entusiasmo y curiosidad.
  • Capacidad para desenvolverte bien en entornos de presión y con plazos ajustados.
  • Ser sistemático/a y ordenado/a para mantener actualizadas nuestras herramientas de gestión.

Será genial si además… 💫

  • Has trabajado en startups o entornos dinámicos.
  • Manejas herramientas de gestión de proyectos (Airtable, Google Sheets).

Qué ofrecemos 🤗

We aim high: Nos marcamos objetivos ambiciosos y trabajamos con determinación y pasión por transformar la educación. Aquí podrás crecer tanto como quieras, rodeado de personas que te impulsan, te inspiran y te ayudan a dar siempre tu mejor versión.

We are owners: Desde el primer día tendrás impacto real. No venimos solo a ejecutar, sino a pensar, decidir y construir. Confiamos en ti para tomar la iniciativa, proponer ideas y ver cómo se convierten en realidad.

We move fast: Trabajamos en un entorno dinámico donde la agilidad y la adaptabilidad marcan la diferencia. Sentirás el ritmo, la energía y la emoción de aprender rápido, actuar con criterio y hacer que las cosas pasen.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Boston VA Research Institute, Inc. (BVARI)




We are a Boston-based epidemiology and biomedical research organization. We are actively engaged in several clinical trials, pharmacoepidemiology, chronic disease epidemiology, genetic epidemiology, biostatistical and genomics research. Our research team oversees study data-related activities and quality improvement initiatives to improve the way translational research is conducted and clinical care is delivered. The multi-disciplinary center consists of a highly talented group of biomedical researchers, clinicians, epidemiologists, biostatisticians, data analysts/programmers, project coordinators, and informatics team with academic affiliations to Harvard Medical School and Boston University. We are looking for candidates with a service-oriented mentality, high sense of ownership of assigned tasks and the ability to solve complex data-related problems.




Project Background:

This role is involved in managing data aspects of large nationwide randomized clinical trials as well as integrating electronic health record (EHR) data with the Million Veteran Program (MVP) data to build cohorts for clinical and genetic research, enabling robust investigation of complex health outcomes in Veterans.




General Statement of Duties:

The Data Analyst will work with the study teams to gather study data requirements, pull all necessary data from electronic health records (EHR), and perform data cleaning, and aggregation/synthesis as appropriate. The Data Analyst will a) generate recruitment lists to the enrollment sites of national clinical trials and perform quality assurance on the data generated, b) develop reports as needed for the Principal Investigator and other project staff as needed, c) perform validation of programs and analytical databases to fulfill specifications and ensure integrity of outcomes, d) turn in assignments in a timely manner, meeting appropriate timelines, e) establish and maintain communication with customers to keep them updated on status of their requests.




Essential Duties and Responsibilities:




Mainly responsible for constructing, cleaning, validating, modifying, and analyzing biomedical research data
Query and extract large-scale EHR and MVP data using SQL
Generate reports and visualizations using tools such as R and Python to effectively communicate findings
Collaborate with biostatisticians, clinicians, epidemiologists, data management team and data analysts/programmers to design, create and deliver analytical databases, reports and analyses
Perform validation of programs and analytical databases to fulfill specifications and ensure integrity of outcomes
Establish and maintain communication with customers to keep them updated on status of their requests
Provide statistical expertise towards research projects and conduct statistical analysis using SAS or R
Proactively identify data issues, troubleshoot problems, and implement solutions with minimal supervision
Establish and maintain communication with study team to keep them updated on status of requests
Turn in assignments in a timely manner, meeting appropriate timelines.




QUALIFICATIONS AND ENTRANCE REQUIREMENTS:

Master’s degree in public health domain, statistics, biostatistics or epidemiology, data science, or a related field with 2+ years of statistical programming experience; OR Bachelor’s degree in a related field with 5+ years of programming experience.
Strong programming and analytical skills, with demonstrated proficiency in SQL and at least one statistical programming language (R, Python, or SAS)
Hands-on experience with Microsoft SQL Server, including working with relational datasets, managing and structuring large observational datasets, and writing efficient complex SQL queries for data extraction, creating cohorts, data transformation, and analysis.
Experience in data visualization and reporting using R or Python.
Basic knowledge of study design and research methodology.
Capable of working independently in writing analytic plans, performing analyses and writing reports to facilitate research decision-making.
Demonstrated ability to think algorithmically and approach problems with a strong coding and problem-solving mindset.
Understanding of the healthcare domain, clinical, epidemiologic and/or genomic data, and clinical trials research is a plus.
Natural Language Processing experience is a plus.
Excellent attention to detail, logic, and organizational skills.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.




The employee must be a resident of Massachusetts upon the start date.




Please note we are unable to provide work authorization and/or visa sponsorship.




Work Environment

This job operates in a professional office environment. This role routinely uses standard office equipment such as computers, phones, photocopiers, filing cabinets and fax machines.




Physical Demands

The physical demands described in this job description are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.




While performing the duties of this job, the employee frequently is required to stand; walk; use hands; and reach with hands and arms.




Selective Service

If you are male application born after December 31, 1959, you must certify that you have registered with the Selective Service System or are exempt from having to do so under the Selective Service Law.




This position requires the ability to occasionally lift office products and supplies, up to 20 pounds.




Position Type/Expected Hours of Work

This is a full-time position, and hours of work and days are Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m.




Travel

No travel is required for this position. Optional travel to scientific meetings may occur from time to time.




Work Authorization/Security Clearance

An employee in this position must complete all appropriate background checks at the time of hire and periodic reappointment.




Other Duties

Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.




Equal Opportunity Employer

Boston VA Research Institute provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, transfer, leaves of absences, compensation, and training.




Pay Range

Minimum $70,000.00, Midpoint $80,000, Maximum $90,000.00

Salary is based on related experience, expertise, and internal equity; generally, new hires can expect pay between the minimum and midpoint of the range.

Title: SQL Data Analyst
Description:
Department: Finance

Employment Type: Permanent

Location: Burlington, Massachusetts

Compensation: $100,000 - $110,000 / year

Description

Highwire, a Veriforce company, is the Contractor Success Platform for asset owners, general contractors, and facilities managers. The world’s most admired organizations use Highwire to ensure their contractors and subcontractors deliver great work, on budget and on schedule, by collaborating with them to dynamically mitigate safety, financial, quality, and sustainability risks during capital projects and facilities maintenance.

To support our growth, we’re looking for a technically minded, detail-obsessed Data Analyst to own the integrity of our internal data. This role is perfect for someone who’s a SQL expert and eager to work directly with complex datasets.

Your mission is to ensure the data that powers Highwire — from customer renewal rates and campaign performance to financial reporting — is accurate, complete, and decision-ready. You’ll take ownership of the end-to-end accuracy of key datasets, using SQL to investigate anomalies, validate transformations, and deliver reliable data to teams across Product, Engineering, Finance, and other business functions. Your work will enable the entire company to move quickly and make informed decisions based on trustworthy data.

What You'll Do


Write and improve SQL queries that power reporting and analysis. You’ll support business teams by extracting and transforming data, building reusable queries, and collaborating with engineering to enhance data models and pipelines.
Deliver clean, trusted data. You’ll ensure that the data used across Finance, Product, Sales, and Engineering is accurate and reliable, enabling fast and confident decision-making and reducing rework and second-guessing.
Identify and fix root-cause issues. You’ll proactively identify bugs, inconsistencies, or oddities in the data that powers our reports, and collaborate with Engineering and Product to determine the root cause of the issue.
Enable real-time reporting. Department heads will rely on you for recurring and ad-hoc reports that are both timely and accurate. You’ll build reports that scale as our teams grow.
Continuously improve data quality. You won’t wait for others to flag data issues. Instead, you’ll take initiative to audit, investigate, and improve datasets before problems reach stakeholders.


Who You Are


SQL Expert. You’re comfortable querying relational databases, navigating schemas, writing joins, and thinking in data logic.
Detail-oriented. You notice inconsistencies that others miss and take pride in spotting the one broken row in a dataset of thousands.
Owner’s mindset. You feel a strong sense of responsibility for the quality of your work and the integrity of the data others rely on. If it’s broken, you’ll fix it.
Adaptable. You’re comfortable working with imperfect or incomplete data, and you adjust quickly to changing product requirements or business needs.
Analytical. You excel at analyzing large datasets, identifying patterns and anomalies, and transforming complex or ambiguous information into actionable insights.
Proactive collaborator. You don’t wait for someone else to raise a flag; you pull in the right people to get the right answer, fast.


This is an ideal role for someone who is confident in writing SQL to explore, transform, and troubleshoot data in a relational database. You might come from a background in data analysis, analytics engineering, operational support, or even Excel/Sheets-heavy reporting work, but the key is that you’ve used SQL hands-on, and you’re excited to deepen that skill and partner closely with leaders across our organization to ensure our data is accurate, accessible, and trustworthy.

Attention to detail is a critical competency for success in this Data Analyst role. To help us identify candidates who read thoroughly, you’ll see a mystery question during the application process. The correct answer is: attention to detail.

What You’ll Get In Return

Our benefits go well beyond the basics. To support your work-life integration, we built a benefits program that takes care of the whole person, including physical, mental, financial, and emotional health.


Medical, Dental, & Vision Insurance. We offer health, dental, and vision insurance to all full-time employees. Best part? You’ll be eligible on day one.
401k. We offer a 401k and a 3% company contribution (even better than a match) to help you get ready to retire no matter how far off that seems.
Parental leave. We take care of new parents by offering paid time off for births, adoptions, and fostering.
Paid Time Off. Outstanding time off benefits.
Mentorship. Our office-based culture fosters meaningful learning, growth, and mentorship for our team.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role you need a very fine eye for detail, experience as a data analyst, and a deep understanding of the popular data analysis tools and databases. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.

Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

RTK Tickets is a dynamic and rapidly expanding company at the intersection of entertainment, technology, and trading. From buzzer-beaters and sold-out stadiums to front-row concerts, we provide a comprehensive platform for buying, selling, and optimizing access to live events — and increasingly, other high-demand assets.




We’re a fast-moving, data-driven brokerage redefining how markets are priced, traded, and optimized across entertainment and emerging asset classes. What began as a two-person idea has evolved into a 20-person team built on passion, precision, and performance.




Now, we’re looking for a Data Analyst to help us build and scale the data infrastructure and predictive models that power how we buy and sell tickets. If you’re excited by real-time markets, complex datasets, and turning raw data into measurable outcomes, you could be a great fit for our team.




What You’ll Do

As a Data Analyst at RTK Tickets, you’ll own the systems and models that drive our pricing decisions. You’ll design and maintain data pipelines that ingest market data from dozens of sources, build predictive models that forecast demand, and work closely with our risk and operations teams to turn insights into actions.

You’ll have end-to-end ownership; from raw data ingestion to production-ready models. Your work will directly influence how we price, buy, and sell across thousands of live events.







Your Day-to-Day

Design, build, and maintain data pipelines that ingest and process transaction and listing data from external marketplaces
Develop and refine predictive models that forecast demand, identify pricing inefficiencies, and optimize offers
Implement data quality frameworks and monitoring systems to ensure fresh, accurate, and reliable data
Build and manage data warehouse pipelines and modeling (ETL/ELT) for large-scale datasets
Collaborate with the risk and operations teams to translate model outputs into actionable decisions
Partner with engineers to integrate pipelines with CI/CD processes and automate data workflows
Present your findings clearly to both technical and non-technical teammates







What You Bring

Required:

1-3 years of experience in data science, data engineering, or a closely related analytical role
Strong Proficiency in Python - including experience with data processing libraries
Solid SQL skills for data analysis, transformation, and pipeline work
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, startup-like environment where you take ownership and adapt quickly
Clear communication skills — able to explain complex ideas simply and confidently
Excitement about AI, automation, and cutting-edge technology

Nice to Have:

Exposure to machine learning model deployment or MLOps practices
Interest in live event markets, trading markets, sports analytics, or alternative asset pricing
Experience with LLM-based automation tools for data extraction or enrichment







Why You’ll Love It Here

At RTK Tickets, you’ll gain hands-on experience at the intersection of data, product, and live entertainment. Your pipelines and models will directly influence how we price and trade across thousands of live events every week.

If you want to grow your career at the intersection of data, markets, and live entertainment, we’d love to hear from you.







Interview Process

Our interview process includes several stages designed to assess both technical skills and team fit. Candidates should be prepared to meet with the hiring team for introductory conversations, followed by a Python-based take-home exercise focused on data ingestion, pipeline design, and predictive modeling - mirroring the kind of end-to-end work this role owns. Candidates will walk through their solution with the team before final interviews with the hiring manager and senior leadership.




Compensation & Benefits

Competitive salary with opportunity for performance-based bonuses
Health and Dental insurance
401(k) retirement plan with company matching
Tickets to select live events

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow.

Are you a student looking to make extra money this summer while gaining valuable work experience? Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow. Our internship program offers practical corporate work experience that could lead to full-time work after graduation. We are seeking students going into their senior year, graduating in the spring of 2027.

The shift is from 9 am to 5 pm, 40 hours per week, starting in June and ending in August. This is a hybrid position in Newton, MA that pays $20 per hour.

The Data Analyst Intern will support the Retirement Operations team by helping transform data into meaningful insights that inform business decisions. This internship is designed to provide hands‑on experience working with real‑world datasets, analytics tools, and business partners while building foundational skills in data analysis, reporting, and data storytelling. The intern will work closely with experienced analysts and stakeholders and receive mentorship throughout the program.

Key Responsibilities


Assist in collecting, cleaning, and validating data from internal and external sources to ensure data quality and accuracy
Support analysis of datasets to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for operational or business improvement
Help build and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations used by leadership and cross‑functional teams
Partner with analysts and business stakeholders to understand data needs and help define metrics and KPIs
Contribute to the development and documentation of data models, data dictionaries, and standard reporting definitions
Perform ad hoc analyses to support business questions and ongoing initiatives
Assist with tracking performance metrics and supporting regular reporting cycles
Collaborate with data engineering, IT, and analytics teams to learn how data pipelines and infrastructure are managed
Follow established data governance, privacy, and security standards


Skills & Qualifications

Required:


Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field (or recent graduate)
Strong interest in data analysis, analytics, or business intelligence
Exposure to SQL or relational databases through coursework, projects, or internships
Familiarity with Excel (basic to intermediate formulas, pivot tables, data analysis concepts)
Ability to analyze data, think critically, and solve problems
Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn
Ability to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences


Preferred:


Exposure to data visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Alteryx
Coursework or project experience using Python or R for data analysis
Familiarity with basic data modeling or data warehousing concepts
Interest in financial services, retirement plans, or a highly metrics‑driven industry
Experience working on academic, personal, or team data projects


Core Competencies


Analytical and quantitative thinking
Curiosity and eagerness to learn
Data visualization and storytelling fundamentals
Collaboration and communication skills
Time management and ability to balance multiple priorities
Growth mindset and openness to feedback


Be aware of employment fraud. All email communications from Ascensus or its hiring managers originate from @ascensus.com or @futureplan.com email addresses. We will never ask you for payment or require you to purchase any equipment. If you are suspicious or unsure about validity of a job posting, we strongly encourage you to apply directly through our website.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Our client is seeking an experienced data analyst to support our Digital Transformation program under the Workday Data workstream.

This is engagement is focused on data analysis, cleansing, profiling, and definition work.

We are looking for someone with a blend of hands-on data skills, strong business acumen, and the ability to engage effectively with cross-functional stakeholders across IT, Data, and Business teams.

Location: Boston area - ability to get to the Boston office when needed.




Keys to this role:

Perform data analysis, cleansing, and mapping across source systems to support rationalization and data cleansing efforts
Conduct data quality analysis on transactional and master data to identify critical data improvement opportunities
Facilitate working sessions with business and technical stakeholders to inform data analysis work
Support content creation for data governance documentation and working group meetings
Propose and apply approved AI tools and methodologies to accelerate data conversion and analysis efforts

Strong business analysis and data analysis capabilities
Demonstrated experience with data quality, cleansing, mapping, and governance processes
Advanced SQL skills
Proficiency with Google Big Query and Looker preferred
Comfortable working with AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, and/or Claude) to perform traditional data analysis responsibilities
Comfort operating in a collaborative, cross-functional environment with both technical and non-technical audiences

Strong Nice to Haves:

Prior experience with Workday Finance and HCM modules
Experience working with financial data; chart of accounts, trial balances, and p&l data preferred

Title: Data Analyst, Reporting & Analytics
Description:
About Us

National Grid is hiring a Data Analyst (Management Data Analysis), for our Data Analysis and Reporting department in Upstate, NY / Downstate, NY or Waltham, NE.

Everyday we deliver safe and secure energy to homes, communities, and businesses. We are there when people need us the most. We connect people to the energy they need for the lives they live. The pace of change in society and our industry is accelerating and our expertise and track record puts us in an unparalleled position to shape the sustainable future of our industry.

To be successful we must anticipate the needs of our customers, reducing the cost of energy delivery today and pioneering the flexible energy systems of tomorrow. This requires us to deliver on our promises and always look for new opportunities to grow, both ourselves and our business

Job Purpose

The Reporting Analyst plays a critical role in transforming raw enterprise data into trusted, actionable insights that drive operational, financial, and strategic decision‑making. This role is responsible for the full reporting lifecycle — from gathering reporting specifications, validating source‑of‑record datasets, and assessing data quality, to designing intuitive visualizations and maintaining an accurate reporting catalog.

This role also functions as the Product Owner for the Snowflake curated data layer, owning prioritization, acceptance criteria, and delivery of features that supported reliable, consumable data assets.

You will partner closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, data governance, and product teams to ensure reporting assets are accurate, compliant, scalable, and aligned with enterprise data management principles. This role requires strong analytical rigor, technical fluency, and a commitment to continuous learning in modern reporting and data engineering tools.

Key Accountabilities

Product Owner — Curated Data Layer (Snowflake)


Act as Product Owner for the Snowflake curated data layer, owning the product vision, roadmap, and backlog to ensure delivery of high-value, consumable data assets.
Engage business and technical stakeholders to capture needs, translate them into clear user stories and acceptance criteria, and define success metrics and adoption goals.
Prioritize features and enhancements based on business value, dependencies, and risk; maintain a groomed backlog and release plan.
Define and document data contracts, acceptance criteria, SLAs and release acceptance requirements in alignment with enterprise standards; ensure items are catalogued for traceability and audit readiness.
Coordinate work across data engineering, QA, analytics, and data governance to ensure correct implementation, testing, lineage, and production readiness.
Monitor product performance and stakeholder feedback, iterate on requirements and backlog, and remove blockers or escalate impediments as needed.


Reporting Requirements & Specification Management


Engage business stakeholders to capture reporting requirements, KPIs, definitions, and intended decision pathways.
Translate business needs into clear, structured reporting specifications including data sources, logic, filters, metrics, and visualization expectations.
Document specifications in alignment with enterprise standards and ensure they are cataloged for traceability and audit readiness.
Partner with data governance to validate metric definitions, business rules, and approved sources of record.
Optimize report performance, usability, and accessibility for diverse business audiences.
Develop reusable semantic models, datasets, and dataflows to support scalable self‑service analytics.


Data Acquisition, Validation & Quality Assessment


Collaborate with data engineering teams to identify, validate, and acquire the correct source‑of‑record datasets from Snowflake, Azure, Databricks, Salesforce, Maximo, and other enterprise platforms.
Perform data profiling, lineage validation, and quality checks using tools such as Informatica IDQ/EDC, Collibra, Kingsway Soft, SSIS, or Matillion.
Investigate anomalies, reconcile discrepancies, and escalate data quality issues with clear root‑cause analysis.
Ensure all datasets used for reporting adhere to enterprise data management and governance standards.


Reporting Catalog Stewardship


Maintain an accurate, up‑to‑date reporting catalog including report purpose, owners, refresh cadence, data sources, and business definitions.
Ensure catalog entries are complete, relevant, and written in business‑friendly language.
Partner with data governance to ensure catalog metadata aligns with enterprise standards and lineage requirements.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Engineering, Finance, or related field.
3–7 years of experience in reporting, analytics, or data management roles.
Strong proficiency in SQL (Snowflake SQL, T‑SQL, or equivalent).
Experience with Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar BI tools.
Familiarity with Snowflake, Azure Data Services, Databricks, or equivalent cloud data platforms.
Experience with data quality and metadata tools (Informatica IDQ/EDC, Collibra).
Ability to interpret business requirements and translate them into technical specifications.
Strong understanding of data modeling, KPI design, and visualization best practices.
Ability to investigate data anomalies and perform root‑cause analysis.
Excellent communication skills with both technical and business audiences.
Experience working in Agile environments (Jira, Confluence).


More Information

Salary

Upstate, NY $75,000 - $88,000

New England $85,000 - $99,000

Downstate, NY $91,000 - 106,000 a year

National Grid utilizes an assessment that evaluates the job qualifications/characteristics using AI or statistically based scoring. For more information, please view NYC Local Law 144.

This position has a career path which provides for advancement opportunities within and across bands as you develop and evolve in the position; gaining experience, expertise and acquiring and applying technical skills. Candidates will be assessed and provided offers against the minimum qualifications of this role and their individual experience.

National Grid is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment regardless of protected class. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable federal, state, and local laws. National Grid maintains affirmative action programs for individuals with disabilities and protected veterans.

Our employment practices are designed to ensure that all individuals are treated fairly and with respect throughout the hiring process and during employment. National Grid complies with all applicable federal, state, and local anti-discrimination laws. We are dedicated to fostering a workplace that is free from unlawful discrimination and harassment, and we encourage a culture of respect for all.

To be successful we must anticipate the needs of our customers, reducing the cost of energy delivery today and pioneering the flexible energy systems of tomorrow. This requires us to deliver on our promises and always look for new opportunities to grow, both ourselves and our business

Job Purpose

The Reporting Analyst plays a critical role in transforming raw enterprise data into trusted, actionable insights that drive operational, financial, and strategic decision‑making. This role is responsible for the full reporting lifecycle — from gathering reporting specifications, validating source‑of‑record datasets, and assessing data quality, to designing intuitive visualizations and maintaining an accurate reporting catalog.

This role also functions as the Product Owner for the Snowflake curated data layer, owning prioritization, acceptance criteria, and delivery of features that supported reliable, consumable data assets.

You will partner closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, data governance, and product teams to ensure reporting assets are accurate, compliant, scalable, and aligned with enterprise data management principles. This role requires strong analytical rigor, technical fluency, and a commitment to continuous learning in modern reporting and data engineering tools.

Key Accountabilities

Product Owner — Curated Data Layer (Snowflake)

  • Act as Product Owner for the Snowflake curated data layer, owning the product vision, roadmap, and backlog to ensure delivery of high-value, consumable data assets.
  • Engage business and technical stakeholders to capture needs, translate them into clear user stories and acceptance criteria, and define success metrics and adoption goals.
  • Prioritize features and enhancements based on business value, dependencies, and risk; maintain a groomed backlog and release plan.
  • Define and document data contracts, acceptance criteria, SLAs and release acceptance requirements in alignment with enterprise standards; ensure items are catalogued for traceability and audit readiness.
  • Coordinate work across data engineering, QA, analytics, and data governance to ensure correct implementation, testing, lineage, and production readiness.
  • Monitor product performance and stakeholder feedback, iterate on requirements and backlog, and remove blockers or escalate impediments as needed.

Reporting Requirements & Specification Management

  • Engage business stakeholders to capture reporting requirements, KPIs, definitions, and intended decision pathways.
  • Translate business needs into clear, structured reporting specifications including data sources, logic, filters, metrics, and visualization expectations.
  • Document specifications in alignment with enterprise standards and ensure they are cataloged for traceability and audit readiness.
  • Partner with data governance to validate metric definitions, business rules, and approved sources of record.
  • Optimize report performance, usability, and accessibility for diverse business audiences.
  • Develop reusable semantic models, datasets, and dataflows to support scalable self‑service analytics.

Data Acquisition, Validation & Quality Assessment

  • Collaborate with data engineering teams to identify, validate, and acquire the correct source‑of‑record datasets from Snowflake, Azure, Databricks, Salesforce, Maximo, and other enterprise platforms.
  • Perform data profiling, lineage validation, and quality checks using tools such as Informatica IDQ/EDC, Collibra, Kingsway Soft, SSIS, or Matillion.
  • Investigate anomalies, reconcile discrepancies, and escalate data quality issues with clear root‑cause analysis.
  • Ensure all datasets used for reporting adhere to enterprise data management and governance standards.

Reporting Catalog Stewardship

  • Maintain an accurate, up‑to‑date reporting catalog including report purpose, owners, refresh cadence, data sources, and business definitions.
  • Ensure catalog entries are complete, relevant, and written in business‑friendly language.
  • Partner with data governance to ensure catalog metadata aligns with enterprise standards and lineage requirements.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Engineering, Finance, or related field.
  • 3–7 years of experience in reporting, analytics, or data management roles.
  • Strong proficiency in SQL (Snowflake SQL, T‑SQL, or equivalent).
  • Experience with Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar BI tools.
  • Familiarity with Snowflake, Azure Data Services, Databricks, or equivalent cloud data platforms.
  • Experience with data quality and metadata tools (Informatica IDQ/EDC, Collibra).
  • Ability to interpret business requirements and translate them into technical specifications.
  • Strong understanding of data modeling, KPI design, and visualization best practices.
  • Ability to investigate data anomalies and perform root‑cause analysis.
  • Excellent communication skills with both technical and business audiences.
  • Experience working in Agile environments (Jira, Confluence).

More Information

Salary

Upstate, NY $75,000 - $88,000

New England $85,000 - $99,000

Downstate, NY $91,000 - 106,000 a year

National Grid utilizes an assessment that evaluates the job qualifications/characteristics using AI or statistically based scoring. For more information, please view NYC Local Law 144.

This position has a career path which provides for advancement opportunities within and across bands as you develop and evolve in the position; gaining experience, expertise and acquiring and applying technical skills. Candidates will be assessed and provided offers against the minimum qualifications of this role and their individual experience.

Our employment practices are designed to ensure that all individuals are treated fairly and with respect throughout the hiring process and during employment. National Grid complies with all applicable federal, state, and local anti-discrimination laws. We are dedicated to fostering a workplace that is free from unlawful discrimination and harassment, and we encourage a culture of respect for all.

Title: Lead Data Analyst
Description:
We’re partnered with a fast-scaling healthcare technology company building modern infrastructure that improves healthcare delivery and patient outcomes at scale.




They’re now looking for a Senior Healthcare Data Analyst to help customers unlock the full value of healthcare data while shaping how trusted, high-quality data products are delivered across the platform.




This role sits at the intersection of healthcare analytics, data quality, customer partnership, and modern AI-enabled operations, helping customers understand, trust, and operationalize complex clinical and claims data at scale.




What's on offer




Total cash comp up to $185k
Remote-first environment with strong flexibility
Opportunity to influence how healthcare data is interpreted and trusted at scale
High-impact role within a mission-driven healthcare technology business
Collaborative culture investing in AI adoption and modern ways of working
Exposure to senior leadership and strategic customer initiatives




The Mandate

Partner directly with customers to solve complex healthcare data and platform challenges
Build trust in the platform by explaining data transformations, logic, and quality outcomes
Collaborate with technical stakeholders to develop advanced customisations and solutions
Contribute to the evolution of a best-in-class validation framework across healthcare datasets
Act as an internal subject matter expert across claims, clinical, and operational healthcare data flows
Drive improvements in data reliability, quality, and customer confidence
Apply AI tooling to improve efficiency, organization, and delivery across multiple initiatives
Translate technical complexity into clear business and operational outcomes




What We’re Looking For

5+ years of experience working with healthcare data environments
Strong knowledge of clinical data models and overall healthcare data ecosystems
Deep understanding of healthcare data standards and feeds including FHIR, HL7, CCD, and full extracts
Familiarity with healthcare terminology including ICD, SNOMED, LOINC, RxNorm, and related frameworks
Experience working with value-based care models and healthcare performance measurement
Strong analytical thinking and structured problem-solving ability
Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly to varied audiences
Experience managing multiple priorities while leveraging AI tools to improve productivity and execution
Genuine interest in adopting AI-enabled workflows and operational practices




Bonus

Advanced SQL capability with experience working across complex datasets and environments
Experience working within health systems, ACOs, CINs, payer organizations, or insurers
Experience working within healthcare technology vendors
Exposure to healthcare data validation, governance, or platform operations environments

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
About The Role

The Business Data Analyst bridges the gap between raw data infrastructure and strategic decision-making. This role focuses on transforming complex datasets from various business systems into actionable insights that drive revenue growth and operational efficiency.

Working within a modern data stack, the position involves partnering with product, finance, and marketing teams to define core KPIs, build automated reporting, and perform deep-dive exploratory analysis. The role is critical for ensuring data integrity and providing the analytical narrative behind the company's performance metrics.

Key Responsibilities


Develop and maintain complex SQL queries and dbt models to transform raw data into clean, analysis-ready tables in Snowflake or BigQuery
Design, build, and optimize automated dashboards in Tableau, Looker, or Sigma that provide real-time visibility into business performance
Perform ad-hoc deep-dive analyses to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for process improvement across different business units
Partner with data engineering to define requirements for new data sources and ensure the accuracy of ingested data via rigorous QA processes
Translate business requirements into technical specifications and deliver data-driven recommendations to non-technical stakeholders
Document data definitions, metric logic, and analytical methodology to ensure a single source of truth across the organization


What We Are Looking For


3–6 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or a similar quantitative role at a high-growth technology company
Expert-level SQL skills with the ability to write complex joins, window functions, and CTEs for large-scale datasets
Hands-on experience with modern BI tools such as Tableau, Looker (LookML), or Sigma Computing
Strong understanding of data modeling concepts and experience working with transformation tools like dbt
Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or Engineering
Bonus: Proficiency in Python or R for statistical analysis; experience with Salesforce or Netsuite data schemas; familiarity with version control via Git

Title: Supply Chain Data Analyst
Description:
Bevi is on a mission to transform how beverages are delivered and consumed. Our connected beverage platform eliminates the need for single-use bottles and cans, making it easy, fun, and sustainable to stay hydrated. As the category leader in IoT-enabled beverage technology, we're building a future where Bevi machines are everywhere people live, work, and connect. We've raised over $160M in venture capital, serve thousands of customers across the US, Canada, UK and Ireland, and we've been rapidly growing year over year, saving over 1 billion bottles from waste. In addition to driving hypergrowth with our current product line, Bevi is heavily investing in new product development.

The Supply Chain Data Analyst will play a critical role in evolving Bevi’s operational efficiency by managing the tactical procurement of components while simultaneously auditing and modernizing our core supply chain workflows. This role serves as a hybrid practitioner and internal consultant, bridging the gap between current manual purchasing processes and a future state powered by automation, seamless system integration, and AI. Operating with a blend of extreme detail orientation and technical aptitude, you will partner with Global Supply Managers (GSMs) and cross-functional teams to ensure data integrity, identify bottlenecks, and propose scalable solutions. This is a high-impact opportunity to influence how a high-growth hardware startup leverages emerging technology to scale its supply chain and master data operations.

Key Responsibilities


Tactical PO Execution: Partner with the Planning and Global Supply Management (GSM) team to execute day-to-day purchasing, managing PO issuance, tracking delivery schedules, and handling routine expedites to align with production needs.
Invoice & Receiving Resolution: Act as the first line of defense for tactical discrepancies, investigating and correcting data when vendor shipments or invoices do not match ERP records.
Master Data Governance: Take ownership of SKU and BOM accuracy, acting as the gatekeeper for our "single source of truth" across core platforms (e.g., NetSuite, Arena) .
Process Mapping & Documentation: Lead the discovery of "current state" procurement workflows, identifying manual handoffs and creating visual maps or SOPs to standardize supply chain operations.
Automation Opportunity Assessment: Analyze documented purchasing and inventory processes to identify repetitive tasks ripe for automation using tools like Zapier, Workato, or native ERP functionalities.
AI Implementation Research: Investigate and pilot AI-driven use cases (such as LLM assisted data cleaning, automated vendor follow-ups, or predictive stock alerting) to reduce administrative overhead.
Continuous Improvement Reporting: Build simple dashboards to track supply chain KPIs (e.g., PO cycle time, on-time delivery) and the "before and after" impact of implemented process changes.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Business Analytics, Industrial Engineering, Information Systems, or 1-3 years of equivalent practical experience.
Strong analytical mindset with an obsession for detail and a knack for "connecting the dots" between master data, procurement execution, and downstream business functions.
Proficiency in ERP systems (NetSuite preferred) and PLM software (Arena), alongside advanced Excel/Google
Sheets skills (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUPs/XLOOKUPs). Exposure to or high interest in automation platforms, process mapping tools (e.g., Lucidchart, Miro), and AI tools (ChatGPT/Claude for work, API basics, or low-code environments).
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate "supply chain pain" into clear technical requirements for engineering and finance stakeholders.
A self-starter attitude with the instinct to fix the root cause of data errors or slow processes, rather than just treating the symptoms in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.


What Success Looks Like


Operational Stability: Consistent, accurate execution of all buyer responsibilities with a visible reduction in "fire drills" caused by data silos or manual handoffs.
Improved Data Integrity: Maintenance of a highly accurate "single source of truth" within our primary systems (NetSuite/Arena) that perfectly mirrors GSM-negotiated terms.
Comprehensive Process Library: Delivery of a centralized repository of documented core procurement and master data workflows that are easy for the team to navigate and update.
Measurable Efficiency Gains: Successful implementation of at least 2-3 automation or AI assisted "quick wins" that reduce manual data entry or administrative work hours for the supply chain team.


At Bevi, We Believe Compensation Is a Powerful Tool To Attract, Retain, And Grow Talent. Our Compensation Philosophy Centers On 5 Principles


Market-driven - We anchor pay decisions in real-time market data
Performance-based - We reward individual impact, not just tenure
Equitable - We ensure fairness across teams, roles, and demographics
Growth-focused - We invest in talent that scales with Bevi
Total Rewards approach - We strategically balance base pay, bonuses, benefits, and equity


The posted compensation range reflects the salary* for this position. Some roles may be eligible for a commission plan or a bonus incentive. All Bevi employees receive equity in the form of stock options. All full-time employees are invited to participate in our Total Rewards plan, which includes health & medical benefits, flexible spending accounts, flexible PTO, and more. Offers to join Bevi are based on a few criteria, including the scope of the role, the candidate's work experience, targeted skills, internal equity of the team, and external market data.


For Sales roles: The posted range reflects base salary plus commission which is the total potential on target earnings (OTE) for the position.


Pay Range

$76,585—$94,605 USD

Benefits


Comprehensive medical, dental and vision insurance plans with BlueCross BlueShield, 95% paid by employer
401(k) with company match
Flexible PTO plus 12 company holidays, and additional paid days for sick leave, etc
Generous fully paid parental leave for both birth parents and non-birth parents
Fully employer paid disability and life insurances
Wellness and fitness reimbursements
Monthly stipends for cell phone use and commuting costs
Onsite snacks, weekly catered lunch, and (of course) unlimited Bevi ... plus composting and terra-cycling, too
Happy hours, team-building events, bagel breakfasts, Values awards - and more.


A note on AI in our hiring process: We use AI to help synthesize interview notes and surface themes from feedback, keeping our process consistent as we grow. Every application is reviewed by a member of our team, and interviews may be recorded to support note-taking, just let us know if you'd prefer we don't. We'll also ask how you're learning and experimenting with AI in your work. Growing our AI fluency is something every Bevi team member is committed to and invested in.

Our commitment to responsible innovation is reflected in our partnership with the Mass AI Coalition, a collaboration that aligns with our dedication to community-driven progress and the future of technology.

We're passionate about supporting career growth and would love to be part of your professional journey. We also know that talent comes in many forms. We value individual accomplishments, specialized knowledge, and genuine passion over simply checking boxes on a requirements list. Whether you're a seasoned expert or an emerging talent, we're looking for people who are ready to build what's next. If any of our open positions interest you, we'd encourage you to apply! A member of the Bevi Talent team 'recruiting@bevi.co' will be reaching out about next steps if we would like to move forward.

Accommodations

Bevi is committed to an inclusive hiring process and we aim to provide accommodations for persons with disabilities. If you need any accommodations for the application or throughout the interview process please contact recruiting@bevi.co.

Title: Master Data Analyst
Description:
Job Overview:

The Master Data Analyst is a member of the Master Data (MD) Operations team and is responsible for ensuring Customer, Vendor, and Finance master data is created, maintained, and governed in accordance with company data standards, internal control requirements, and defined Service Level Agreements (SLAs). This role supports accurate and timely data to enable efficient operations, reliable reporting, and sound business decision-making across the enterprise.

This position reports to the Master Data Manager and Master Data Supervisor and operates in a hybrid work environment in our Burlington, MA headquarters.

Position Responsibilities


Ensure Customer, Vendor, and Finance master data is created, validated, maintained, and governed per company data standards, governance policies, internal controls, and SLAs.
Process new requests and change requests for master data objects including customers, vendors, and finance data (e.g., GL, Cost Centers, Profit Centers), ensuring data accuracy, completeness, and required approvals.
Maintain master data hierarchies, mappings, and reference data to support operational, financial, and reporting needs.
Review and approve master data updates and perform periodic data cleansing and data quality reviews.
Support business projects, system enhancements, testing, and validation activities related to master data and Master Data Management (MDM) solutions.
Partner with key stakeholder groups to understand business needs and deliver timely, accurate master data support.
Identify and implement process and system improvement opportunities; collaborate with manager and team to optimize end-to-end master data processes.
Ensure compliance with SOX and internal controls to mitigate operational, business, and financial reporting risk.
Participate in master data audits, governance forums, and cross-functional meetings.
Demonstrate core competencies including customer focus, accountability, problem solving, collaboration, and drive for results.



Business Support


Complete special requests and ad hoc analysis or reporting as requested.
Operate within internal and SOX controls by ensuring requests are processed and reviewed per established guidelines.
Escalate compliance failures and internal/SOX control issues to management on a regular basis.
Manage assigned workload to ensure SLA, productivity, and quality metrics are consistently met.



It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Total Rewards:


Salary Range: $62,500 - $72,000
Actual placement within the compensation range may vary depending on experience, skills, and other factors
Benefits, subject to election and eligibility: Medical, Dental, Vision, Disability, Paid Time Off (including paid parental leave, vacation, and sick time), 401k with company match, Tuition Reimbursement, and Mileage Reimbursement
Annual bonus based on performance and eligibility



Requirements:


Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, Information Systems, or related field, or 3+ years of equivalent work experience.
Experience with Customer, Vendor, and/or Finance Master Data and data standards applications.
Intermediate to Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (e.g., VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, advanced functions).



Competencies


Strong attention to detail and data accuracy
Ability to manage and prioritize high volumes of requests in a fast-paced environment
Effective written and verbal communication skills
Customer-service mindset
Ability to collaborate effectively and contribute to a team-oriented environment



Company Overview:

Keurig Dr Pepper (Nasdaq: KDP) is a leading beverage company with more than 150 owned, licensed and partner brands that meet a wide range of needs and occasions. Our North American refreshment beverage business holds leadership positions across carbonated soft drinks, water, juice and mixers with a portfolio of iconic brands such as Dr Pepper®, Canada Dry®, Mott’s®, A&W®, Peñafiel®, GHOST®, 7UP®, Snapple®, Clamato® and Core Hydration®. Our global coffee business spans more than 100 markets and includes the leading Keurig® single‑serve brewing system in the U.S. and Canada, along with powerhouse brands such as Peet’s, L’OR and Jacobs, and other regional coffee leaders. Our more than 50,000 employees aim to enhance the experience of every beverage and coffee occasion while making a positive impact for people, communities and the planet.
