Job Overview:

The Master Data Analyst is a member of the Master Data (MD) Operations team and is responsible for ensuring Customer, Vendor, and Finance master data is created, maintained, and governed in accordance with company data standards, internal control requirements, and defined Service Level Agreements (SLAs). This role supports accurate and timely data to enable efficient operations, reliable reporting, and sound business decision-making across the enterprise.

This position reports to the Master Data Manager and Master Data Supervisor and operates in a hybrid work environment in our Burlington, MA headquarters.

Position Responsibilities

  • Ensure Customer, Vendor, and Finance master data is created, validated, maintained, and governed per company data standards, governance policies, internal controls, and SLAs.
  • Process new requests and change requests for master data objects including customers, vendors, and finance data (e.g., GL, Cost Centers, Profit Centers), ensuring data accuracy, completeness, and required approvals.
  • Maintain master data hierarchies, mappings, and reference data to support operational, financial, and reporting needs.
  • Review and approve master data updates and perform periodic data cleansing and data quality reviews.
  • Support business projects, system enhancements, testing, and validation activities related to master data and Master Data Management (MDM) solutions.
  • Partner with key stakeholder groups to understand business needs and deliver timely, accurate master data support.
  • Identify and implement process and system improvement opportunities; collaborate with manager and team to optimize end-to-end master data processes.
  • Ensure compliance with SOX and internal controls to mitigate operational, business, and financial reporting risk.
  • Participate in master data audits, governance forums, and cross-functional meetings.
  • Demonstrate core competencies including customer focus, accountability, problem solving, collaboration, and drive for results.


Business Support

  • Complete special requests and ad hoc analysis or reporting as requested.
  • Operate within internal and SOX controls by ensuring requests are processed and reviewed per established guidelines.
  • Escalate compliance failures and internal/SOX control issues to management on a regular basis.
  • Manage assigned workload to ensure SLA, productivity, and quality metrics are consistently met.

About Pembroke & Co.:

Founded in 2015, Pembroke & Co. is the superior solution for loss prevention, operational excellence, and consulting.

We have a culture built on the ethics and morals of the highest standard. We are self-starters, resourceful innovators, and creative thinkers. We believe in transparency with our clients as well as with our team members. Open discussions where everyone’s voice is heard are the key to our strong foundation.

Position Overview:

The Video & Data Analyst is the backbone of our operations department. Analysts are responsible for reviewing, monitoring, and analyzing transaction data, video feed, financial reports and social media reviews for numerous establishments. This may include: restaurants, bars, hotels, convenience stores, trades, etc.

Analysts have a thorough understanding of the businesses we support and find ways to bring value to our clients every day.

Job Responsibilities:


Monitor, review and analyze video surveillance and point-of sales (POS) transaction data for discrepancies, internal theft, fraud, etc.
Analyze business trends through video surveillance to determine ways the business can improve sales, customer counts, shrink, profits, and the customer experience.
Monitor for social media reviews through numerous platforms for useful information tied to sales, brand reputation, and customer satisfaction.
Compile and deliver reports of findings with detailed information and still pictures.
Conduct pre-delivery reviews of reports for accuracy and value, utilizing discretion and independent judgement.
Meets deadlines and deliverables consistently, while maintaining high-quality standards.
Assist with ad-hoc requests from clients in a timely and accurate manner.
Additional tasks as required.



Qualifications:


Bachelor’s degree in business, communications, psychology, criminal justice, finance, mathematics, or a related field is required.
Experience in loss prevention, law enforcement, human resources, customer service, legal services, or consulting is preferred. Prior experience in hospitality or retail is encouraged.
Experience utilizing CCTV video surveillance is a plus.
Proficient with MS Office applications.
Demonstrates discretion; understands the importance of sensitive and confidential material.
Strong attention to detail with an investigative mindset; enjoys identifying issues, uncovering patterns, and delivering actionable insights to clients.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
Excellent verbal and written communication.



Flexible work from home options available.

Title: Data & Product Analyst (Benchmarking), CoStar Debt Solutions
Description:
Job Description

Data & Product Analyst (Benchmarking)

Location: Boston, in-office

About The Role

CoStar Debt Solutions is building the next generation of CRE analytics software — a platform where data, benchmarking intelligence, and client workflows come together in a single experience to support how lenders originate, evaluate, and manage risk.

A central part of this vision is our Benchmarking product, which enables lenders to compare loan performance, pricing, and risk characteristics against a trusted market dataset. This product is rapidly evolving, with significant investment in expanding coverage, improving methodologies, and delivering more actionable insights directly into client workflows.

We’re hiring a Data & Product Analyst to help drive the continued growth of this Benchmarking product — both as a data asset and as a highly client-facing solution.

This role combines data analysis, product development, and direct client engagement, with a strong emphasis on supporting client adoption and enabling the sales process. You will work closely with clients and internal sales teams to demonstrate product value, answer analytical questions, and ensure clients can effectively leverage benchmarking insights in their workflows.

In year one, this role will focus on analyzing benchmark data, supporting product enhancements, and actively participating in client meetings, product demos, and sales support activities. Over time, the role expands into shaping benchmark methodologies, influencing product strategy, and helping scale how benchmarking insights are delivered across the platform.

This is a hybrid role at the intersection of data analysis, product development, and client/sales engagement — ideal for someone who can translate complex datasets into clear insights and communicate them effectively to both internal and external audiences.

What You’ll Own


The analysis of complex datasets that power the Benchmarking product — identifying trends, inconsistencies, and opportunities to improve data coverage and quality
The evolution of benchmark methodologies, outputs, and usability in partnership with Product Managers and cross-functional teams
Direct engagement with clients, including leading and supporting client conversations to understand benchmarking use cases, answer analytical questions, and gather actionable feedback
Active support of sales and go-to-market efforts, including participating in product demonstrations, responding to client inquiries, and helping articulate the value of benchmarking insights
Ongoing client support and enablement, ensuring users can interpret and apply benchmark data effectively in their workflows
Collaboration with engineering teams to ensure accurate delivery of benchmark data, calculations, and features
Monitoring product usage and client feedback to identify areas for improvement and drive continuous enhancements
Data validation, quality checks, and troubleshooting to maintain the reliability and integrity of benchmark datasets
Staying current on industry trends and competitive benchmarking solutions to inform product direction


What We Expect You To Bring


Bachelor’s degree from an accredited, not-for-profit, in person, university or college. Ideally in Data Analytics, Data Science, Business Analytics, Finance, Economics, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
2+ years of experience working with data analytics, financial data, or data-driven products
Strong hands-on experience working with large datasets and performing detailed analysis
Strong communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively with both technical stakeholders and clients
Proven ability to support client-facing interactions, including presenting, explaining analytical concepts, and responding to client questions in real time
Experience with analytics or benchmarking products, or similar data-driven solutions
Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving skills
A track record of commitment to prior employers


Nice To Have


Exposure to SQL, Python, or similar languages
Experience with benchmarking, peer comparison, or performance analytics solutions
Experience supporting client-facing products or working in a customer-oriented environment
Familiarity with financial services, lending, or real estate data
Experience with data visualization tools
Understanding of APIs and data integration concepts


The Growth Path

The first year in this role is focused on deeply understanding the benchmark dataset, client needs, and current product capabilities — contributing directly to data analysis, active client engagement, sales support, and incremental product improvements. You will play a key role in client conversations, product demonstrations, and helping drive adoption of benchmarking insights in real-world workflows.

Beyond that, the role expands into a more strategic position:


Shaping how benchmark methodologies evolve as the dataset scales
Driving enhancements to how insights are surfaced within client workflows
Partnering with sales and client teams to improve how benchmarking is positioned, adopted, and delivered
Influencing product direction based on client usage patterns, feedback, and market trends


This role is a strong path into broader product ownership, client-facing product strategy, or advanced analytics leadership, particularly for someone interested in building and scaling data-driven products that directly support client outcomes and business growth.

What’s Unique About This Role

Most data analyst roles focus on generating insights. This role goes further — connecting those insights directly to product decisions and client outcomes.

You’ll Not Only Analyze Data, But Also


Work directly with clients and sales teams to translate insights into adoption and business value
Play a key role in client-facing discussions that shape both product direction and commercial success
Help bridge the gap between data, product, and revenue-generating activities


What’s in it for you?

When you join CoStar Group, you’ll experience a collaborative and innovative culture working alongside the best and brightest to empower our people and customers to succeed. We offer you generous compensation and performance-based incentives. CoStar Group also invests in your professional and academic growth with internal training, and tuition reimbursement.

Our Benefits Package Includes (but Is Not Limited To)


Comprehensive healthcare coverage: Medical / Vision / Dental / Prescription Drug
Life, legal, and supplementary insurance
Virtual and in person mental health counseling services for individuals and family
Commuter and parking benefits
401(K) retirement plan with matching contributions
Employee stock purchase plan
Paid time off
Tuition reimbursement
On-site fitness center and/or reimbursed fitness center membership costs (location dependent)
Access to CoStar Group’s Employee Resource Groups
Complimentary gourmet coffee, tea, hot chocolate, fresh fruit, and other healthy snacks


We welcome all qualified candidates who are currently eligible to work full-time in the United States to apply. However, please note that CoStar Group is not able to provide visa sponsorship for this position.

CoStar Group is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer; we maintain a drug-free workplace and perform pre-employment substance abuse testing

CoStar Group is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer; we maintain a drug-free workplace and perform pre-employment substance abuse testing

Title: Principal Data Governance Analyst 252416
Description:
Principal, Data Governance

Direct hire

Hybrid remote- Wednesdays onsite in Boston, MA

$130k-140k




We are seeking a Principal, Data Governance to join a growing Enterprise Data & Analytics function. This is a highly visible, strategic role for a senior leader who can help stand up and scale a modern data governance program while partnering closely with both technical and business stakeholders.

This role is ideal for someone who combines technical depth (data, SQL, lineage) with strong communication and leadership skills, and is excited to drive adoption of governance practices across an organization.




🔑 Core Responsibilities

You will play a key role in advancing five foundational pillars of the data governance program:

Operating Model

Develop and refine the data governance operating model, including policies, standards, and best practices

Metadata Management

Drive adoption and optimization of enterprise data catalog tools
Ensure seamless integration with modern data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks)

Data Quality

Establish data quality standards, rules, and monitoring frameworks
Partner with teams to improve trust and reliability of data assets

Data Stewardship

Lead governance forums (e.g., Data Governance Council)
Enable and support data stewards across the organization

Master Data Management (MDM)

Contribute to long-term strategy and roadmap for MDM and reference data




🛠 Key Duties

Act as a “train-the-trainer”, developing job aids, documentation, and training materials to promote data literacy
Partner with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into governance requirements
Read and interpret SQL to validate data lineage and metadata accuracy
Manage metadata workflows, approvals, and cataloging processes




✅ Required Qualifications

7+ years of experience in data governance, metadata management, data quality, or data stewardship
Strong experience with Snowflake (required)
Hands-on experience with at least one data governance tool:
Atlan, Collibra, Alation, or data.world
Deep understanding of:
Data lineage, metadata management, and data cataloging
Data quality frameworks and tools (e.g., Informatica IDQ, Talend, DataGaps)
Experience working within modern data ecosystems (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, BI tools)




💡 Key Skills & Attributes

Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills (this is a highly collaborative role)
Ability to influence across technical and non-technical teams
Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset
Experience working in regulated environments (e.g., familiarity with HIPAA, GDPR, CCPA is a plus)




🎓 Education

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Analytics, Business, Engineering, or related field required
Master’s degree preferred




📍 Work Environment

Hybrid schedule (onsite midweek)




🌟 Why Join?

This is a unique opportunity to help shape and scale a data governance program from the ground up, working at the intersection of strategy, technology, and business enablement.

If you’re looking for a role where you can drive real impact and establish best-in-class data practices, we’d love to connect.

Title: Analyst, Growth Analytics
Description:
Position Overview

Join our Business Analytics team, where data meets creativity to drive smarter media decisions. As the Analyst, you’ll collaborate with internal teams, external partners, and data providers to transform insights into action — actively contributing to optimizing media investments and shaping strategy across digital and traditional channels.

In this role, you’ll dive deep into the evolving world of digital and media ecosystems, gaining experience with broad range of analytics:

Data tracking challenges with cookies and SDKs
Website tagging and measurement strategies
The impact of walled gardens on analytics
TV and out-of-home (OOH) attribution models




You’ll gain hands-on experience with cutting-edge analytics tools and methodologies, helping to build a foundation in media measurement, attribution, and optimization. This opportunity is designed to launch your career in media analytics, connecting you with a brand that’s woven into America’s DNA.




Why This Role Stands Out




This hybrid position blends media analytics with web analytics, offering exposure to multiple specialties across the analytics landscape. You’ll have access to mentorship, professional development, and real-world projects that accelerate your growth. If you’re curious, analytical, and eager to learn, this role opens doors to a dynamic career in data-driven marketing.




Responsibilities

Develop an understanding of client’s industry, business objectives and strategy, media plans, and consumer journey to provide context for analysis and reporting needs
Data Extraction, Transformation & Loading (ETL), Quality & Hygiene
Coordinate with technical teams to ensure accurate and comprehensive web analytics implementation and tracking across web properties.
Provide insight into site visitor behaviour and engagement to identify optimization opportunities across websites to help inform site design changes.
Apply independent critical thinking to analyse vast data sets to uncover media insights (what’s working, what’s not working) to drive strategic decisions.
Participate in brainstorming sessions with communication planners, creative assets, media planners, buyers, clients and other partner agencies
Contribute to the department’s overall capabilities and understanding of media and communications channels
Work with Director and senior analysts to develop report content (Word, Excel, Tableau, Looker, PowerPoint) in support of ongoing deliverables, as well as ad hoc reporting




Required Skills and Experience

Must possess strong interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills
Highly motivated and collaborative team player with strong interpersonal skills
Expertise in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint and Word
Experience working with large datasets to uncover insights that are essential to business optimizations is a plus




Desired Skills and Experience

1+ years of experience in a professional services or corporate environment
Experience using data visualization tools, such as: Excel 365, Tableau or Snowflake preferred
Experience with ad-serving platforms such as Google Campaign Manager is a plus

Join our Business Analytics team, where data meets creativity to drive smarter media decisions. As the Analyst, you’ll collaborate with internal teams, external partners, and data providers to transform insights into action — actively contributing to optimizing media investments and shaping strategy across digital and traditional channels.

In this role, you’ll dive deep into the evolving world of digital and media ecosystems, gaining experience with broad range of analytics:

  • Data tracking challenges with cookies and SDKs
  • Website tagging and measurement strategies
  • The impact of walled gardens on analytics
  • TV and out-of-home (OOH) attribution models


You’ll gain hands-on experience with cutting-edge analytics tools and methodologies, helping to build a foundation in media measurement, attribution, and optimization. This opportunity is designed to launch your career in media analytics, connecting you with a brand that’s woven into America’s DNA.


Why This Role Stands Out


This hybrid position blends media analytics with web analytics, offering exposure to multiple specialties across the analytics landscape. You’ll have access to mentorship, professional development, and real-world projects that accelerate your growth. Title: Analyst, Insights, Data & Intelligence
Description:
Who We Are

Burson, part of WPP, is the global communications leader built to create value for clients through reputation. With highly specialized teams, industry-leading technologies and breakthrough creative, we help brands and businesses redefine reputation as a competitive advantage so they can lead today and into the future. When you work at Burson, you are part of a global community of lifelong learners who thrive at the edge of innovation.

WPP (LSE/NYSE: WPP) is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit WPP.com

For more information visit bursonglobal.com and follow us on LinkedIn and Instagram.

More About The Role

The Business Insights, Data & Intelligence (BIDI) team sits at the heart of this work—turning data into strategic insight that drives storytelling, campaign performance, and business outcomes.

Due to our expanding client roster and scopes, Burson is seeking to hire a group of Analysts to join our best-in-class Business Insights, Data & Intelligence (BIDI) team, supporting some of the world’s most recognizable global brands such as The Coca-Cola Company, Verizon and Google. This opportunity is ideal for a recent graduate with internship experience in digital media, marketing, communications, or analytics who is excited to explore the intersection of social media, consumer behavior/social listening, and data-driven storytelling.

As part of a highly collaborative team, you’ll gain hands-on experience uncovering cultural insights, monitoring online conversations, and helping brands make informed decisions through social and digital intelligence. This is a unique opportunity to launch a career in analytics at one of the world’s leading global communications agencies while working across high-profile client accounts.

This role can be based in Atlanta, the New York metro area, or Los Angeles, with a preference for Atlanta-based candidates.

Relocation assistance will be available for candidates open to relocating to Atlanta.

What You'll Do


Support social listening, media monitoring, and reporting initiatives across a portfolio of global brands.
Track cultural moments, emerging conversations, competitor activity, and consumer sentiment across social and digital platforms.
Utilize industry-leading tools such as Brandwatch, Sprinklr, and NewsWhip to uncover actionable insights tied to brand reputation, campaign performance, sustainability, and corporate communications initiatives.
Translate complex social and digital data into clear, compelling narratives and presentations for internal teams and clients.
Help lead recurring reports, dashboards, and performance analyses to measure brand health and campaign impact.
Monitor online conversations and media trends surrounding brand, industry, and corporate topics in real time.
Partner with cross-functional teams across strategy, creative, media, and communications in a fast-paced agency environment.
Gain exposure to both agency operations and client-facing work while developing analytical and storytelling skills.


Experience That Contributes To Success


Bachelor’s degree in communications, marketing, journalism, public relations, data analytics, business, or a related field.
1-2+ years of experience in analytics, insights, or intelligence roles working within or for the PR, Communications, or Advertising industry, with a deep understanding of its unique data landscapes, client demands, and strategic challenges.
Strong curiosity about social media trends, consumer behavior, internet culture, and data-driven insights.
Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills with the ability to synthesize information clearly and concisely.
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail, with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint; familiarity with pivot tables, Boolean search logic, dashboards, or data visualization tools is a plus.
Comfortable learning new technologies and navigating a dynamic, fast-paced environment.
Collaborative mindset with a willingness to learn, contribute ideas, and work across teams.
Exposure to social listening or analytics platforms such as Brandwatch, Sprinklr, Talkwalker, or Meltwater is a plus.
Additional language fluency in Spanish, Portuguese, or French is a bonus.


Burson is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

Individual compensation varies based on job-related factors, including location, business needs, level of responsibility, experience, and qualifications. We offer a competitive benefits package; click here for more details: https://mybenefits.wpp.com/public/welcome.

The base salary for this position at the time of this posting may range between:

$35,000—$75,000 USD

You Belong At Burson

Our vision is for Burson to be the leading ‘academy company’ for creative communications professionals and emerging leaders. We provide a dynamic environment where ambitious colleagues come to elevate their skills, expand their networks, and ultimately, produce the best solutions for today’s leading brands. Our commitment to your growth is reflected in our robust benefits and people programs, which include professional development opportunities, mentorship programs with industry leaders, a vibrant and inclusive company culture fostering collaboration and innovation and packages designed to reward and retain top talent. We invest in our people because we believe their success is our success.

At Burson, we’re an agency of learn-it-alls. We recognize that the things we do best, creativity and communications, require a level of nuanced understanding. That’s why it’s imperative for us to hire extraordinarily talented people of all backgrounds and identities. Without that we can’t do the essential work of inspiring bold ideas, fostering constructive dialogue, cultivating meaningful experiences, and driving innovation and creativity for our partners. Your unique point of view—your talent—is what makes you a #BursonPerson, and it’s how we deliver exceptional results for our clients, together.

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office around four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

When you click the "Submit Application" button below, this will send any information you add below to Burson. Before you do this, we think it's a good idea to read through our  Recruitment Privacy Policy. California residents should read our  California Recruitment Privacy Notice. This explains what we do with your personal data when you apply for a role with us, and, how you can update the information you have provided us with or how to remove it.

Title: Healthcare Data Analyst (Clinic Administration)
Description:
The Healthcare Data Analyst will join a collaborative team at Joslin Diabetes Center supporting clinical operations, grounded in patient safety and improvement science to participate in projects that promote a culture of excellence.

The Healthcare Data Analyst will be responsible for acquisition, analysis, interpretation, and reporting of sensitive data to support quality and performance measurement and track improvement efforts. They must be able to work independently and collaboratively to locate and validate data, identify data requirements, and communicate data specifications to team members and leaders. They must be able to develop reporting/analytic solutions, but also be able to draw meaningful conclusions and make recommendations for actions.

The Healthcare Data Analyst will leverage software applications to design solutions and perform detailed data mapping and analysis. They will not only manage data and design new processes, but will be accountable for the timely and accurate production of routine monitoring reports and special projects. This requires experience working with databases, establishing data connections and running queries, aggregating and synthesizing data for business plans, trends, and projections, along with creativity, curiosity, and initiative to evolve the tools and reporting systems to meet the priorities of a dynamic healthcare environment. They will need an understanding of healthcare financial and operational processes, data management, as well as manipulation and interpretation of large databases.

This role is expected to have onsite training for the first few months then transition to a hybrid schedule.

As a health care organization, we have the responsibility to do everything in our power to care for and protect our patients, our colleagues and our communities. Beth Israel Lahey Health requires that all staff be vaccinated against influenza (flu) as a condition of employment.


Responsible for acquiring, validating, and querying data from data repositories and then using available tools to create dashboards, reports, and effectively synthesizing the data for the end user.
Works with members across teams to define problems and clarify objectives; determines data needs and assists in designing collection tools and reports. Evaluates data and reports to assess if reporting meets end user requirements.
Develops executive level presentations including key observations of statistical, financial and qualitative information. Provides consulting support in developing strategic recommendations to aid decision making by senior management.
Stays abreast of healthcare trends and market development as context for analyses, and becomes proficient with the health system's decision support and other systems.
Collaborates with Information Systems and enterprise data analytics team(s) to obtain and maintain sources and mapping tables.
Ensures data integrity and data quality, regularly reviews data for accuracy, identifies trends, and communicates changes.
Other duties as assigned.
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Allied Health or related required. Master's degree in Public Health, Finance, Epidemiology, Biostatistics or equivalent preferred.
2-5 years related work experience.
Strong experience in data management and analytic tools and techniques; Strong experience in report writing, analytics, visualizations, dashboard creation; Strong experience demonstrating analytical, financial, qualitative and quantitative skills.
Advanced technical computer skills as required for technical support specific to functional area and related systems.



The pay range listed for this position is the annual base salary range the organization reasonably and in good faith expects to pay for this position at this time. Actual compensation is determined based on several factors, that may include seniority, education, training, relevant experience, relevant certifications, geography of work location, job responsibilities, or other applicable factors permissible by law.

USD $72,800.00/Yr.

USD $93,600.00/Yr.

Title: Data Analyst – Front Office - Material Data
Description:
For this U.S. based position, the expected compensation range is $101,600 - $152,400 per year, which includes base pay and short-term incentive.

The compensation range for this full-time position applies to candidates located within the United States. Our salary ranges are determined by reviewing roles of similar responsibility and level. Within the salary range, individual pay is determined by several factors including performance, knowledge, job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Schneider Electric also offers a comprehensive benefits package to support our employees, inclusive of medical (with member reward points), dental, vision, and basic life insurance, Benefit Bucks (credits to apply towards your benefits) flexible work arrangements, paid family leaves, 401(k) + match, well-being and recognition (including service anniversary) programs, 12 holidays per year,15 days of paid time off per year (pro-rated in the first year of employment based on start date), opportunity to purchase company stock (eligibility depends on start date), and military leave benefits.

You must submit an online application to be considered for the position. The Company will accept applications on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

If you believe this job posting is not compliant with applicable state pay transparency laws in the U.S., please notify the Company as soon as possible upon discovery by completing this form Job Posting Compliance Form.

Join the Titan Data Transformation team supporting Front Office (ART 1) initiatives focused on material data. This role is responsible for enabling the successful migration and transformation of customer master data into SAP S/4 HANA, ensuring alignment across sales, distribution, and commercial processes.

Key responsibilities include:


Working in an agile environment with business and technical team to identifying data migration requirements and dependencies.
Responsible for understanding and executing data mapping and data migrations with business and functional analysts.
Work with the business on data clean up
Work with data conversion and data load teams to drive end-to-end migration activities.
Assist in the development of data quality and readiness checks and identify solution changes which could impact checks.
Trouble shoot data extraction, conversion, and load errors
Validate accuracy of migrated data to expected outcomes
Assist with the development of lifecycle processes


Additional Responsibilities:


Support migration and governance of customer master data across sales and distribution processes
Partner with commercial and sales teams to define customer data requirements and resolve data gaps
Validate customer hierarchies, account structures, and sales area data for S/4 readiness
Identify and remediate inconsistencies impacting order processing and customer lifecycle data


Primary Skills/Qualifications:


Master data management and governance experience
5+ years SE manufacturing process knowledge or working in a manufacturing environment which applies data governance principles
3+ years of hands-on experience working with data, data tables and ETL processes
3+ years of hands-on experience working in the SAP environment
Excellent at solving problems and working through complex analytics
Candidate is proactive, collaborative, and persistent
Organized, detail oriented and rigorous
Advanced Excel skills
Intermediate SQL proficiency


Travel Requirement:

This role may require travel up to approximately 50–75% based on project phase and deployment needs.

Passport Requirement:

A valid passport is required as this role may support cross-border collaboration and deployment activities.

  • 3+ years of hands-on experience working in the SAP environment
  • Excellent at solving problems and working through complex analytics
  • Candidate is proactive, collaborative, and persistent
  • Organized, detail oriented and rigorous
  • Advanced Excel skills
  • Intermediate SQL proficiency

Travel Requirement:

This role may require travel up to approximately 50–75% based on project phase and deployment needs.

Passport Requirement:

A valid passport is required as this role may support cross-border collaboration and deployment activities.

Title: Analyst I, Full Stack (Core Analytics)
Description:
Affirm is reinventing credit to make it more honest and friendly, giving consumers the flexibility to buy now and pay later without any hidden fees or compounding interest.

We’re looking for a driven, analytically-minded professional to join the Core Analytics team within the Risk & Analytics organization! Our team improves the developer experience across Affirm by building scalable data infrastructure, streamlining workflows, and fostering a culture of learning and documentation. This role sits at the intersection of data and engineering, while staying focused on turning developer workflows and system reliability into clear, actionable signals that accelerate delivery.

The Risk & Analytics team makes crucial decisions that direct Affirm’s business strategy. We analyze performance not just to report on it, but to determine where to invest resources for maximum impact through building tools and analytical frameworks. We take ownership of the entire analytical lifecycle, from initial question to final business decision, ensuring Affirm grows efficiently and intelligently.

The ideal candidate will have strong technical and problem-solving skills, an eagerness to collaborate with different teams, curiosity to learn more every day, and patience to work through difficult problems.

What you will do


Own end-to-end analytics workflows: define success metrics, generate insights and hypotheses, and build scalable data models
Identify and drive improvements across the developer and analytics lifecycle: uncover bottlenecks, quantify their impact, then partner with teams to drive alignment and deliver measurable impact.
Build and maintain high-quality analytics foundations: establish best practices for testing, alerting, documentation, and code review to ensure data is accurate, reliable, and trusted.
Support efforts to identify and organize key business metrics, helping improve discoverability, alignment, and clarity across teams.
Continuously seek out implementing improved operational workflows and more effective analytics solutions.
Elevate the analytics community at Affirm: contribute to shared tooling, reviewing colleagues pull requests, drive knowledge sharing, and increase cross-team collaboration.


What we look for


Passion to redefine the lending and financial industry for the better
1-3 years of experience in an analytically-driven role (analytics, consulting, finance, etc.); Preferably previous experience as an Analytics Engineer, Business Intelligence, or Data Analyst roles
Strong working knowledge of SQL (preferably Snowflake)
Knowledge of analytics development tools such as Git, Sigma, DBT, Snowflake, Claude Code and Cursor
Practical and theoretical knowledge of A/B testing is a plus
Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work cross-functionally and influence both technical and non-technical audiences
Ability to think creatively and critically and thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic, and often ambiguous work environment
Bachelor's or Master's degree in a quantitatively rigorous subject area like statistics, math, finance, economics desired
Enough laziness to find a faster way to do your least favorite tasks
Humility and team player - you go out of your way to help teammates


Base Pay Grade - I

Equity Grade - 5

Employees new to Affirm typically come in at the start of the pay range. Affirm focuses on providing a simple and transparent pay structure which is based on a variety of factors, including location, experience and job-related skills.

Base pay is part of a total compensation package that may include equity rewards, monthly stipends for health, wellness and tech spending, and benefits (including 100% subsidized medical coverage, dental and vision for you and your dependents.)

USA base pay range (CA, WA, NY, NJ, CT) per year: $115,000 - $155,000

USA base pay range (all other U.S. states) per year: $102,000 - $142,000

Affirm is proud to be a remote-first company! The majority of our roles are remote and you can work almost anywhere within the country of employment. Affirmers in proximal roles have the flexibility to work remotely, but will occasionally be required to work out of their assigned Affirm office. A limited number of roles remain office-based due to the nature of their job responsibilities.

Benefits

We’re extremely proud to offer competitive benefits that are anchored to our core value of people come first. Some key highlights of our benefits package include:


Health care coverage - Affirm covers all premiums for all levels of coverage for you and your dependents
Flexible Spending Wallets - generous stipends for spending on Technology, Food, various Lifestyle needs, and family forming expenses
Time off - competitive vacation and holiday schedules allowing you to take time off to rest and recharge
ESPP - An employee stock purchase plan enabling you to buy shares of Affirm at a discount


We believe It’s On Us to provide an inclusive interview experience for all, including people with disabilities. We are happy to provide reasonable accommodations to candidates in need of individualized support during the hiring process.

[For U.S. positions that could be performed in Los Angeles or San Francisco] Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, Affirm will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

By clicking "Submit Application," you acknowledge that you have read Affirm's Global Candidate Privacy Notice and hereby freely and unambiguously give informed consent to the collection, processing, use, and storage of your personal information as described therein.

Title: Junior Biomedical Data Analyst
Description:
About LifeCanvas Technologies




LifeCanvas Technologies, an MIT spinoff venture, is revolutionizing the way we see and study biological systems. LifeCanvas offers cutting-edge technologies and end-to-end solutions that enable holistic molecular imaging of biological systems, accelerating breakthrough discoveries in science and the development of new therapeutic strategies. As a key partner in this global effort, our computational team works on enabling scientific innovation and directly collaborates with global researchers in bioengineering, neuroscience, and oncology.




Job Summary

We are seeking an ambitious and motivated Junior Biomedical Data Analyst to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience with biomedical data analysis, machine learning, or scientific computing, and a strong interest in building scalable software solutions for both research and industrial applications in a fast-paced start-up environment. In this role, you will support the analysis of large-scale 3D fluorescence microscopy datasets generated by LifeCanvas technologies. You will work closely with our tissue processing, image acquisition, and Contract Research teams to run analyses, validate outputs, troubleshoot issues, and help generate high-quality quantitative results for internal teams and external scientific partners.




Key Responsibilities:

Run, validate, and troubleshoot analyses for Contract Research projects, ensuring accurate results and timely project completion.
Perform quality control on analysis outputs to validate results.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure smooth data acquisition, integration, and downstream analysis.
Communicate analytical findings clearly to internal teams and external scientific partners.
Assist with maintaining and improving components of LifeCanvas image-analysis pipelines for large-scale 3D fluorescence microscopy datasets.
Support the development of data engineering workflows, including database management, ETL processes, and handling of large imaging datasets.




Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in computer science, bioengineering, computational biology, electrical engineering, or a relevant field.
Experience with image processing for biological and/or biomedical applications
Experience using Python for data science, scientific computing, automation, and image analysis.
Strong problem-solving skills with a self-motivated and detail-oriented mindset and the ability to work in a team environment.
Adaptable and proactive in a fast-paced environment, comfortable working across multiple concurrent projects and evolving requirements.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams.




Preferred Qualifications:

Familiarity with light microscopy, neuroscience, and immunohistochemistry
Familiarity with best practices for software engineering.
Exposure to machine learning or deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow.
Experience in working with data engineering/analysis pipelines.




Benefits:

Competitive salary.
Full insurance package for full-time employees.
Flexible time off.
401(k) benefits.

Title: Business System Analyst
Description:
Our client is a global technology and consulting firm engaged with a leading asset management organization to modernize and enhance its investment technology platform. This role is embedded directly within the client environment, supporting a high-visibility initiative focused on investment performance, attribution, and portfolio data systems.




Role Overview

We are seeking a Senior Business Analyst with part-time product management responsibilities to own requirements, backlog, and agile delivery for a complex investment platform. The right candidate brings deep asset management domain knowledge, hands-on technical skills, and a track record of driving delivery in fast-moving, cross-functional environments.




Key Responsibilities

Business & Domain Analysis

Lead requirements elicitation sessions with business and operations stakeholders across front, middle, and back-office teams
Translate complex asset management processes into user stories, acceptance criteria, and functional specifications
Map current and future-state workflows, identifying gaps and optimization opportunities

Agile Delivery & Part-Time PM (20–40%)

Facilitate agile ceremonies including sprint planning, stand-ups, retrospectives, and backlog refinement
Own and groom the product backlog in partnership with the Product Owner
Track sprint velocity, manage dependencies, and communicate delivery status to stakeholders and leadership
Coordinate cross-functional delivery across data engineering, analytics, and QA workstreams

Technical & Data Collaboration

Write and maintain SQL queries for data validation, reconciliation, and ad hoc analysis
Build BI visualizations, preferably using Power BI, to support decision-making and executive readouts
Champion AI-augmented practices across the SDLC — requirements drafting, test case generation, automated documentation




Required Qualifications

5+ years as a Business Systems Analyst or equivalent within asset management or financial services
Strong understanding of front, middle, and back office operations — trade lifecycle, portfolio accounting, investment performance, reconciliation, and client reporting
Working knowledge of financial instruments including equities, fixed income, derivatives, alternatives, ETFs, and mutual funds
Demonstrated ability to independently drive requirements gathering, stakeholder workshops, and documentation
Experience facilitating agile ceremonies in a Scrum or Kanban environment
Proficiency in SQL for querying, data validation, and analytical support




Preferred Qualifications

CFA, CIPM, CAIA, or equivalent designation (or active candidacy)
Understanding of investment performance measurement — TWR, MWR, attribution analysis, benchmark construction, and risk measures such as VaR, tracking error, and Sharpe ratio
Experience with Power BI for dashboard development or data visualization
Working knowledge of Python for scripting, data manipulation, or automation
Familiarity with cloud data platforms such as Microsoft Fabric, Azure Synapse, Snowflake, or Databricks
Experience with JIRA, Azure DevOps, or equivalent agile planning tools




What We're Looking For

A self-starter who takes ownership and drives work forward without being asked
Intellectual curiosity about markets, instruments, and the investment management value chain
Strong communication skills bridging technical teams and business stakeholders
Enthusiasm for experimenting with and integrating AI tools into planning, requirements, testing, and delivery
Collaborative mindset with a bias toward clarity, transparency, and continuous improvement

Title: Lead Data Analyst (Network)
Description:
About Agero:Wherever drivers go, we’re leading the way. Agero’s mission is to rethink the vehicle ownership experience through a powerful combination of passionate people and data-driven technology, strengthening our clients’ relationships with their customers. As the #1 B2B, white-label provider of digital driver assistance services, we’re pushing the industry in a new direction, taking manual processes, and redefining them as digital, transparent, and connected. This includes: an industry-leading dispatch management platform powered by Swoop; comprehensive accident management services; knowledgeable consumer affairs and connected vehicle capabilities; and a growing marketplace of services, discounts and support enabled by a robust partner ecosystem. The company has over 150 million vehicle coverage points in partnership with leading automobile manufacturers, insurance carriers and many others. Managing one of the largest national networks of service providers, Agero responds to approximately 12 million service events annually. Agero, a member company of The Cross Country Group, is headquartered in Medford, Mass., with operations throughout North America. To learn more, visit https://www.agero.com/.

Note: For our technical positions, we love to get you started in person! You may be required to travel to Medford for your initial onboarding. Don't worry about the logistics - once you're hired, we handle all travel arrangements and expenses for you.

Position Summary

The Lead Data Analyst (Network) is at the forefront of data-driven initiatives for the group, acting as a linchpin between raw data and actionable insights. This position dissects complex datasets, identifies  patterns, and leverages those insights to drive business growth. Collaborates closely with various internal and external stakeholders, helping them understand the data and how this information can shape future strategies.

Responsibilities:

Advanced Analytics: Develop and deploy advanced analytical models to improve our understanding of Network supply and demand, identify operational inefficiencies, and deliver actionable recommendations to leadership
Performance Monitoring: Implement real-time dashboards and reporting to track and assess Network performance against established metrics
Network Strategy Innovation: Challenge the status quo and be willing to propose and test new strategies for increasing competition and engagement within our service provider network
Stakeholder Feedback Analysis: Analyze feedback, ratings, and reviews from multiple sources and stakeholders to gain insights into service quality, customer satisfaction, and provider satisfaction, identifying actionable insights for improvement
Service Provider Insights: Develop operational and performance data to generate actionable insights and recommendations for 3rd party service providers, enabling them to enhance their service delivery, optimize processes, and align more closely with organizational objectives
Capacity Analysis and Modeling: Analyze the capacity of our Network, identifying gaps, weaknesses, and opportunities to improve operational efficiency and service quality, making recommendations for Network recruiting, retention, and optimization
Testing: Leads test and pilots for process changes as a a result of analysis and communicates the results and recommendations to all areas of management
Subject matter expert: Serves as an internal data analytics expert by ensuring consistency in data analysis methodologies and sharing of best practices

Experience and Skills:

BA/BS Degree or equivalent experience. Degree that emphasizes data analysis and communication (e.g., computer science, social sciences, physical sciences, math, engineering, or statistics) preferred
5+ years of business and data analysis experience
Excellent communication and presentation abilities
Strong problem-solving skills and business acumen
Experience with data management, specifically with validating and auditing data and reports from multiple systems
Demonstrated project and task management capability
Past experience with BI, Google Workspace, and CRM Tools
Proficiency in SQL and working with relational databases is required
An analytical professional with attention to detail and high standard of performance excellence

Hiring In:

United States: Arizona, California, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Massachusetts, Michigan, New Hampshire, New York State, New Mexico, North Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia

The anticipated closing date to submit applications for this role is June 20th, 2026. Join our Greenhouse Candidate Portal to track your application status and receive instant alerts for future openings.

The base salary range presented represents the anticipated low and high end salary range for new hires in this position. Your final base salary will be determined based on factors such as work location, experience, job related skills, and relevant training and education. The range listed is just one component of the total compensation package provided by Agero to employees.

National Pay Range: $110,000 USD - $135,000 USD

Life at Agero:

At Agero, you'll find a workplace where your unique perspective is not just welcomed, it's celebrated. We believe that our differences make us stronger, and we're committed to creating an environment where every employee feels a sense of belonging. If you're looking for a company that values your individuality, provides opportunities for growth, and champions open communication, Agero is the place for you. Join our team and help us drive the future of driver assistance, while experiencing a workplace where you can truly thrive.

Benefits Built for Well-being: 

Agero’s innovation is driven by a workforce where all associates feel like they can truly thrive. Agero offers a wide range of benefits to promote well-being, encourage personal development, and ensure financial stability. Our benefits include:

Health and Wellness: Healthcare, dental, vision, disability, life insurance, and mental health benefits for associates and their families.
Financial Security: 401(k) plan with company match and tuition assistance to support your future goals.
Work-Life Balance: Flexible time off, paid sick leave, and ten paid holidays annually.
For Contact Center Roles: Accrual of up to 3 weeks Paid Time Off per year, paid sick leave, and ten paid holidays annually.
Family Support: Parental planning benefits to assist associates through life’s milestones.
Bonus/Incentive Programs

Join Agero and experience a workplace that invests in your success both personally and professionally.

*Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full‑time basis. This position is not eligible for employer visa sponsorship now or in the future.

*It is unlawful in Massachusetts to required or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Excellent at solving problems and working through complex analytics

Advanced Excel skills

Title: Intern | ALS - Data Modernization and Analysis
Description:
ENABLE your future through light.

Excelitas is a global technology leader with more than 7,500 employees, focused on delivering market-driven solutions to fulfill the illumination, optical, detection and imaging needs of OEMs and end-users across the biomedical, semiconductor, industrial, consumer products, scientific, security, defense and aerospace sectors.

ENGAGE with us today and make your contribution to the future! Join the team that leading technology companies turn to for cutting-edge photonic innovation. At Excelitas Technologies you are how we EXCEL.




Overview

Excelitas Technologies, a leader in photonics and frequency tunable laser solutions, is seeking an intern to support data modernization efforts within our wafer fabrication operations. This 8‑week, on‑site internship offers hands‑on experience with manufacturing data processing, code migration, and basic image analysis. An extension may be available based on performance and project needs.

Responsibilities

Clean, standardize, and prepare wafer‑fab data for database upload.
Support data validation and documentation of data sources and formats.
Assist in converting legacy code (ex., VB6 to C#.NET), including code translation and functional checks.
Perform basic image feature analysis (e.g., particle counting) and label data for future ML or AI workflows.

Preferred Qualifications

Pursuing a degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Applied Physics, or related fields.
Exposure to C#, Python, SQL or LabVIEW.
Interest in image analysis or feature extraction.

What You’ll Gain

Exposure to real wafer‑fab workflows in a photonics‑focused company.
Hands‑on experience with data modernization and legacy code migration.
Mentorship from experienced engineering staff.

Compensation:

Pay Range: $22hr - $27hr Depending on Experience




This position requires the use of information which is subject to the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR)

Visa sponsorship is not available for any position at Excelitas

Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer

Minorities/Females/Disability/Veteran/Gender Identity/Sexual Orientation

Excelitas is seeking leaders and innovators to join our global team!

Visit: https://jobs.excelitas.com/

Title: Data Analyst / Engineer, Artificial Intelligence & Technology
Description:
Position Requirements




Data Infrastructure & Engineering

Design, build, and maintain a centralized data lakehouse using Microsoft Fabric, Azure Data Lake, and OneLake, consolidating data from Yardi, Argus, Juniper Square, SharePoint, Chatham, and other source systems.
Develop and manage automated data pipelines that extract, clean, and load data from all source systems into a single, reliable layer accessible to Power BI, AI models, and future tools.
Execute daily, weekly, and monthly data integrity processes crucial for supporting quantitative models and portfolio reporting.
Optimize data gathering and validation processes for efficiency and scalability as the firm’s data footprint grows.
Ensure data governance and consistency across all integrated platforms, reducing reliance on stale exports and manual reporting.




Analytics & Reporting

Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards for property performance, portfolio analytics, investment underwriting, and leadership reporting.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop data-driven narratives using visualization dashboards for internal asset operations and investor reporting.
Conduct research and curate datasets from internal and external sources; derive actionable insights to inform leasing, acquisitions, and capital planning decisions.
Provide financial analysis support across business units including investments, asset management, and accounting.
Translate complex data into clear, strategic insights that drive informed business strategies for senior leadership.




AI Data Layer & Model Integration

Serve as the data infrastructure owner for AI initiatives including Ask Bulfinch, automated deal memos, lease abstraction, and email triage—ensuring AI models have access to clean, well-structured Bulfinch data.
Integrate Azure OpenAI and/or Claude API with the firm’s data layer, enabling AI tools to query and reason over live portfolio information.
Collaborate with the external AI Strategist and AI Steering Committee on data requirements for new AI tool builds and model deployments.
Support Dataverse implementation for structured CRM-style records, deal tracking, and Power Apps integrations where applicable.




Cross-Functional Collaboration

Facilitate data capture through internal systems, enabling business owners across acquisitions, accounting, property management, and leasing to harness available information effectively.
Work closely with the IT Specialist on M365 administration, system access, security, and infrastructure to ensure a stable, secure data environment.
Support and actively contribute to Bulfinch’s AI Steering Committee, providing data infrastructure input on governance, initiative prioritization, and adoption best practices.
Collaborate with IT Specialist on technology vendor coordination and data providers including Yardi, Argus, Juniper Square, and Chatham.




QUALIFICATIONS

Required

3–5 years of professional experience in data engineering, data analytics, or a related field—ideally within commercial real estate, PropTech, finance, or a data-intensive industry.
Proficiency in Microsoft database management tools including Fabric, Azure SQL, and OneLake; hands-on experience building and maintaining data pipelines across multiple source systems.
Strong command of SQL, Excel, and VBA for data transformation, querying, and automation.
Demonstrated experience with Power BI or Tableau for dashboard development and enterprise reporting.
Experience analyzing large, complex datasets with a focus on structure, integrity, and scalability.
Strong communication and presentation skills; ability to translate complex data concepts for non-technical stakeholders across acquisitions, accounting, and property management.
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Computer Science, Statistics, Data Analytics, Data Science, or a related field.




Preferred

Microsoft Certified: Fabric Data Engineer Associate (DP-700) certification
Proficiency in Python, R, or similar data-centric programming languages.
Experience with relational databases and REST APIs for integrating third-party platforms.
Hands-on experience with Azure OpenAI, Claude API, or other LLM integrations for AI-powered applications.
Familiarity with Microsoft Dataverse and Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate).
Experience with CRE platforms such as Yardi, Argus, Juniper Square, MRI, or Chatham.
Basic understanding of accounting processes and familiarity with private equity, investment risk, or capital markets.
Working knowledge of commercial real estate fundamentals—asset classes, leasing, asset management, or investment underwriting.
Exposure to the PropTech landscape (VTS, Measurabl, CoStar, Placer.ai, etc.).
Graduate degree (MS in Data Science, Computer Science, or related field) a plus.

Title: Analyst Reporting & Analysis
Description:
Category/Area of Expertise: Data-Science & Analytics

Job Requisition: 504010

Address: USA-MA-Quincy-1385 Hancock Street

Store Code: ADUSA Distribution Services (5172986)

ADUSA Distribution is the distribution company of Ahold Delhaize USA, providing distribution services to one of the largest grocery retail supply chains in the nation. ADUSA Distribution fosters a culture that inspires and empowers our associates to always deliver exceptional service and value. Our team is supporting the supply chain network evolution to an integrated self-distribution model of the future, serving leading omnichannel grocery brands - Food Lion, Giant Food, The GIANT Company, Hannaford and Stop & Shop. To learn more about our company and the other supply chain companies of Ahold Delhaize USA, visit www.adusasc.com.

What You'll Do (Role Overview)

As an Analyst III, Reporting and Analysis, you'll play a critical role in shaping how data drives decisions across our Distribution & Transportation network.

In this role, you'll go beyond building reports you'll unlock insights, influence strategy, and help leaders understand what's happening across the business and what to do next.

You'll work with large, complex data sets, translate them into meaningful stories, and provide clear recommendations that help drive smarter, faster decisions.

Why This Role Matters

Data is at the heart of how we operate and this role helps ensure we're using it to its fullest potential.

As a trusted partner to leadership, you will:


Turn data into actionable insights that drive performance
Support key initiatives across Distribution, Transportation, and Supply Chain
Help improve the tools, reporting, and analytics that guide our business forward


Your work will directly impact operational efficiency, strategic priorities, and overall business performance.

What You'll Be Responsible For

Turn Data into Insights


Analyze complex data sets to uncover trends, risks, and opportunities
Connect insights to broader Distribution & Transportation and Supply Chain strategies
Provide recommendations that help leaders make confident, data-driven decisions


Build & Enhance Reporting


Design, create, and maintain reporting that is accurate, scalable, and user-friendly
Deliver both recurring and ad hoc analysis based on evolving business needs
Ensure stakeholders understand and can effectively use reporting tools and outputs


Partner Across the Business


Collaborate closely with Distribution & Transportation leadership, Supply Chain teams, IT, and Data Governance
Gather and refine reporting requirements to deliver meaningful, impactful solutions
Serve as a key bridge between data and the business ensuring alignment and clarity


Solve Complex Problems


Perform advanced analysis to uncover deeper insights
Use business modeling to evaluate potential outcomes and guide decision-making
Proactively identify opportunities to improve processes, performance, and efficiency


Tell the Story Behind the Data


Transform large, complex data sets into clear, compelling narratives
Develop presentation-ready materials for leadership reviews and strategic discussions
Help leaders understand not just the data but the story, impact, and next steps


Drive Innovation & Continuous Improvement


Stay current on analytics tools and emerging best practices
Expand capabilities in reporting, visualization, and modeling
Support a culture of data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement


Be a Key Contributor


Provide subject matter expertise on analytics tools and reporting capabilities
Support key initiatives, projects, and cross-functional teams
Help champion change by using data to guide growth and transformation


What You Bring

We're looking for someone who is curious, analytical, and confident working with both data and people someone who enjoys solving problems and influencing outcomes.

Key Skills & Strengths


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience working with large data sets
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (Power Query, Power Pivots, etc.)
Experience with data visualization and analytics tools (Power BI, Alteryx, etc.)
Experience creating calculated expressions (DAX, Calculate fields, Excel Formulas, etc.)
Ability to query using SQL in Databricks
Knowledge of Power Automate and Power Apps
Ability to translate complex analysis into clear, actionable recommendations
Strong storytelling and presentation skills
Knowledge of supply chain, inventory, forecasting, or distribution operations (preferred)
Strong organizational and time management abilities
Ability to collaborate, influence, and build relationships across teams
Creative, innovative thinker with a proactive mindset
Ability to navigate change and drive continuous improvement


Education & Experience


Bachelor's degree in business, economics, engineering, math, statistics, or a related field
Analytics certifications are a plus
3-5 years of experience in analytics, reporting, or a data-driven environment


What Makes This Opportunity Exciting


You'll work directly with leadership and influence real business decisions
You'll turn data into insights that drive measurable impact across the network
You'll gain exposure to multiple areas of the business and expand your influence
You'll help shape how analytics supports the future of Distribution & Transportation
You'll be part of a team that values innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement


Let's Build What's Next Together

If you're passionate about using data to tell stories, solve problems, and drive meaningful change, this is your opportunity to make a lasting impact.

We are an organization that values diversity and encourages all qualified individuals to apply without regard to sex, race, color, ancestry, national origin, citizenship status, religion, age, marital status (including civil unions), military service, veteran status, pregnancy (including childbirth and related medical conditions), genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, legally recognized disability, domestic violence victim status or any other characteristic protected by law.

Our organization is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities, including applicants. If you have a disability and require assistance in the application process, please email recruiting@adusasc.com

  • Collaborate closely with Distribution & Transportation leadership, Supply Chain teams, IT, and Data Governance
  • Gather and refine reporting requirements to deliver meaningful, impactful solutions
  • Serve as a key bridge between data and the business ensuring alignment and clarity

Solve Complex Problems

  • Perform advanced analysis to uncover deeper insights
  • Use business modeling to evaluate potential outcomes and guide decision-making
  • Proactively identify opportunities to improve processes, performance, and efficiency

Tell the Story Behind the Data

  • Transform large, complex data sets into clear, compelling narratives
  • Develop presentation-ready materials for leadership reviews and strategic discussions
  • Help leaders understand not just the data but the story, impact, and next steps

Drive Innovation & Continuous Improvement

  • Stay current on analytics tools and emerging best practices
  • Expand capabilities in reporting, visualization, and modeling
  • Support a culture of data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement

Be a Key Contributor

  • Provide subject matter expertise on analytics tools and reporting capabilities
  • Support key initiatives, projects, and cross-functional teams
  • Help champion change by using data to guide growth and transformation

What You Bring

We're looking for someone who is curious, analytical, and confident working with both data and people someone who enjoys solving problems and influencing outcomes.

Key Skills & Strengths

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience working with large data sets
  • Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (Power Query, Power Pivots, etc.)
  • Experience with data visualization and analytics tools (Power BI, Alteryx, etc.)
  • Experience creating calculated expressions (DAX, Calculate fields, Excel Formulas, etc.)
  • Ability to query using SQL in Databricks
  • Knowledge of Power Automate and Power Apps
  • Ability to translate complex analysis into clear, actionable recommendations
  • Strong storytelling and presentation skills
  • Knowledge of supply chain, inventory, forecasting, or distribution operations (preferred)
  • Strong organizational and time management abilities
  • Ability to collaborate, influence, and build relationships across teams
  • Creative, innovative thinker with a proactive mindset
  • Ability to navigate change and drive continuous improvement

Education & Experience

  • Bachelor's degree in business, economics, engineering, math, statistics, or a related field
  • Analytics certifications are a plus
  • 3-5 years of experience in analytics, reporting, or a data-driven environment

What Makes This Opportunity Exciting

  • You'll work directly with leadership and influence real business decisions
  • You'll turn data into insights that drive measurable impact across the network
  • You'll gain exposure to multiple areas of the business and expand your influence
  • You'll help shape how analytics supports the future of Distribution & Transportation
  • You'll be part of a team that values innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement

Let's Build What's Next Together

If you're passionate about using data to tell stories, solve problems, and drive meaningful change, this is your opportunity to make a lasting impact.

We are an organization that values diversity and encourages all qualified individuals to apply without regard to sex, race, color, ancestry, national origin, citizenship status, religion, age, marital status (including civil unions), military service, veteran status, pregnancy (including childbirth and related medical conditions), genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, legally recognized disability, domestic violence victim status or any other characteristic protected by law.

Our organization is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities, including applicants. If you have a disability and require assistance in the application process, please email recruiting@adusasc.com

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
At WHOOP, we're on a mission to unlock human performance and healthspan. WHOOP empowers members to perform at a higher level through a deeper understanding of their bodies and daily lives.

WHOOP is hiring Product Analysts to embed across the product organization and serve as strategic analytics partners to teams across Healthcare, Algorithms, AI, Hardware, and other product areas. In this role, you will help define how WHOOP measures product success, uncover insights that shape roadmap decisions, and translate complex data into clear recommendations that improve the member experience. You will bring strong analytical rigor, product intuition, and intellectual curiosity to help teams make faster, smarter, and more impactful decisions with data.

Responsibilities


Partner with Product, Engineering, Research, Design, and cross-functional leadership to inform strategy and roadmap decisions with data.
Own the analytical narrative for your product area, illustrated by defining KPIs, refining measurement frameworks, and surfacing insights that drive action.
Support experimentation and feature launches end-to-end: from defining success metrics to post-launch analysis and iteration.
Build scalable, self-serve reporting & tooling that lets your stakeholders make decisions without waiting on you.
Develop deep context on your product domain and use it to ask sharper questions, challenge assumptions, and push the product forward.
Proactively surface insights and recommendations that go beyond the ask; identify opportunities others haven't seen yet.
Collaborate with a high-performance analytics team in a fast-paced environment where intellectual curiosity is the baseline expectation.


Qualifications


You've done this before: 2-6+ years in an analytical role where you owned business-critical metrics or initiatives. (We're hiring across levels; we'll figure out the right fit together.)
You use AI tools proactively and hold AI-assisted work to the same bar as your own.
You can take a vague question, wrangle the right data, isolate insights, and communicate actionable recommendations to stakeholders who aren't data people.
You're a strong storyteller: you know how to make a compelling, concise case for action.
You're technically sharp: advanced SQL is table stakes. Familiarity with modern data stacks (Snowflake, dbt, ELT workflows) and visualization tools (Hex, Sigma, Amplitude, Looker) is a plus.
You have product sense: you think about the member experience, not just the numbers.
You're curious about how things work. Algorithms, hardware, health science: you don't need to be an expert, but you should want to understand.
You're a self-starter who thrives in ambiguity and doesn't wait to be told what to work on.


TEAMS WE'RE HIRING FOR

We’re Looking For Multiple Analysts To Work Across Our Product Teams. During The Interview Process, We'll Work With You To Find The Right Team Fit Based On Your Interests And Strengths


AI Product: The future of intelligent coaching and personalized insights powered by AI/ML.
Algorithms: Where the science meets the product; sleep, recovery, strain, and how algorithmic outputs translate into member value.
Hardware Product: The physical device experience; sensors, wearability, and next-gen hardware.
Healthcare Product: Building WHOOP's Health Operating System; health outcomes, clinical features, personalized wellness.


This role is based in the WHOOP office located in Boston, MA. The successful candidate must be prepared to relocate if necessary to work out of the Boston, MA office.

Interested in the role, but don’t meet every qualification? We encourage you to still apply! At WHOOP, we believe there is much more to a candidate than what is written on paper, and we value character as much as experience. As we continue to build a diverse and inclusive environment, we encourage anyone who is interested in this role to apply.

WHOOP is an Equal Opportunity Employer and participates in E-verify to determine employment eligibility

The WHOOP compensation philosophy is designed to attract, motivate, and retain exceptional talent by offering competitive base salaries, meaningful equity, and consistent pay practices that reflect our mission and core values.

At WHOOP, we view total compensation as the combination of base salary, equity, and benefits, with equity serving as a key differentiator that aligns our employees with the long-term success of the company and allows every member of our corporate team to own part of WHOOP and share in the company’s long-term growth and success.

The U.S. base salary range for this full-time position is $100,000 - $155,000. Salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within each range, individual pay is based on factors such as job-related skills, experience, performance, and relevant education or training.

In addition to the base salary, the successful candidate will also receive benefits and a generous equity package.

These ranges may be modified in the future to reflect evolving market conditions and organizational needs. While most offers will typically fall toward the starting point of the range, total compensation will depend on the candidate’s specific qualifications, expertise, and alignment with the role’s requirements.
