Job Summary

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Data Integrity Analyst to join our team in North Carolina. This is a full-time opportunity for recent graduates and early-career professionals with 0–2 years of experience who are looking to build a career in Data Integrity, Quality Assurance, Compliance, and Regulatory Operations within a regulated life sciences environment.

The successful candidate will play a critical role in ensuring the integrity, accuracy, consistency, and reliability of GxP data across laboratory, manufacturing, and quality operations. Working closely with cross-functional teams, the Data Integrity Analyst will support compliance initiatives, documentation reviews, audit readiness activities, and continuous improvement efforts while ensuring adherence to regulatory requirements and industry best practices.


Essential Responsibilities

  • Support Data Integrity initiatives across Quality, Manufacturing, Laboratory, and operational functions.
  • Review and evaluate paper-based and electronic records for compliance with ALCOA+ principles, GMP requirements, and internal procedures.
  • Assist in Data Integrity assessments, gap analyses, and remediation activities to strengthen compliance and quality systems.
  • Support investigations involving data discrepancies, documentation errors, and potential Data Integrity concerns.
  • Perform reviews of audit trails, electronic records, and system-generated data to identify compliance risks and improvement opportunities.
  • Assist with the implementation and maintenance of CAPA, Change Control, and Quality Management System processes.
  • Ensure documentation is complete, accurate, and maintained in accordance with regulatory requirements and company standards.
  • Support the creation, revision, and periodic review of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and controlled documents.
  • Participate in internal audits, inspection readiness activities, and regulatory compliance initiatives.
  • Collaborate with Quality Assurance, Validation, Manufacturing, Laboratory, and Regulatory Affairs teams to support data lifecycle management and compliance objectives.
  • Prepare reports, metrics, and supporting documentation related to Data Integrity and quality performance.
  • Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives aimed at enhancing data reliability, compliance, and operational effectiveness.


Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Life Sciences, Biotechnology, Biology, Chemistry, Pharmacy, Engineering, or a related scientific discipline.
  • 0–2 years of experience in pharmaceutical, biotechnology, medical device, laboratory, manufacturing, or other regulated environments.
  • Foundational knowledge of GMP, GxP, Data Integrity, and documentation practices.
  • Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills with exceptional attention to detail.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications, including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
  • Ability to work effectively in a collaborative, team-oriented environment.


Preferred Qualifications

  • Familiarity with ALCOA+ principles and Data Integrity guidance.
  • Exposure to Quality Assurance, Compliance, Validation, or Regulatory Affairs functions.
  • Knowledge of CAPA, Deviations, Change Controls, and Quality Management Systems.
  • Experience with electronic documentation systems, audit trail reviews, or regulated data management processes.
  • Understanding of FDA and global regulatory expectations related to Data Integrity.

Title: Data Sales Analyst
Description:
Important information: To be immediately considered, please send an updated version of your resume to somp767@kellyservices.com.




Job Title: Business Professional - Analyst, Sales Operations

Pay rate: $42 Per hour

Location: Mahwah, NJ, 07430

Duration: 12 months

Type : W2 contract




This contract role supports Sales Operations through the execution, validation, and maintenance of sales reporting and dashboards. The position is highly detail-oriented and focused on ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and timely delivery of recurring and ad hoc reporting. This role does not own reporting strategy, but plays a critical role in executing against defined priorities.

Key Responsibilities

Execute the build and maintenance of Power BI dashboards and reports based on defined requirements
Perform data validation and reconciliation across systems (e.g., Power BI, source files, internal databases) to ensure accuracy and consistency
Support monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting cycles, including data pulls, formatting, and quality checks
Maintain and update Excel-based reporting tools, including large datasets, formulas, and data transformations
Assist in preparing standard sales performance reports for leadership review
Support ad hoc reporting requests related to salesforce, hospital, and surgeon-level data
Identify and flag data discrepancies, gaps, or inconsistencies and escalate as needed
Execute routine audits of reports and datasets to ensure ongoing data integrity
Track and update field sales alignment and hierarchy changes in reporting outputs
Partner with Sales Ops, Analytics, and IT teams to validate data inputs and outputs, ensuring alignment across systems
Follow established data governance and reporting standards; ensure outputs align with approved definitions and logic
Assist in documenting reporting processes and maintaining clear, repeatable workflows




Required Skills & Experience

Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, formulas, large dataset handling)
Working experience with Power BI (report building, basic data modeling, dashboard updates)
High attention to detail with a focus on data accuracy and validation
Ability to follow defined processes and execute with consistency
Comfortable working with large datasets and performing reconciliation across multiple sources
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage recurring deliverables on tight timelines

Nice to Have

Experience working with sales data, commissions, or field performance reporting
Familiarity with data validation, auditing, or QA processes
Exposure to tools such as Azure, SQL, or enterprise data environments (supporting, not leading)

Title: Chart Analyst
Description:
Summary

The Chart Analyst is responsible for accurate and timely analysis of the patient medical record. The record is the communication tool to support delivery of optimum patient care by the clinician/provider. In a professional and courteous manner, calls are generated to referring and/or specialty offices and other facilities to request records for the patient visit/test. Documents received are reviewed to determine if further reports are needed for the patient visit (i.e. bypass report from an out-of-state facility). Records are sent to scan/index in a timely manner. Laboratory results received are electronically flowed to create a result trend of values. Responsible for answering department calls in a courteous manner and performing a quick release of information for continuity of patient care purposes. Responsible for delivering quality performance and service excellence while adhering to the Parkview Health Standards of Behavior.

Education

Must be a high school graduate or the equivalent with GED. Basic Medical Terminology desired.

Licensure/Certification

N/A

Experience

Minimum of two years’ experience in a hospital or medical office setting desired. Experience with Microsoft suite of products preferred.

Other Qualifications

Must have good verbal and written communication skills. Must be able to keyboard 35 words per minute. Ability to multi-task, work independently using critical thinking skills.

Why Parkview?

We have everything you’re looking for in a career; great compensation and benefits; skill development and career advancement programs and more. At Parkview, we are committed to providing excellent care to each patient within our clinics and medical practices. The success of this mission begins with care team members like you.


Benefits: Health benefits, paid time off, employee assistance, community vendor discounts, payroll deduction options
Culture: World-class teamwork, Personalized health journeys for co-workers and family members
